http_server.description:
The default value is `http://proxy.example.com:8080`.  The HTTP proxy server to use.

This is optional, but if you have a proxy server, you can set it here.
http_server.title: Proxy HTTP Server

https_server.description:
The default value is `https://proxy.example.com:8080`.  The HTTPS proxy server to use.

This is optional, but if you have a proxy server, you can set it here.
https_server.title: Proxy HTTPS Server

items.description: The default value is ``.  Bypass URL.

username.description: The default value is ``.  Username for Proxy Authentication.  Only required if proxy requires authentication.

abstract.proxy_servers.description:
This dictionary provides the required configuration settings for the network proxy settings.

Optional fields are:
  * `http_server`
  * `https_server`
  * `no_proxy`
  * `password`
  * `username`
abstract.proxy_servers.title: OpenShift Container Platform (OCP) - Machine Network Gateway

abstract.sensitive_variables.description: Object sent by user to configure range of unified ports as FC/Ethernet or ports as breakout.
abstract.sensitive_variables.title: Sensitive Variables

cco_password.description:
Cisco.com Authentication Password.  It must meet the following criteria:
 * One Uppercase Letter
 * One Lowercase Letter
 * One Number
 * One Special Character: `!`, `@`, `#`, `$`, `%`, `^`, `&`, `*`, `+`, `_`, `=`, `-`
 * Be between 12 and 60 Characters in Length.
cco_password.title: cco_password

certificate.description:
X.509 certificate in PEM (Privacy-Enhanced Mail) format.

Requirements:
  * Must begin with `-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----`
  * Must end with `-----END CERTIFICATE-----`
  * Body must be Base64-encoded DER data, line-wrapped at 64 characters.
  * Intermediate and root CA certificates follow the same format.
  * Certificates can be chained by concatenating multiple PEM blocks.

Example:
```
-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
MIID...base64...
-----END CERTIFICATE-----
```
certificate.title: certificate

cert_mgmt_passphrase.description:
Pure Storage Certificate Management - Passphrase for encrypted private key.  A number Between 1-64 to denote the variables cert_mgmt_passphrase_[1-64].  i.e. `export cert_mgmt_passphrase_1='passphrasehere'`.

Key Requirements:
  * Required only if private key is encrypted with a passphrase
  * Character Set: Alphanumeric and special characters typically allowed
  * Length: 1 to 255 characters
  * Omit if private key is not passphrase-protected
cert_mgmt_passphrase.title: cert_mgmt_passphrase

drive_security_passphrase.description:
Drive Security Current/New Security Key Passphrase for Manual or Remote Key Management.

Intersight drive security passphrases for Self-Encrypting Drives (SEDs) must be 8 to 32 characters long, including at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one number, and one special character. These alphanumeric keys are critical for securing data on UCS servers, and if lost, cannot be recovered. 

Key Requirements & Best Practices:
  * `Length`: 8–32 characters.
  * `Complexity`: At least one: uppercase, lowercase, number, and special character.
  * `Storage`: Passphrases are not stored by the system; save them externally.
  * `Impact`: A lost passphrase causes irrecoverable loss of drive data.
  * `Modification`: When re-keying or updating policies, ensure the "Drive Security is Already Enabled" box is checked if applicable.
drive_security_passphrase.title: drive_security_passphrase

fabric_interconnect_password.description:
Cisco UCS Fabric Interconnect administrator password.  A number Between 1-64 to denote the variables fabric_interconnect_password_[1-64].  i.e. `export fabric_interconnect_password_1='Cisco123!'`.

Key Requirements:
  * Length: 8 to 127 characters.
  * No whitespace characters.
  * Best Practice: Use a mix of uppercase letters, lowercase letters, digits, and special characters.
fabric_interconnect_password.title: fabric_interconnect_password

ipmi_key.description: IMPI Key for an IPMI Policy.
ipmi_key.title: ipmi_key

iscsi_boot_password.description: iSCSI Boot Password.  Password of Initiator or Target Interface. Enter between 12 and 16 characters, including special characters except spaces, tabs, line breaks.
iscsi_boot_password.title: iscsi_boot_password

iso_web_server_password.description:
Password for HTTP/HTTPS basic authentication to the ISO web server.  A number Between 1-64 to denote the variables iso_web_server_password_[1-64].  i.e. `export iso_web_server_password_1='password123'`.

Key Requirements:
  * Length: 6 to 255 characters.
  * No whitespace characters.
  * Best Practice: Use a strong password; avoid `/`, `#`, backtick, and `,` characters to prevent URL parsing issues.
iso_web_server_password.title: iso_web_server_password

ldap_binding_password.description:
LDAP Binding Password.  Intersight LDAP binding passwords must be a string, typically up to 254 characters long, excluding spaces, tabs, and line breaks. The password connects Intersight to Active Directory (AD) with read capabilities. For security, it is recommended to use a strong password and, if using Terraform, set this via secure variables. 

Key Requirements:
  * Characters: Any string excluding spaces, tabs, or line breaks.
  * Length: Up to 254 characters.
  * Best Practice: Ensure the Bind DN user has appropriate read-only access in AD.
ldap_binding_password.title: ldap_binding_password

linux_password.description:
Generic Linux/Unix user password enforced by PAM (pam_pwquality / pam_cracklib defaults).

Key Requirements:
  * Length: 8 to 255 characters.
  * Complexity: Must include characters from at least three of the following four categories:
    - Uppercase letters `A-Z`
    - Lowercase letters `a-z`
    - Digits `0-9`
    - Special characters: `!@#$%^&*()-_=+[]{}|;:',.<>?/`~`
  * No whitespace (spaces, tabs, newlines).
  * Best Practice: Use 16+ characters with all four categories for stronger security.
linux_password.title: linux_password

local_user_password.description:
Local User Password.  Cisco Intersight local user passwords require 8 to 127 characters when "Enforce Strong Password" is enabled (default), including at least three of these categories: uppercase letters, lowercase letters, digits, and special characters (! , @, #, $, %, ^, &, *, -, _, +, =). If strong password enforcement is disabled, 8–20 characters are required. 

Key Requirements & Configuration
  * Length: 8 to 127 characters (with Strong Password) or 8 to 20 characters (without).
  * Complexity: Must include characters from three of the following: `A-Z`, `a-z`, `0-9`, and `!@#$%^&*- _+=`.
  * Restriction: Must not contain the username.
  * Expiry & Security: Policies can set an expiry duration (1–3650 days), notification period, and grace period.
  * Admin User: Password changes for the admin user can be handled via the Local User Policy. 

Policy Options
  * Enforce Strong Password: Default true, enforced.
  * Password History: Tracks history to prevent reuse.
  * Disable Strong Password: If unchecked, 8-20 characters of any type can be used.
local_user_password.title: local_user_password

private_key.description:
Private key in PEM (Privacy-Enhanced Mail) format.

Supported PEM headers:
  * `-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----` — PKCS#8 unencrypted (RSA, EC, Ed25519)
  * `-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----` — PKCS#1 RSA private key
  * `-----BEGIN EC PRIVATE KEY-----` — SEC1 EC private key
  * `-----BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----` — OpenSSH format (Ed25519, ECDSA, RSA)

Requirements:
  * Must begin and end with matching `-----BEGIN/END <TYPE> PRIVATE KEY-----` markers.
  * Body must be Base64-encoded, line-wrapped at 64 characters (except OpenSSH format).
  * Private keys must be kept strictly confidential and never committed to version control.
  * Prefer encrypted private keys (passphrase-protected) for storage at rest.

Example:
```
-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----
MIIE...base64...
-----END PRIVATE KEY-----
```
private_key.pattern:
^-----BEGIN (PRIVATE KEY|RSA PRIVATE KEY|EC PRIVATE KEY|OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY|ENCRYPTED PRIVATE KEY)-----
[A-Za-z0-9+/=
]+
-----END (PRIVATE KEY|RSA PRIVATE KEY|EC PRIVATE KEY|OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY|ENCRYPTED PRIVATE KEY)-----
?$
private_key.title: private_key

pure_api_token.description:
Pure Storage Array API Token.  A number Between 1-64 to denote the variables pure_api_token_[1-64].  i.e. `export pure_api_token_1='token_string'`.

Key Requirements:
  * Required for authentication with Pure Storage FlashArray and FlashBlade systems
  * Length: Typically 20-128 characters depending on Pure Storage version
  * Character Set: Alphanumeric and special characters
  * Sensitive: Never commit to version control; use environment variables for deployment
  * Best Practice: Use dedicated API tokens with minimal required permissions
pure_api_token.title: pure_api_token

redfish_password.description:
Redfish/BMC (iDRAC, iLO, CIMC) administrator password.  A number Between 1-64 to denote the variables redfish_password_[1-64].  i.e. `export redfish_password_1='Cisco123!'`.

Key Requirements:
  * Length: 8 to 127 characters.
  * No whitespace characters.
  * Best Practice: Use a mix of uppercase letters, lowercase letters, digits, and special characters consistent with your BMC vendor requirements.
redfish_password.title: redfish_password

root_password.description: REPLACE Root Password in plain text.  The Script will convert to an encrypted password.
root_password.title: Root Password

snmp_community_string.description:
SNMP Community String.

Avoid using the @ and : characters in Cisco SNMP community strings. The @ character is reserved for indexing (VLAN designation), causing potential mapping issues, while : can cause delimiters issues in management systems. Most other characters (-=;,. ~#$%^&*()_+{}|<>?) are generally allowed, but it's best to avoid special characters. 

Key takeaways:
 * Avoid `@ and `:`.  Specifically, the `@` symbol is used for SNMP community string indexing (e.g., `community@vlan`).
 * Characters Allowed: Standard alphanumeric characters, along with special characters like `-`, `_`, `.`, `!`, `#`, `$`, `%`, etc., are generally supported.
 * Impact: Using an @ symbol can cause the switch to misunderstand which VLAN you are trying to query.
 * Best Practice: Use strong, long strings, but avoid the reserved `@` and `:` characters to prevent polling errors. 

If you are encountering errors, you may want to try:
 * Checking for errors in the CLI: Use show running-config to verify the string actually applied correctly.
 * Using alphanumeric characters: Try avoiding symbols entirely if you are still experiencing issues.
snmp_community_string.title: snmp_community_string

snmp_password.description:
SNMP Auth/Privacy Password.  Cisco Intersight SNMPv3 policies require SHA for authentication (auth) and AES-128 or AES-256 for privacy (priv). Passwords must be a minimum of 8 characters and can be up to 64 characters if in plain text. AuthPriv is the recommended, highly secure option, requiring unique passwords for both authentication and encryption. 

Key SNMPv3 Password and Policy Requirements:
Auth Protocol: SHA (Secure Hash Algorithm) is required; MD5 is not supported.
Priv Protocol: AES (Advanced Encryption Standard) 128-bit is supported and conforms with RFC 3826.
Password Length: Minimum 8 characters.
Security Level: AuthPriv (Authentication and Privacy) is the recommended security level for encryption.
User Constraints: You can define a maximum of 15 trap users, and the username can be up to 32 characters.
snmp_password.title: snmp_password

ssh_public_key.description:
SSH public key in OpenSSH authorized_keys format.

Supported key types:
  * `ssh-ed25519` — 256-bit EdDSA (recommended)
  * `ecdsa-sha2-nistp256` / `ecdsa-sha2-nistp384` / `ecdsa-sha2-nistp521` — ECDSA
  * `ssh-rsa` — RSA (minimum 2048-bit recommended)
  * `ssh-dss` — DSA (deprecated, avoid)

Format: `<key-type> <base64-encoded-key> [optional-comment]`

Examples:
  * `ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAA... user@host`
  * `ssh-rsa AAAAB3NzaC1yc2EAAAA... user@host`
ssh_public_key.title: ssh_public_key

vmedia_password.description:
When configuring Virtual Media (vMedia) in Cisco Intersight for OS installation or firmware updates, passwords for HTTPS and CIFS shares must adhere to specific character restrictions to prevent mounting failures.
Password Requirements by Protocol
  * CIFS (Common Internet File System): Supported characters include standard alphanumeric characters, but the password cannot contain the following special characters: `!`, `@`, `\`, or ` (backtick).
  * HTTPS: The password cannot contain the following characters: `/`, `#`, `, or `,`.
  * General Character Set: Generally, accepted characters for passwords in these fields include `~#$%^&*()_+-={[}]|;:'<>.?/`. 

Key Considerations for vMedia Mounts
  * Username Requirement: A username must be configured on the CIFS/HTTPS server for authentication.
  * CIFS Authentication: Intersight supports various authentication protocols for CIFS, including Ntlm, Ntlmi, Ntlmssp, Ntlmsspi, Ntlmv2, and Ntlmv2i.
  * Port Requirements: Ensure port 445 is open for CIFS, and port 443 (HTTPS) or 80 (HTTP) is open for web-based shares.
  * URL Length: The maximum allowed length for the HTTP/HTTPS URL is 2048 characters. 


If you are experiencing mounting issues, it is often due to special characters in the password that are interpreted incorrectly by the file server.
vmedia_password.title: vmedia_password

assignment_order.description: The default value is `sequential`.  Assignment order decides the order in which the next identifier is allocated.  * `default` - The next identifier is allocated based on the default order defined by the system.  * `sequential` - The next identifier is allocated in a sequential order.
assignment_order.title: assignment_order

List of 'adapter.ConfigPolicy' resources

Optional Attributes:

  * `enable_fip`

add_vic_adapter_configuration.title: add_vic_adapter_configuration - Add Vic Adapter Configuration

allOf.title: adapter_configuration

intersight.adapter_configuration.description: An Adapter Configuration Policy configures the Ethernet and Fibre-Channel settings for the VIC adapter.
intersight.adapter_configuration.title: adapter_configuration - Adapter Configuration - Intersight Uri: adapter/ConfigPolicies

enable_fip.description: The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true`.  Status of FIP protocol on the adapter interfaces.
enable_fip.title: enable_fip

enable_lldp.description: The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true`.  Status of LLDP protocol on the adapter interfaces.
enable_lldp.title: enable_lldp

enable_physical_nic_mode.description: The default value is `False`.  The default value is `false`.  When Physical NIC Mode is enabled, up-link ports of the VIC are set to pass-through mode. This allows the host to transmit packets without any modification. When Physical NIC Mode is enabled, VLAN tagging of the packets will not happen.
enable_physical_nic_mode.title: enable_physical_nic_mode

enable_port_channel.description: The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true`.  When Port Channel is enabled, two vNICs and two vHBAs are available for use on the adapter card. When disabled, four vNICs and four vHBAs are available for use on the adapter card. Disabling port channel reboots the server. Port Channel is supported only for Cisco VIC 1455/1457 adapters.
enable_port_channel.title: enable_port_channel

pci_slot.description: The default value is `MLOM`.  PCIe slot where the VIC adapter is installed. Supported values are (1-15) and MLOM.
pci_slot.title: pci_slot

intersight.adapter_configuration.add_vic.description:
Settings applicable for the Data Center Ethernet (DCE) interfaces on the adapter card.

Required Attributes:
  * `dce_interface_1_fec_mode`
  * `dce_interface_2_fec_mode`
  * `dce_interface_3_fec_mode`
  * `dce_interface_4_fec_mode`

dce_interface_1_fec_mode.description:
The default value is `cl91`.  Forward Error Correction (FEC) mode setting for the DCE interface of the adapter. FEC mode setting is supported only for Cisco VIC 14xx adapters. FEC mode 'cl74' is unsupported for Cisco VIC 1495/1497. This setting will be ignored for unsupported adapters and for unavailable DCE interfaces.
  * `cl74` - Use cl74 standard as FEC mode setting. 'Clause 74' aka FC-FEC ('FireCode' FEC) offers simple, low-latency protection against 1 burst/sparse bit error, but it is not good for random errors.
  * `cl91` - Use cl91 standard as FEC mode setting. 'Clause 91' aka RS-FEC ('ReedSolomon' FEC) offers better error protection against bursty and random errors but adds latency.
  * `Off` - Disable FEC mode on the DCE Interface.
dce_interface_1_fec_mode.title: dce_interface_1_fec_mode - Dce Interface 1 Fec Mode - object_type: adapter.DceInterfaceSettings

dce_interface_2_fec_mode.description:
The default value is `cl91`.  Forward Error Correction (FEC) mode setting for the DCE interface of the adapter. FEC mode setting is supported only for Cisco VIC 14xx adapters. FEC mode 'cl74' is unsupported for Cisco VIC 1495/1497. This setting will be ignored for unsupported adapters and for unavailable DCE interfaces.
  * `cl74` - Use cl74 standard as FEC mode setting. 'Clause 74' aka FC-FEC ('FireCode' FEC) offers simple, low-latency protection against 1 burst/sparse bit error, but it is not good for random errors.
  * `cl91` - Use cl91 standard as FEC mode setting. 'Clause 91' aka RS-FEC ('ReedSolomon' FEC) offers better error protection against bursty and random errors but adds latency.
  * `Off` - Disable FEC mode on the DCE Interface.
dce_interface_2_fec_mode.title: dce_interface_2_fec_mode - Dce Interface 2 Fec Mode - object_type: adapter.DceInterfaceSettings

dce_interface_3_fec_mode.description:
The default value is `cl91`.  Forward Error Correction (FEC) mode setting for the DCE interface of the adapter. FEC mode setting is supported only for Cisco VIC 14xx adapters. FEC mode 'cl74' is unsupported for Cisco VIC 1495/1497. This setting will be ignored for unsupported adapters and for unavailable DCE interfaces.
  * `cl74` - Use cl74 standard as FEC mode setting. 'Clause 74' aka FC-FEC ('FireCode' FEC) offers simple, low-latency protection against 1 burst/sparse bit error, but it is not good for random errors.
  * `cl91` - Use cl91 standard as FEC mode setting. 'Clause 91' aka RS-FEC ('ReedSolomon' FEC) offers better error protection against bursty and random errors but adds latency.
  * `Off` - Disable FEC mode on the DCE Interface.
dce_interface_3_fec_mode.title: dce_interface_3_fec_mode - Dce Interface 3 Fec Mode - object_type: adapter.DceInterfaceSettings

FEC mode 'cl74' is unsupported for Cisco VIC 1495/1497. This setting will be ignored for unsupported adapters and for unavailable DCE interfaces.
  * `cl74` - Use cl74 standard as FEC mode setting. 'Clause 74' aka FC-FEC ('FireCode' FEC) offers simple, low-latency protection against 1 burst/sparse bit error, but it is not good for random errors.
  * `cl91` - Use cl91 standard as FEC mode setting. 'Clause 91' aka RS-FEC ('ReedSolomon' FEC) offers better error protection against bursty and random errors but adds latency.
  * `Off` - Disable FEC mode on the DCE Interface.

enable.description: The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true`.  Admin state for the AuditD feature.
enable.title: enable

minimum_severity_to_report.description:
The default value is `notifications`.  The log level for the AuditD feature.

Options are:
  * `notifications` - Generated logs are of Notification level, providing information about normal but significant system events requiring awareness.
  * `emergencies` - Generated logs will be of Emergency log level, indicating a critical and unstable system state.
  * `alerts` - Generated logs are of Alert level, indicating critical issues needing immediate attention to prevent system disruption.
  * `critical` - Generated logs are of Critical level, signaling severe issues that may cause system failure if not addressed immediately.
  * `errors` - Generated logs are of Error level, indicating significant problems that affect functionality but do not cause system failure.
  * `warnings` - Generated logs are of Warning level, highlighting potential issues that require attention but do not yet impact functionality.
  * `information` - Generated logs are of Information level, detailing routine operational messages without indicating any issues or errors.
  * `debugging` - Generated logs are of Debugging level, providing detailed information to help diagnose and troubleshoot system issues.
minimum_severity_to_report.title: minimum_severity_to_report

intersight.auditd.description: A policy to configure the kernel level AuditD settings in the Fabric Interconnect/Servers.
intersight.auditd.title: auditd - AuditD - Intersight Uri: auditd/Policies

bios_template.description:
The default value is ``.  Name of a BIOS Template to Configure.

Important Note: Add `-tpm/Tpm` to enable TPM.  Without `-tpm/Tpm`, TPM is disabled.

Options are:

  * `AnalyticalDatabaseSystems-M6-Intel(-Tpm)` - BIOS Policy for Analytical Database Systems on Cisco UCS M6 Servers with Intel processors`
  * `AnalyticalDatabaseSystems-M7-Intel(-Tpm)` - BIOS Policy for Analytical Database Systems on Cisco UCS M7 Servers with Intel processors`
  * `AnalyticsDatabaseSystems-M5-Intel(-Tpm)` - BIOS Policy for Analytics Database Decision-Support System on Cisco UCS M5 Servers with Intel processors`
  * `Analytics-M8-AMD(-Tpm)` - BIOS Policy for Analytical Database Systems on Cisco UCS M8 Servers with AMD processors`
  * `CPUIntensive-M6-Intel(-Tpm)` - BIOS Policy for CPU Intensive on Cisco UCS M6 Servers with Intel processors`
  * `CPUIntensive-M7-Intel(-Tpm)` - BIOS Policy for CPU Intensive on Cisco UCS M7 Servers with Intel processors`
  * `CPUIntensive-M8-AMD(-Tpm)` - BIOS Policy for CPU Intensive on Cisco UCS M8 Servers with AMD processors`
  * `ComputationIntensive-M6-AMD(-Tpm)` - BIOS Policy for CPU Intensive on Cisco UCS M6 Servers with AMD processors`

intersight.bios.description: Policy for setting BIOS tokens on the endpoint.
intersight.bios.title: bios - Bios - Intersight Uri: bios/Policies

allOf.description: Boot order policy models a reusable boot order configuration that can be applied to multiple servers via profile association. It supports advanced boot order configuration on Cisco CIMC servers.

boot_devices.description:
List of Boot Devices and their Attributes to Assign to the Boot Policy.

`flex_mmc` - UCS Standalone Only - Required Attributes:`
  * `device_name`
  * `device_type`

`flex_mmc` - Optional Attributes:
  * `subtype` - Default is `flexmmc-mapped-dvd`.

`http_boot` Required Attributes:`
  * `device_name`
  * `device_type`
  * `interface_source`

`http_boot` Optional Attributes:
  * `enabled` - Default is `true`.
  * `interface_name` - Required if `interface_source` is `name`.
  * `ip_config_type` - Default is `DHCP`.
  * `ip_type` - Default is `IPv4`.
  * `ipv4_config` - Required if `ip_config_type` is `Static` and `ip_type` is `IPv4`.
  * `ipv6_config` - Required if `ip_config_type` is `Static` and `ip_type` is `IPv6`.
  * `mac_address` - Required if `interface_source` is `mac`.
  * `port` - Required if `interface_source` is `port`.
  * `protocol` - Default is `HTTPS`.
  * `slot` - Default is `MLOM`.
  * `uri` - Required for Static IPv4/IPv6.  Note: Must be FQDN is protocol is `HTTPS`

`iscsi_boot` Required Attributes:
  * `device_name`
  * `device_type`
  * `interface_name`

`iscsi_boot` Optional Attributes:
  * `bootloader`
  * `enabled` - Default is `true`.
  * `port`
  * `slot`

`local_cdd` Required Attributes:`
  * `device_name`
  * `device_type`

`local_cdd` Optional Attributes:
  * `enabled` - Default is `true`.

`local_disk` Required Attributes:`
  * `device_name`
  * `device_type`

`local_disk` Optional Attributes:
  * `bootloader`
  * `enabled` - Default is `true`.
  * `slot` - Default is `MSTOR-RAID`.

`nvme` Required Attributes:`
  * `device_name`
  * `device_type`

`nvme` Optional Attributes:
  * `bootloader`
  * `enabled` - Default is `true`.

`pch_storage` Required Attributes:`
  * `device_name`
  * `device_type`

`pch_storage` Optional Attributes:
  * `bootloader`
  * `enabled` - Default is `true`.
  * `lun` - Default is `0`.

`pxe_boot` Required Attributes:`
  * `device_name`
  * `device_type`
  * `interface_source`

`pxe_boot` Optional Attributes:
  * `bootloader`
  * `enabled` - Default is `true`.
  * `interface_name` - Required if `interface_source` is `name`.
  * `ip_type` - Default is `IPv4`.
  * `mac_address` - Required if `interface_source` is `mac`.
  * `port` - Required if interface_source is `port`.
  * `slot` - Default is `MLOM`.

`san_boot` Required Attributes:
  * `device_name`
  * `device_type`
  * `interface_name`
  * `target_wwpn`

`san_boot` Optional Attributes:
  * `bootloader`
  * `enabled` - Default is `true`.
  * `lun` - Default is `0`.
  * `slot` - Default is `MLOM`.

`sd_card` Required Attributes:
  * `device_name`
  * `device_type`
  * `subtype` - Default is `SDCARD`.

`sd_card` Optional Attributes:
  * `bootloader`
  * `enabled` - Default is `true`.
  * `lun` - Default is `0`.

`uefi_shell` Required Attributes:
  * `device_name`
  * `device_type`

`uefi_shell` Optional Attributes:
  * `enabled` - Default is `true`.

`usb` Required Attributes:`
  * `device_name`
  * `device_type`
  * `subtype` - Default is `usb-cd`.

`usb` Optional Attributes:
  * `enabled` - Default is `true`.

`virtual_media` Required Attributes:
  * `device_name`
  * `device_type`
  * `subtype` - Default is `kvm-mapped-dvd`.

`virtual_media` Optional Attributes:
  * `enabled` - Default is `true`.
boot_devices.title: boot_devices - Boot Devices - object_type: boot.DeviceBase

boot_mode.description:
The default value is `Uefi`.  Sets the BIOS boot mode. UEFI uses the GUID Partition Table (GPT) whereas Legacy mode uses the MBR partitioning scheme. To apply this setting, Please reboot the server.
  * `Uefi` - UEFI mode uses the GUID Partition Table (GPT) to locate EFI Service Partitions to boot from.
  * `Legacy` - Legacy mode refers to the traditional process of booting from BIOS. Legacy mode uses the MBR to locate the bootloader.
boot_mode.title: boot_mode

enable_secure_boot.description: The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true`.  If UEFI secure boot is enabled, the boot mode is set to UEFI by default. Secure boot enforces that device boots using only software that is trusted by the Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM).
enable_secure_boot.title: enable_secure_boot

allOf.title: boot_order

intersight.boot_order.description: Boot order policy models a reusable boot order configuration that can be applied to multiple servers via profile association. It supports advanced boot order configuration on Cisco CIMC servers.
intersight.boot_order.title: boot_order - Boot Order - Intersight Uri: boot/PrecisionPolicies

intersight.boot_order.boot_devices.description: Lists properties that are common to all device types. It serves as base class for other device types.

device_name.description: The default value is `default`. A name that helps identify a boot device. It must start and end with an alphanumeric character, may include underscores and hyphens, and can be 1-30 characters long. The name cannot be a reserved keyword by itself (`all`, `ALL`, `CDROM`, `EFI`, `EOD`, `FDD`, `HDD`, `HDDANY`, `HTTP`, `ISCSI`, `ISCSIANY`, `LOCALCDD`, `LOCALHDD`, `NULL`, `NVME`, `NVMEANY`, `PCHSTORAGE`, `CHSTORANY`, `PXE`, `SAN`, `SANANY`, `SDANY`, `SDCARD`, `UEFISHELL`, `USB`, `USBCD`, `USBFDD`, `USBHDD`, `VMCIMCCD`, `VMCIMCHDD`, `VMEDIA`, `VMFDD`, `VMKVMCD`, `VMKVMHDD`). Reserved words can be used when combined with other characters (for example, `EU_CDROM-3`).
device_name.title: device_name

device_type.description:
The default value is `local_disk`.  The Boot Order Type to Assign to the Boot Device.  Allowed Values are:
  * `flex_mmc` - UCS Standalone Only
  * `http_boot`
  * `iscsi_boot`
  * `local_cdd`
  * `local_disk`
  * `nvme`
  * `pch_storage`
  * `pxe_boot`
  * `san_boot`
  * `sd_card`
  * `uefi_shell`
  * `usb`
  * `virtual_media`
device_type.title: device_type

enabled.description: The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true`.  Specifies if the boot device is enabled or disabled.
enabled.title: enabled

interface_name.description: The default value is `vnic0`.  The name of the underlying virtual ethernet interface used by the PXE or SAN boot device.
interface_name.title: interface_name

interface_source.description:
The default value is ``.  The default value is `name` when `device_type` is `http_boot` or `pxe_boot`.  Lists the supported Interface Source for device. Supported values are:
  * `name` - Use interface name to select virtual ethernet interface.
  * `mac` - Use MAC address to select virtual ethernet interface.
  * `port` - Use port to select virtual ethernet interface.
interface_source.title: interface_source

ip_config_type.description:
The default value is ``.  The default value is `DHCP` when `device_type` is `http_boot`.  The IP config type to use during the `http_boot` process. For DHCP configuration, the IP address, DNS server, netmask and gateway details are obtained from DHCP server. For static configuration, please provide the IP address, DNS server, netmask, and gateway details.
  * `DHCP` - The type of the IP config is DHCP.
  * `Static` - The type of the IP config is Static.
ip_config_type.title: ip_config_type

ip_type.description:
The default value is ``.  The default value is `IPv4` when `device_type` is `http_boot` or `pxe_boot`.  The IP Address family type to use during the `pxe_boot` and `http_boot` process(s).
  * `IPv4` - The IPv4 address family type.
  * `IPv6` - The IPv6 address family type.
ip_type.title: ip_type

lun.description: The default value is `0`.  The default value is `1`.  The Logical Unit Number (LUN) of the device. For `san_boot` configuration to be deployed on a server with 1300 family of Cisco VIC adapters, the recommendation is for the boot LUN to be numbered as 0 to ensure that LUN is mounted as the first disk from which the server boots.
lun.title: lun

mac_address.description: The default value is `00:25:B5:0A:00:10`.  The MAC Address of the underlying virtual ethernet interface used by `http_boot` and `pxe_boot` when `interface_source` is `mac`.
mac_address.title: mac_address

port.description: The default value is `-1`.  `iscsi_boot` can be 0-255.  `pxe_boot` is the Port ID of the adapter and values can be between -1 (not defined) and 255.
port.title: port

protocol.description:
The default value is ``.  The default value is `HTTPS` when `device_type` is `http_boot`.  Protocol to be used for HTTP boot. HTTPS require root certificate for authentication.
  * `HTTPS` - Secure HTTP protocol, certificate required for authentication.
  * `HTTP` - HTTP protocol without security certificate requirement.
protocol.title: protocol

slot.description: The default value is `MSTOR-RAID`.  Options for `iscsi_boot`, `pxe_boot` and `san_boot` are 1-255, MLOM, L1, L2.  `scsi_boot` and `pxe_boot` also support L and OCP.  For `local_disk` values can be 1-205, FMEZZ1-SAS, HBA, M, MRAID, MRAID1, MRAID2, MSTOR-RAID, SAS, RAID.
slot.title: slot

subtype.description:
The default value is `None`.  The subtype for the selected device type.

The Following Boot Order Device Types utilize the defined Subtype Attribute:

`flex_mmc` - Below are the Supported Subtype Values:`
  * `None` - No sub type for FlexMMC boot device.
  * `flexmmc-mapped-dvd` - Use of FlexMMC DVD as subtype for embedded storage.
  * `flexmmc-mapped-hdd` - Use of FlexMMC HDD as subtype for embedded storage.

`sd_card` - Below are the Supported Subtype Values:`
  * `None` - No sub type for SD card boot device.
  * `flex-util` - Use of FlexUtil (microSD) card as sub type for SD card boot device.
  * `flex-flash` - Use of FlexFlash (SD) card as sub type for SD card boot device.
  * `SDCARD` - Use of SD card as sub type for the SD Card boot device.

`usb` - Below are the Supported Subtype Values:`
  * `None` - No sub type for USB boot device.
  * `usb-cd` - Use of Compact Disk (CD) as sub-type for the USB boot device.
  * `usb-fdd` - Use of Floppy Disk Drive (FDD) as sub-type for the USB boot device.
  * `usb-hdd` - Use of Hard Disk Drive (HDD) as sub-type for the USB boot device.

`virtual_media` - Below are the Supported Subtype Values:`
  * `None - No sub type for virtual media.
  * `cimc-mapped-dvd` - The virtual media device is mapped to a virtual DVD device.
  * `cimc-mapped-hdd` - The virtual media device is mapped to a virtual HDD device.
  * `kvm-mapped-dvd` - A KVM mapped DVD virtual media device.
  * `kvm-mapped-fdd` - A KVM mapped FDD virtual media device.
  * `kvm-mapped-hdd` - A KVM mapped HDD virtual media device.
subtype.title: subtype

target_wwpn.description: The default value is ``.  The WWPN Address of the Target fibre channel interface used by the SAN boot device. Value must be in hexadecimal format xx:xx:xx:xx:xx:xx:xx:xx.
target_wwpn.title: target_wwpn

uri.description: The default value is ``.  Must be FQDN if `protocol` is `HTTPS`.  Boot resource location in URI format.  Required if device_type is `http_boot` and ip_config_type is `Static`.
uri.title: uri

intersight.boot_order.boot_devices.title: boot_device

intersight.boot_order.boot_devices.boot_.description: Details of the bootloader to be used.

description.description:
The default value is ``.  Carries more information about the bootloader.  Optional for Uefi.

Required Attributes:
  * `name`
  * `path

Optional Attributes:
  * `description`
description.title: description

name.description: The default value is `BOOTx64.EFI`.  Name of the bootloader image.  Optional for Uefi.
name.title: name

path.description: The default value is `\EFI\BOOT\`.  Path to the bootloader image.  Optional for Uefi, used by boot.Iscsi, boot.LocalDisk, boot.Nvme, boot.PchStorage, boot.San, boot.SdCard
path.title: path

intersight.boot_order.boot_devices.boot_.title: bootloader - Boot Loader Parameters

intersight.boot_order.boot_devices.ipv4_.description:
Http Boot Static IPv4 Settings.  Options are:

  * `dns_ip`
  * `gateway_ip`
  * `network_mask`
  * `static_ip

dns_ip.description: The default value is `208.67.220.220`.  IP address for the DNS server.  Example `208.67.220.220`.
dns_ip.title: dns_ip

gateway_ip.description: The default value is `198.18.0.1`.  IP address of the default gateway.  Example `198.18.0.1`.
gateway_ip.title: gateway_ip

network_mask.description: The default value is `255.255.255.0`.  Network mask for IPv4 Static Configuration.  Example `255.255.255.0`.
network_mask.title: network_mask

static_ip.description: The default value is `198.18.0.10`.  'Ipv4 static address.  Example `198.18.0.10`.
static_ip.title: static_ip

intersight.boot_order.boot_devices.ipv4_.title: ipv4_config - Http Boot Static IPv4 Configuration.

intersight.boot_order.boot_devices.ipv6_.description:
Http Boot Static IPv6 Settings.  Options are:

  * `dns_ip`
  * `gateway_ip`
  * `prefix_length`
  * `static_ip

dns_ip.description: The default value is `2620:119:53::53`.  IP address for the DNS server.  Example `2620:119:53::53`.
dns_ip.title: dns_ip

gateway_ip.description: The default value is `2001:db8::1`.  IP address of the default gateway.  Example `2001:db8::1`.
gateway_ip.title: gateway_ip

prefix_length.description: The default value is `64`.  A prefix length which masks the IP address and divides the IP address into network address and host address.  Example `255.255.255.0`.
prefix_length.title: prefix_length

static_ip.description: The default value is `2001:db8::10`.  'Ipv6 static address.  Example `2001:db8::10`.
static_ip.title: static_ip

intersight.boot_order.boot_devices.ipv6_.title: ipv6_config - Http Boot Static IPv6 Configuration.

allOf.description: Definition of the list of properties defined in 'certificatemanagement.Policy', excluding properties defined in parent classes.

assigned_sensitive_data.description: The default value is `False`.  The default value is `false`.  Flag to confirm that you have set the file location of the PEM Certificate File.  If false the Drive Security Policy will not run.
assigned_sensitive_data.title: assigned_sensitive_data

certificates.description:
List of Certificates and their Properties.  Each certificate policy can only have one IMC certificate.  Multiple RootCA certificates are supported for each policy.

IMC Certificate Required Attributes are:
  * `certificate_file`
  * `private_key_file`
  * `type`

IMC Certificate Optional Attributes:
  * `cert_type: KMIPClient - Set KMIP certificate on the selected end point`
  * `enabled`

RootCA Certificate Required Attributes are:
  * `certificate_file`
  * `name`
  * `type`

RootCA Certificate Optional Attributes:
  * `enabled`
certificates.title: certificates

allOf.title: certificate_management

intersight.certificate_management.description: Certificate Management policy models a reusable certificate and private key configuration that can be applied to multiple servers via profile association.
intersight.certificate_management.title: certificate_management - Certificate Management - Intersight Uri: certificatemanagement/Policies

certificate_file.description: Absolute path to the certificate file on the host. File must be in PEM format. Example: `/etc/pki/certs/server.crt` or `/opt/certs/certificate.pem`. For IMC type, both certificate_file and private_key_file are required. For RootCA type, only certificate_file is required.
certificate_file.title: certificate_file

cert_type.description:
The default value is `None`.  Certificate Type for the certificate management.
  * `None` - Set certificate on the selected end point .
  * `KMIPClient` - Set KMIP certificate on the selected end point.
cert_type.title: cert_type

enabled.description: The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true`.  Enable/Disable the certificate in Certificate Management policy.
enabled.title: enabled

name.description: The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true`.  Enable/Disable the certificate in Certificate Management policy.
name.title: name

private_key_file.description: Absolute path to the private key file on the host. File must be in PEM format. Example: `/etc/pki/private/server.key` or `/opt/certs/private-key.pem`. Required for IMC type certificates only.
private_key_file.title: private_key_file

type.description:
The default value is `IMC`.  Certificate Object Type to Assign.
  * `IMC` - Set the object_type to `certificatemanagement.Imc`.
  * `RootCA` -  Set the object_type to `certificatemanagement.RootCaCertificate`.
type.title: type

configuration_from_intersight_only.description: The default value is `False`.  The default value is `true`.  Enables configuration lockout on the endpoint.

intersight.drive_security.description: The drive security policy models the KMIP server related configuration that can be applied on multiple servers. Storage controller encryption can be enabled through this policy using remote keys from a KMIP server.

intersight.drive_security.manual_key.description:
Definition of the list of properties defined in 'storage.LocalKeySetting', excluding properties defined in parent classes.

Required Attributes:
  * `new_security_key_passphrase`

Optional Attributes:
  * `current_security_key_passphrase`

current_security_key_passphrase.description: The default value is `0`.  Current Security Key Passphrase which is already configured on the server. From the security context, the passphrase should be at least 8 characters long and should include at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one number, and one special character.  A number Between 1-64 that maps to variable `drive_security_current_security_key_passphrase_[1-64]`.  i.e. `export drive_security_current_security_key_passphrase_1='cisco123'`.
current_security_key_passphrase.title: current_security_key_passphrase

new_security_key_passphrase.description: The default value is `1`.  New Security Key Passphrase to be configured on the server. From the security context, the passphrase should be at least 8 characters long and should include at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one number, and one special character.  A number Between 1-64 that maps to variable `drive_security_new_security_key_passphrase_[1-64]`.  i.e. `export drive_security_new_security_key_passphrase_1='cisco123'`.
new_security_key_passphrase.title: new_security_key_passphrase

current_security_key_passphrase.description: The default value is `0`.  Current Security Key Passphrase which is already configured on the server. From the security context, the passphrase should be at least 8 characters long and should include at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one number, and one special character.  A number Between 1-64 to denote the variables drive_security_current_security_key_passphrase_[1-64].  i.e. `export drive_security_current_security_key_passphrase_1='cisco123'`.
current_security_key_passphrase.title: current_security_key_passphrase

server_public_root_ca_certificate.description: The default value is `1`.  The server public root ca certificate sensitive variable identifier.  A number Between 1-64 to denote the variables drive_security_server_ca_certificate_[1-64].  i.e. `export drive_security_server_ca_certificate_1='cisco123'`.

allOf.description: An Ethernet adapter policy governs the host-side behavior of the adapter, including how the adapter handles traffic. For each VIC Virtual Ethernet Interface various features like VXLAN, NVGRE, ARFS, Interrupt settings, and TCP Offload settings can be configured.

adapter_template.description:
The default value is `VMware`.  Name of a Pre-Configured Adapter Template.  Options are:

  * `16RxQs-4G`
  * `16RxQs-5G`
  * `Linux`
  * `Linux-NVMe-RoCE`
  * `Linux-v2`
  * `MQ`
  * `MQ-SMBd`
  * `MQ-SMBd-v2`
  * `MQ-v2`
  * `SMBClient`
  * `SMBServer`
  * `Solaris`
  * `SRIOV-HPN`
  * `usNIC`
  * `usNICOracleRAC`
  * `VMware`
  * `VMwareNVMeRoCEv2`
  * `VMwarePassThru`
  * `VMware-High-Trf`
  * `VMware-v2`
  * `Win-AzureStack`
  * `Win-HPN`
  * `Win-HPN-SMBd`
  * `Win-HPN-SMBd-v2`
  * `Win-HPN-v2`
  * `Windows`
adapter_template.title: adapter_template

enable_accelerated_receive_flow_steering.description: The default value is `False`.  The default value is `false`.  Status of Accelerated Receive Flow Steering on the virtual ethernet interface.
enable_accelerated_receive_flow_steering.title: enable_accelerated_receive_flow_steering

enable_advanced_filter.description: The default value is `False`.  The default value is `false`.  Enables advanced filtering on the interface.
enable_advanced_filter.title: enable_advanced_filter

enable_ether_channel_pinning.description: The default value is `False`.  The default value is `false`.  Enables Ether Channel Pinning to combine multiple physical links between two network switches into a single logical link. Transmit Queue Count should be at least 2 to enable ether channel pinning.
enable_ether_channel_pinning.title: enable_ether_channel_pinning

enable_geneve_offload.description: The default value is `False`.  The default value is `false`.  GENEVE offload protocol allows you to create logical networks that span physical network boundaries by allowing any information to be encoded in a packet and passed between tunnel endpoints.
enable_geneve_offload.title: enable_geneve_offload

enable_interrupt_scaling.description: The default value is `False`.  The default value is `false`.  Enables Interrupt Scaling on the interface.
enable_interrupt_scaling.title: enable_interrupt_scaling

enable_nvgre_offload.description: The default value is `False`.  The default value is `false`.  Network Virtualization using Generic Routing Encapsulation Settings.
enable_nvgre_offload.title: enable_nvgre_offload

enable_precision_time_protocol.description: The default value is `False`.  The default value is `false`.  Status of Precision Time Protocol (PTP) on the virtual ethernet interface. PTP can be enabled only on one vNIC on an adapter.
enable_precision_time_protocol.title: enable_precision_time_protocol

enable_vxlan_offload.description: The default value is `False`.  The default value is `false`.  Status of the Virtual Extensible LAN Protocol on the virtual ethernet interface.
enable_vxlan_offload.title: enable_vxlan_offload

receive_side_scaling_enable.description: The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true`.  Receive Side Scaling allows the incoming traffic to be spread across multiple CPU cores.
receive_side_scaling_enable.title: receive_side_scaling_enable

uplink_failback_timeout.description: The default value is `5`.  Uplink Failback Timeout in seconds when uplink failover is enabled for a vNIC. After a vNIC has started using its secondary interface, this setting controls how long the primary interface must be available before the system resumes using the primary interface for the vNIC.
uplink_failback_timeout.title: uplink_failback_timeout

allOf.title: ethernet_adapter

intersight.ethernet_adapter.description: An Ethernet adapter policy governs the host-side behavior of the adapter, including how the adapter handles traffic. For each VIC Virtual Ethernet Interface various features like VXLAN, NVGRE, ARFS, Interrupt settings, and TCP Offload settings can be configured.
intersight.ethernet_adapter.title: ethernet_adapter - Ethernet Adapter - Intersight Uri: vnic/EthAdapterPolicies

intersight.ethernet_adapter.completion.description:
Ethernet Adapter Policy - Completion Queue resource settings.  Options are:

  * `queue_count`
  * `ring_size`

queue_count.description: The default value is `5`.  The number of completion queue resources to allocate. In general, the number of completion queue resources to allocate is equal to the number of transmit queue resources plus the number of receive queue resources.
queue_count.title: queue_count

ring_size.description: The default value is `1`.  The number of descriptors in each completion queue.
ring_size.title: ring_size

intersight.ethernet_adapter.completion.title: completion - Completion Queue Settings - object_type: vnic.CompletionQueueSettings

intersight.ethernet_adapter.interrupt_se.description:
Ethernet Adapter Policy - Interupt Settings.  Otpions are:
  * `coalescing_type`
  * `interrupts`
  * `mode`
  * `timer

coalescing_type.description:
The default value is `MIN`.  Interrupt Coalescing Type. This can be one of the following:- MIN  - The system waits for the time specified in the Coalescing Time field before sending another interrupt event IDLE - The system does not send an interrupt until there is a period of no activity lasting as least as long as the time specified in the Coalescing Time field.
  * `MIN` - The system waits for the time specified in the Coalescing Time field before sending another interrupt event.
  * `IDLE` - The system does not send an interrupt until there is a period of no activity lasting as least as long as the time specified in the Coalescing Time field.
coalescing_type.title: coalescing_type

interrupts.description: The default value is `8`.  The number of interrupt resources to allocate. Typical value is be equal to the number of completion queue resources.
interrupts.title: interrupts

mode.description:
The default value is `MSIx`.  Preferred driver interrupt mode. This can be one of the following:- MSIx - Message Signaled Interrupts (MSI) with the optional extension. MSI  - MSI only. INTx - PCI INTx interrupts. MSIx is the recommended option.
  * `MSIx` - Message Signaled Interrupt (MSI) mechanism with the optional extension (MSIx). MSIx is the recommended and default option.
  * `MSI` - Message Signaled Interrupt (MSI) mechanism that treats messages as interrupts.
  * `INTx` - Line-based interrupt (INTx) mechanism similar to the one used in Legacy systems.
mode.title: mode

timer.description: The default value is `125`.  The time to wait between interrupts or the idle period that must be encountered before an interrupt is sent. To turn off interrupt coalescing, enter 0 (zero) in this field.
timer.title: timer

intersight.ethernet_adapter.interrupt_se.title: interrupt_settings - Interrupt Settings - object_type: vnic.EthInterruptSettings

intersight.ethernet_adapter.receive.description:
Ethernet Adapter Policy - Receive Queue Settings.  Options are:

  * `queue_count`
  * `ring_size`

queue_count.description: The default value is `4`.  The number of queue resources to allocate.
queue_count.title: queue_count

ring_size.description: The default value is `512`.  The number of descriptors in each queue.
ring_size.title: ring_size

intersight.ethernet_adapter.receive.title: receive - Receive Queue Settings - object_type: vnic.EthRxQueueSettings

intersight.ethernet_adapter.receive_side.description:
Ethernet Adapter Policy - Receive Side Scaling Settings.  Options are:

  * `enable_ipv4_hash`
  * `enable_ipv6_extensions_hash`
  * `enable_ipv6_hash`
  * `enable_receive_side_scaling`
  * `enable_tcp_and_ipv4_hash`
  * `enable_tcp_and_ipv6_extensions_hash`
  * `enable_tcp_and_ipv6_hash`
  * `enable_udp_and_ipv4_hash`
  * `enable_udp_and_ipv6_hash`

enable_ipv4_hash.description: The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true`.  When enabled, the IPv4 address is used for traffic distribution.
enable_ipv4_hash.title: enable_ipv4_hash

enable_ipv6_extensions_hash.description: The default value is `False`.  The default value is `false`.  When enabled, the IPv6 extensions are used for traffic distribution.
enable_ipv6_extensions_hash.title: enable_ipv6_extensions_hash

enable_ipv6_hash.description: The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true`.  When enabled, the IPv6 address is used for traffic distribution.
enable_ipv6_hash.title: enable_ipv6_hash

enable_receive_side_scaling.description: The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true`.  Receive Side Scaling allows the incoming traffic to be spread across multiple CPU cores.
enable_receive_side_scaling.title: enable_receive_side_scaling

enable_tcp_and_ipv4_hash.description: The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true`.  When enabled, both the IPv4 address and TCP port number are used for traffic distribution.
enable_tcp_and_ipv4_hash.title: enable_tcp_and_ipv4_hash

enable_tcp_and_ipv6_extensions_hash.description: The default value is `False`.  The default value is `false`.  When enabled, both the IPv6 extensions and TCP port number are used for traffic distribution.
enable_tcp_and_ipv6_extensions_hash.title: enable_tcp_and_ipv6_extensions_hash

enable_tcp_and_ipv6_hash.description: The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true`.  When enabled, both the IPv6 address and TCP port number are used for traffic distribution.
enable_tcp_and_ipv6_hash.title: enable_tcp_and_ipv6_hash

enable_udp_and_ipv4_hash.description: The default value is `False`.  The default value is `false`.  When enabled, both the IPv4 address and UDP port number are used for traffic distribution.
enable_udp_and_ipv4_hash.title: enable_udp_and_ipv4_hash

enable_udp_and_ipv6_hash.description: The default value is `False`.  The default value is `false`.  When enabled, both the IPv6 address and UDP port number are used for traffic distribution.
enable_udp_and_ipv6_hash.title: enable_udp_and_ipv6_hash

intersight.ethernet_adapter.receive_side.title: receive_side_scaling - Recieve Side Scaling Hash Settings - object_type: vnic.RssHashSettings

intersight.ethernet_adapter.roce_setting.description:
Ethernet Adapter Policy - RoCE Settings.  Options are:

  * `class_of_service`
  * `enable_rdma_over_converged_ethernet`
  * `memory_regions`
  * `queue_pairs`
  * `resource_groups`
  * `version`

class_of_service.description:
The default value is `5`.  The Class of Service for RoCE on this virtual interface.
  * `5` - RDMA CoS Service Level 5.
  * `1` - RDMA CoS Service Level 1.
  * `2` - RDMA CoS Service Level 2.
  * `4` - RDMA CoS Service Level 4.
  * `6` - RDMA CoS Service Level 6.
class_of_service.title: class_of_service

enable_rdma_over_converged_ethernet.description: The default value is `False`.  The default value is `false`.  If enabled sets RDMA over Converged Ethernet (RoCE) on this virtual interface.
enable_rdma_over_converged_ethernet.title: enable_rdma_over_converged_ethernet

memory_regions.description: The default value is `131072`.  The number of memory regions per adapter. Recommended value = integer power of 2.
memory_regions.title: memory_regions

queue_pairs.description: The default value is `256`.  The number of queue pairs per adapter. Recommended value = integer power of 2.
queue_pairs.title: queue_pairs

resource_groups.description: The default value is `2`.  The number of resource groups per adapter. Recommended value = be an integer power of 2 greater than or equal to the number of CPU cores on the system for optimum performance.
resource_groups.title: resource_groups

version.description:
The default value is `1`.  Configure RDMA over Converged Ethernet (RoCE) version on the virtual interface. Only RoCEv1 is supported on Cisco VIC 13xx series adapters and only RoCEv2 is supported on Cisco VIC 14xx series adapters.
  * `1` - RDMA over Converged Ethernet Protocol Version 1.
  * `2` - RDMA over Converged Ethernet Protocol Version 2.
version.title: version

intersight.ethernet_adapter.roce_setting.title: roce_settings - ROCE Settings - object_type: vnic.RoceSettings

intersight.ethernet_adapter.tcp_offload.description:
Ethernet Adapter Policy - TCP Offload Settings.  Options are:

  * `enable_large_receive_offload`
  * `enable_large_send_offload`
  * `enable_rx_checksum_offload`
  * `enable_tx_checksum_offload`

enable_large_receive_offload.description: The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true`.  Enables the reassembly of segmented packets in hardware before sending them to the CPU.
enable_large_receive_offload.title: enable_large_receive_offload

enable_large_send_offload.description: The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true`.  Enables the CPU to send large packets to the hardware for segmentation.
enable_large_send_offload.title: enable_large_send_offload

enable_rx_checksum_offload.description: The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true`.  When enabled, the CPU sends all packet checksums to the hardware for validation.
enable_rx_checksum_offload.title: enable_rx_checksum_offload

enable_tx_checksum_offload.description: The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true`.  When enabled, the CPU sends all packets to the hardware so that the checksum can be calculated.
enable_tx_checksum_offload.title: enable_tx_checksum_offload

intersight.ethernet_adapter.tcp_offload.title: tcp_offload - TCP Offload Settings - object_type: vnic.TcpOffloadSettings

intersight.ethernet_adapter.transmit.description:
Ethernet Adapter Policy - Transmit Queue Settings.  Options are:

  * `queue_count`
  * `ring_size`

queue_count.description: The default value is `1`.  The number of queue resources to allocate.
queue_count.title: queue_count

ring_size.description: The default value is `256`.  The number of descriptors in each queue.
ring_size.title: ring_size

intersight.ethernet_adapter.transmit.title: transmit - Transmit Queue Settings - object_type: vnic.EthTxQueueSettings

allOf.description: An Ethernet Network policy determines if the port can carry single VLAN (Access) or multiple VLANs (Trunk) traffic. You can specify the VLAN to be associated with an Ethernet packet if no tag is found.

default_vlan.description: The default value is `0`.  Native VLAN ID of the virtual interface or the corresponding vethernet on the peer Fabric Interconnect to which the virtual interface is connected. Setting the ID to 0 will not associate any native VLAN to the traffic on the virtual interface.
default_vlan.title: default_vlan

qinq_vlan.description: The default value is `2`.  The VLAN ID for VIC QinQ (802.1Q-in-802.1Q) Tunneling.  Any value other than 0 will enable QnQ Tunneling.
qinq_vlan.title: qinq_vlan

vlan_mode.description:
The default value is `ACCESS`.  Option to determine if the port can carry single VLAN (Access) or multiple VLANs (Trunk) traffic.
  * `ACCESS` - An access port carries traffic only for a single VLAN on the interface.
  * `TRUNK` - A trunk port can have two or more VLANs configured on the interface. It can carry traffic for several VLANs simultaneously.
vlan_mode.title: vlan_mode

allOf.title: ethernet_network

intersight.ethernet_network.description: An Ethernet Network policy determines if the port can carry single VLAN (Access) or multiple VLANs (Trunk) traffic. You can specify the VLAN to be associated with an Ethernet packet if no tag is found.
intersight.ethernet_network.title: ethernet_network - Ethernet Network - Intersight Uri: vnic/EthNetworkPolicies

action_on_uplink_fail.description:
The default value is `linkDown`.  Determines the state of the virtual interface (vethernet / vfc) on the switch when a suitable uplink is not pinned.
  * `linkDown` - The vethernet will go down in case a suitable uplink is not pinned.
  * `warning` - The vethernet will remain up even if a suitable uplink is not pinned.
action_on_uplink_fail.title: action_on_uplink_fail

cdp_enable.description: The default value is `False`.  The default value is `false`.  Enables CDP on an interface.
cdp_enable.title: cdp_enable

lldp_enable_receive.description: The default value is `False`.  The default value is `false`.  Determines if the LLDP frames can be received by an interface on the switch.
lldp_enable_receive.title: lldp_enable_receive

lldp_enable_transmit.description: The default value is `False`.  The default value is `false`.  Determines if the LLDP frames can be transmitted by an interface on the switch.
lldp_enable_transmit.title: lldp_enable_transmit

mac_register_mode.description:
The default value is `nativeVlanOnly`.  Determines the MAC addresses that have to be registered with the switch.
  * `nativeVlanOnly` - Register only the MAC addresses learnt on the native VLAN.
  * `allVlans` - Register all the MAC addresses learnt on all the allowed VLANs.
mac_register_mode.title: mac_register_mode

mac_security_forge.description:
The default value is `allow`.  Determines if the MAC forging is allowed or denied on an interface.
  * `allow` - Allows mac forging on an interface.
  * `deny` - Denies mac forging on an interface.
mac_security_forge.title: mac_security_forge

intersight.ethernet_network_control.description: The features that are applied on a vethernet that is connected to the vNIC.
intersight.ethernet_network_control.title: ethernet_network_control - Ethernet Network Control - Intersight Uri: fabric/EthNetworkControlPolicies

allowed_vlans.description: The default value is `1-10`.  Allowed VLAN IDs of the vnic or ethernet uplink. A list of comma seperated VLAN ids and/or VLAN id ranges.
allowed_vlans.title: action_on_uplink_fail

native_vlan.description: The default value is `1`.  Native VLAN ID of the vnic or ethernet uplink. If the native VLAN is not a part of the allowed VLANs, it will automatically be added to the list of allowed VLANs.
native_vlan.title: native_vlan

qinq_vlan.description: The default value is `0`.  The VLAN ID for VIC QinQ (802.1Q-in-802.1Q) Tunneling.  Any value other than 0 will enable QnQ Tunneling.
qinq_vlan.title: qinq_vlan

intersight.ethernet_network_group.description: The allowed VLAN/s on an interface.
intersight.ethernet_network_group.title: ethernet_network_group - Ethernet Network Group - Intersight Uri: fabric/EthNetworkGroupPolicies

allOf.description: An Ethernet Quality of Service (QoS) policy assigns a system class to the outgoing traffic for a vNIC. This system class determines the quality of service for the outgoing traffic. For certain adapters additional controls can be specified like burst and rate on the outgoing traffic.

burst.description: The default value is `10240`.  The burst traffic, in bytes, allowed on the vNIC.
burst.title: burst

class_of_service.description: The default value is `0`.  Class of Service to be associated to the traffic on the virtual interface.
class_of_service.title: class_of_service

enable_trust_host_cos.description: The default value is `False`.  The default value is `false`.  Enables usage of the Class of Service provided by the operating system.
enable_trust_host_cos.title: enable_trust_host_cos

mtu.description: The default value is `1500`.  The Maximum Transmission Unit (MTU) or packet size that the virtual interface accepts.
mtu.title: mtu

priority.description:
The default value is `Best Effort`.  The priority matching the System QoS specified in the fabric profile.
  * `Best Effort` - QoS Priority for Best-effort traffic.
  * `FC` - QoS Priority for FC traffic.
  * `Platinum` - QoS Priority for Platinum traffic.
  * `Gold` - QoS Priority for Gold traffic.
  * `Silver` - QoS Priority for Silver traffic.
  * `Bronze` - QoS Priority for Bronze traffic.
priority.title: priority

rate_limit.description: The default value is `0`.  The value in Mbps (0-10G/40G/100G depending on Adapter Model) to use for limiting the data rate on the virtual interface.
rate_limit.title: rate_limit

allOf.title: ethernet_qos

intersight.ethernet_qos.description: An Ethernet Quality of Service (QoS) policy assigns a system class to the outgoing traffic for a vNIC. This system class determines the quality of service for the outgoing traffic. For certain adapters additional controls can be specified like burst and rate on the outgoing traffic.
intersight.ethernet_qos.title: ethernet_qos - Ethernet QoS - Intersight Uri: vnic/EthQosPolicies

fc_target_zoning_type.description:
The default value is `SIMT`.  Type of FC zoning. Allowed values are SIST, SIMT and None.
  * `SIST` - `a.k.a - Single Initiator Single Target`, The system automatically creates one zone for each vHBA and storage port pair. Each zone has two members.
  * `SIMT` - `a.k.a - Single Initiator Multiple Target` The system automatically creates one zone for each vHBA. Configure this type of zoning if the number of zones created is likely to exceed the maximum supported number of zones.
  * `None` - FC zoning is not configured.
fc_target_zoning_type.title: fc_target_zoning_type

targets.description:
List of target WWPN members that are part of the zone.

Required Attributes:
  * `name`
  * `switch_id`
  * `vsan_id`
  * `wwpn`
targets.title: targets - Fc Target Members - object_type: fabric.FcZoneMember

intersight.fc_zone.description: List of target path entries of storage arrays that are used to configure zones on the switch.
intersight.fc_zone.title: fc_zone - FC Zone - Intersight Uri: fabric/FcZonePolicies

intersight.fc_zone.targets.description: SAN target or WWPN that is to be part of the FC zone.

switch_id.description:
The default value is `A`.  Unique identifier for the Fabric object.
  * `A` - Switch Identifier of Fabric Interconnect A.
  * `B` - Switch Identifier of Fabric Interconnect B.
switch_id.title: switch_id

vsan_id.description: The default value is `3914`.  VSAN with scope defined as Storage in the VSAN policy.
vsan_id.title: vsan_id

wwpn.description: The default value is `20:00:00:25:B5:00:00:00`.  WWPN that is a member of the FC zone.
wwpn.title: wwpn

allOf.description: A Fibre Channel Adapter policy governs the host-side behavior of the adapter, including how the adapter handles traffic. You can enable FCP Error Recovery, change the default settings of Queues and Interrupt handling for performance enhancement.

adapter_template.description:
The default value is `VMware`.  Name of Pre-Configured Adapter Templates.  Options are:

  * `FCNVMeInitiator`
  * `FCNVMeTarget`
  * `Initiator`
  * `Linux`
  * `Solaris`
  * `Target`
  * `VMware`
  * `Windows`
  * `WindowsBoot`
adapter_template.title: adapter_template

error_detection_timeout.description: The default value is `2000`.  Error Detection Timeout, also referred to as EDTOV, is the number of milliseconds to wait before the system assumes that an error has occurred.
error_detection_timeout.title: error_detection_timeout

io_throttle_count.description: The default value is `512`.  The maximum number of data or control I/O operations that can be pending for the virtual interface at one time. If this value is exceeded, the additional I/O operations wait in the queue until the number of pending I/O operations decreases and the additional operations can be processed.
io_throttle_count.title: io_throttle_count

lun_queue_depth.description: The default value is `20`.  The number of commands that the HBA can send and receive in a single transmission per LUN.
lun_queue_depth.title: lun_queue_depth

maximum_luns_per_target.description: The default value is `1024`.  The maximum number of LUNs that the Fibre Channel driver will export or show. The maximum number of LUNs is usually controlled by the operating system running on the server. Lun Count value can exceed 1024 only for vHBA of type 'FC Initiator' and on servers having supported firmware version.
maximum_luns_per_target.title: maximum_luns_per_target

resource_allocation_timeout.description: The default value is `10000`.  Resource Allocation Timeout, also referred to as RATOV, is the number of milliseconds to wait before the system assumes that a resource cannot be properly allocated.
resource_allocation_timeout.title: resource_allocation_timeout

allOf.title: fibre_channel_adapter

intersight.fibre_channel_adapter.description: A Fibre Channel Adapter policy governs the host-side behavior of the adapter, including how the adapter handles traffic. You can enable FCP Error Recovery, change the default settings of Queues and Interrupt handling for performance enhancement.
intersight.fibre_channel_adapter.title: fibre_channel_adapter - Fibre Channel Adapter - Intersight Uri: vnic/FcAdapterPolicies

intersight.fibre_channel_adapter.error_r.description:
Fibre-Channel Adapter Policy - Error Recovery Settings.  Options are:

  * `enable_fcp_error_recovery`
  * `io_retry_timeout`
  * `link_down_timeout`
  * `port_down_io_retry`
  * `port_down_timeout`

enable_fcp_error_recovery.description: The default value is `False`.  The default value is `false`.  Enables Fibre Channel Error recovery.
enable_fcp_error_recovery.title: enable_fcp_error_recovery

io_retry_timeout.description: The default value is `5`.  The number of seconds the adapter waits before aborting the pending command and resending the same IO request.
io_retry_timeout.title: io_retry_timeout

link_down_timeout.description: The default value is `30000`.  The number of milliseconds the port should actually be down before it is marked down and fabric connectivity is lost.
link_down_timeout.title: link_down_timeout

port_down_io_retry.description: The default value is `8`.  The number of times an I/O request to a port is retried because the port is busy before the system decides the port is unavailable.
port_down_io_retry.title: port_down_io_retry

port_down_timeout.description: The default value is `10000`.  The number of milliseconds a remote Fibre Channel port should be offline before informing the SCSI upper layer that the port is unavailable. For a server with a VIC adapter running ESXi, the recommended value is 10000. For a server with a port used to boot a Windows OS from the SAN, the recommended value is 5000 milliseconds.
port_down_timeout.title: port_down_timeout

intersight.fibre_channel_adapter.error_r.title: error_recovery - Error Recovery Settings - object_type: vnic.FcErrorRecoverySettings

intersight.fibre_channel_adapter.flogi.description:
Fibre-Channel Adapter Policy - Error Recovery Settings.  Options are:

  * `retries`
  * `timeout`

intersight.fibre_channel_adapter.interru.description:
Fibre-Channel Adapter Policy - Interrupt Settings.  Options are:
  * `mode`

mode.description:
The default value is `MSIx`.  The preferred driver interrupt mode. This can be one of the following:  MSIx - Message Signaled Interrupts (MSI) with the optional extension. MSI  - MSI only. INTx - PCI INTx interrupts. MSIx is the recommended option.
  * `MSIx` - Message Signaled Interrupt (MSI) mechanism with the optional extension (MSIx). MSIx is the recommended and default option.
  * `MSI` - Message Signaled Interrupt (MSI) mechanism that treats messages as interrupts.
  * `INTx` - Line-based interrupt (INTx) mechanism similar to the one used in Legacy systems.
mode.title: mode

priority.description:
The default value is `auto`.    Default is `auto`.  Configure the Priority Flow Control (PFC) for each port to enable the no-drop behavior for the CoS defined by the System QoS Policy and an Ethernet QoS policy. If Auto and On is selected for PFC, the Receive and Send link level flow control will be Off.
  * `auto` - Enables the no-drop CoS values to be advertised by the DCBXP and negotiated with the peer.A successful negotiation enables PFC on the no-drop CoS.Any failures because of a mismatch in the capability of peers causes the PFC not to be enabled.
  * `on` - Enables PFC on the local port regardless of the capability of the peers.
  * `off` - Disable PFC on the local port regardless of the capability of the peers.
priority.title: priority

receive.description:
The default value is `Disabled`.  Link-level Flow Control configured in the receive direction.
  * `disabled` - Admin configured Disabled State.
  * `Enabled` - Admin configured Enabled State.
receive.title: receive

send.description:
The default value is `Disabled`.  Link-level Flow Control configured in the send direction.
  * `disabled` - Admin configured Disabled State.
  * `Enabled` - Admin configured Enabled State.
send.title: send

intersight.imc_access.description:
Enables you to manage and configure your network through mapping of IP pools to the server profile. This policy allows you to configure a VLAN and associate it with an IP address through the IP pool address.  In-Band IP address, Out-of-Band IP address, or both In-Band and Out-of-Band IP addresses can be configured using IMC Access Policy and are supported on the following:`
  * Drive Security, SNMP, Syslog, and vMedia policies
  * vKVM, IPMI, SOL, and vMedia policies using vKVM client

allOf.description: Intelligent Platform Management Interface Over LAN Policy.

enabled.description: The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true`.  State of the IPMI Over LAN service on the endpoint.
enabled.title: enabled

encryption_key.description: The default value is `1`.  The Encryption Key Sensitive Variable Identifier.  The encryption key to use for IPMI communication. It should have an even number of hexadecimal characters and not exceed 40 characters. Use “00” to disable encryption key use. This configuration is supported by all standalone rack servers. FI-attached rack servers with firmware at minimum of 4.2.3a support this configuration. FI-attached blade servers with firmware at minimum of 5.1.0.x support this configuration. IPMI commands using this key should append zeroes to the key to achieve a length of 40 characters.  A number Between 1-64 to denote the variables ipmi_key_[1-64].  i.e. `export ipmi_key_1='cisco123'`.
encryption_key.title: encryption_key

privilege.description:
The default value is `read-only`.  The highest privilege level that can be assigned to an IPMI session on a server. This configuration is supported by all standalone rack servers. FI-attached rack servers with firmware at minimum of 4.2.3a support this configuration. FI-attached blade servers do not support privilege level. Privilege level will be ignored for unsupported servers.
  * `admin` - Privilege to perform all actions available through IPMI.
  * `user` - Privilege to perform some functions through IPMI but restriction on performing administrative tasks.
  * `read-only` - Privilege to view information throught IPMI but restriction on making any changes.
privilege.title: privilege

  * `chap`
  * `mutual_chap`
  * `none`

dhcp_vendor_id_iqn.description: The default value is `iqn.1984-12.com.cisco:ucs000`.  Auto target interface that is represented via the Initiator name or the DHCP vendor ID. The vendor ID can be up to 32 alphanumeric characters.

initiator_ip_pool.title: initiator_ip_pool

initiator_ip_source.description:
The default value is `DHCP`.  Default is Source Type of Initiator IP Address - Auto/Static/Pool.
  * `DHCP` - The IP address is assigned using DHCP, if available.
  * `Static` - Static IPv4 address is assigned to the iSCSI boot interface based on the information entered in this area.
  * `Pool` - An IPv4 address is assigned to the iSCSI boot interface from the management IP address pool.
initiator_ip_source.title: initiator_ip_source

iscsi_adapter_policy.description: The default value is `default`.  The Name of the iSCSI Adapter Policy to Assign to the iSCSI Boot Policy. If this policy is originating from a shared organization, include the organization name with the policy via `org_name/policy_name`.

primary_target_policy.title: primary_target_policy

secondary_target_policy.description: The default value is ``.  The Name of the iSCSI Static Target Policy to Assign to the iSCSI Boot Policy. If this policy is originating from a shared organization, include the organization name with the policy via `org_name/policy_name`.
secondary_target_policy.title: secondary_target_policy

target_source_type.description:
The default value is `Auto`.  Source Type of Targets - Auto/Static.
  * `Static` - Type indicates that static target interface is assigned to iSCSI boot.
  * `Auto` - Type indicates that the system selects the target interface automatically during iSCSI boot.

intersight.iscsi_boot.description: Configuration parameters to enable a server to boot its operating system from an iSCSI target machine located remotely over a network.
intersight.iscsi_boot.title: iscsi_boot - iSCSI Boot - Intersight Uri: vnic/IscsiBootPolicies

intersight.iscsi_boot.initiator_static_i.description:
IPV4 configurations such as Netmask, Gateway and DNS for iSCSI Initiator.

Required Attributes:
  * `ip_address`
  * `gateway`
  * `netmask`

Optional Attributes:
  * `primary_dns`
  * `secondary_dns`

gateway.description: The default value is `198.18.0.1`.  Gateway to assign.  Example `198.18.0.1`.
gateway.title: gateway

ip_address.description: The default value is `198.18.0.10`.  The IPv4 address assigned to the iSCSI target.  Example `198.18.0.10`.
ip_address.title: ip_address

netmask.description: The default value is `255.255.255.0`.  A subnet mask is a 32-bit number that masks an IP address and divides the IP address into network address and host address.  Example `255.255.255.0`.
netmask.title: netmask

primary_dns.description: The default value is `208.67.220.220`.  IP Address of the primary Domain Name System (DNS) server.  Example `208.67.220.220`.
primary_dns.title: primary_dns

secondary_dns.description: The default value is `208.67.222.222`.  IP Address of the secondary Domain Name System (DNS) server.  Example `208.67.222.222`.
secondary_dns.title: secondary_dns

intersight.iscsi_boot.initiator_static_i.title: initiator_static_ipv4_config - Initiator Static Ipv4 Config

allOf.description: Configuration parameters that defines the reachability of iSCSI Target portal.

ip_address.description: The default value is `198.18.32.60`.  The IPv4 or IPv6 address assigned to the iSCSI target.
ip_address.title: IP Address - IPv4/IPv6

ip_protocol.description: The default value is `IPv4`.  Type of the IP address requested for iSCSI vNIC - IPv4/IPv6.
ip_protocol.title: IP Protocol

lun_id.description: The default value is `0`.  The Identifier of the LUN.
lun_id.title: lun_id

port.description: The default value is `3260`.  The port associated with the iSCSI target.
port.title: port

target_name.description: The default value is `iqn.1984-12.com.cisco:ucs000`.  Qualified Name (IQN) or Extended Unique Identifier (EUI) name of the iSCSI target.
target_name.title: target_name

allOf.title: iscsi_static_target

intersight.iscsi_static_target.title: iscsi_static_target - iSCSI Static Target - Intersight Uri: vnic/IscsiStaticTargetPolicies

enable_azure_stack_host_qos.description: The default value is `False`.  The default value is `false`.  Enabling AzureStack-Host QoS on an adapter allows the user to carve out traffic classes for RDMA traffic which ensures that a desired portion of the bandwidth is allocated to it.
enable_azure_stack_host_qos.title: enable_azure_stack_host_qos

iqn_pool.description: The default value is ``.  The default value is IQN Pool to Assign to the Policy. If this pool is originating from a shared organization, include the organization name with the pool via `org_name/pool_name`.
iqn_pool.title: iqn_pool

iqn_static_identifier.description: The default value is ``.  User provided static iSCSI Qualified Name (IQN) for use as initiator identifiers by iSCSI vNICs in a Fabric Interconnect domain.
iqn_static_identifier.title: iqn_static_identifier

target_platform.description:
The default value is `FIAttached`.  The platform for which the server profile is applicable. It can either be a server that is operating in standalone mode or which is attached to a Fabric Interconnect managed by Intersight.
  * `FIAttached` - Servers which are connected to a Fabric Interconnect that is managed by Intersight.
  * `Standalone` - Servers which are operating in standalone mode.
  * `UnifiedEdgeServer` - Servers which are part of a Unified Edge deployment.
target_platform.title: target_platform

vnic_placement_mode.description:
The default value is `custom`.  The mode used for placement of vNICs on network adapters. It can either be Auto or Custom.

  * `custom` - The placement of the vNICs / vHBAs on network adapters is manually chosen by the user.
  * `auto` - The placement of the vNICs / vHBAs on network adapters is automatically determined by the system.
vnic_placement_mode.title: vnic_placement_mode
