intersight.lan_connectivity.description: A LAN Connectivity Policy determines the network resources and the connections between the server and the LAN on the network. This policy uses Consistent Device Naming to configure the vNIC. You can configure a usNIC or VMQ connection for the vNIC to improve network performance.
intersight.lan_connectivity.title: lan_connectivity - LAN Connectivity - Intersight Uri: vnic/LanConnectivityPolicies

cdn_values.description: Only used when cdn_source is `user` (aka user defined).  A List of one or two CDN value(s), one for each vNIC.

items.description: The default value is `default`.  The CDN value entered in case of user defined mode.
items.title: cdn_value

cdn_values.title: cdn_values - Cdn Values

enable_failover.description: The default value is `False`.  The default value is `false`.  Enabling failover ensures that traffic from the vNIC automatically fails over to the secondary Fabric Interconnect, in case the specified Fabric Interconnect path goes down. Failover applies only to Cisco VICs that are connected to a Fabric Interconnect cluster.
enable_failover.title: enable_failover

ethernet_adapter_policy.description: The default value is `default`.  The Name of the Ethernet Adapter Policy to Assign to the vNIC. If this policy is originating from a shared organization, include the organization name with the policy via `org_name/policy_name`.
ethernet_adapter_policy.title: ethernet_adapter_policy

ethernet_network_control_policy.description: The default value is `default`.  The Name of the Ethernet Network Control Policy to Assign to the vNIC. If this policy is originating from a shared organization, include the organization name with the policy via `org_name/policy_name`.
ethernet_network_control_policy.title: ethernet_network_control_policy

ethernet_network_group_policies.description: The Name(s) of the Ethernet Network Group Policies to Assign.  If it is a list of 1 then the policy will be used by both vNICs.  If the list is greater than 1, then the list must be an even number, and it will be split between the vNICs. If this policy is originating from a shared organization, include the organization name with the policy via `org_name/policy_name`.

mac_address_pools.title: mac_address_pools - Mac Address Pools - Intersight Uri: macpool/Pools

mac_addresses_static.description:
A List of Static MAC Addresses to Assign to the vNIC's if the Allocation Type is set to STATIC.  The WWPN address must be in hexadecimal format xx:xx:xx:xx:xx:xx:xx:xx.
Allowed ranges are 20:00:00:00:00:00:00:00 to 20:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF or from 50:00:00:00:00:00:00:00 to 5F:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF.
To ensure uniqueness of WWN's in the SAN fabric, you are strongly encouraged to use the WWN prefix - 20:00:00:25:B5:xx:xx:xx.

items.description:
The default value is `default`.  The MAC address must be in hexadecimal format xx:xx:xx:xx:xx:xx.
To ensure uniqueness of MACs in the LAN fabric, you are strongly encouraged to use the
following MAC prefix 00:25:B5:xx:xx:xx.
items.title: mac_address

intersight.lan_connectivity.vnics.placem.description:
Placement Settings for the virtual interface.

Required Attributes:
  * `pci_order` and/or `switch_ids`

Optional Attributes:
  * `automatic_pci_link_assignment`
  * `automatic_slot_id_assignment`
  * `pci_links`
  * `slot_ids`
  * `uplink_ports`

automatic_pci_link_assignment.description: The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true` if `pci_links` is undefined, otherwise this is ignored.  Enable or disable automatic assignment of the PCI Link in a dual-link adapter. This option applies only to 13xx series VICs that support dual-link. If enabled, the system determines the placement of the vNIC/vHBA on either of the PCI Links.
automatic_pci_link_assignment.title: automatic_pci_link_assignment

automatic_slot_id_assignment.description: The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true` if `slot_ids` is undefined, otherwise this is ignored.  Enable or disable automatic assignment of the VIC slot ID. If enabled and the server has only one VIC, the same VIC is chosen for all the vNICs. If enabled and the server has multiple VICs, the vNIC/vHBA are deployed on the first VIC. The Slot ID determines the first VIC. MLOM is the first Slot ID and the ID increments to 2, 3, and so on.
automatic_slot_id_assignment.title: automatic_slot_id_assignment

pci_links.description: The PCI Link used as transport for the virtual interface. All VIC adapters have a single PCI link except VIC 1385 which has two.

items.description: The default value is `0`.  Adapter port on which the virtual interface will be created.  Range is `0-3`.
items.title: pci_link

pci_links.title: pci_links - Pci Link - Object Type: vnic.PlacementSettings

pci_order.description: The order in which the virtual interface is brought up. The order assigned to an interface should be unique for all the Ethernet and Fibre-Channel interfaces on each PCI link on a VIC adapter. The order should start from zero with no overlaps. The maximum value of PCI order is limited by the number of virtual interfaces (Ethernet and Fibre-Channel) on each PCI link on a VIC adapter. All VIC adapters have a single PCI link except VIC 1340, VIC 1380 and VIC 1385 which have two.

items.description: The default value is `0`.  PCI Order Assigned to the Interface. The order should start from zero with no overlaps, including LAN and SAN Policies for the PCI Link.
items.title: pci_order

pci_order.title: pci_order - Pci Order

slot_ids.description: List of PCIe Slots where the VIC adapter is installed. Supported values are (1-15) and MLOM.

items.description: The default value is ``.  PCIe Slot where the VIC adapter is installed. Supported values are (1-15) and MLOM.
items.title: slot_id

slot_ids.title: slot_ids - Slot Ids - Object Type: vnic.PlacementSettings

switch_ids.description:
A List of the fabric port(s) to which the vNICs will be associated.

  * `A` - Fabric A of the FI cluster.
  * `B` - Fabric B of the FI cluster.
  * `None` - Fabric Id is `None` for a standalone server use-case where the server is not connected to Fabric Interconnects.

items.description:
The default value is `None`.  The fabric port to which the vNICs will be associated.

  * `A` - Fabric A of the FI cluster.
  * `B` - Fabric B of the FI cluster.
  * `None` - Fabric Id is `None` for a standalone server use-case where the server is not connected to Fabric Interconnects.
items.title: switch_id

switch_ids.title: switch_ids - Switch Ids - Object Type: vnic.PlacementSettings

uplink_ports.description: Adapter port on which the virtual interface will be created.  This attribute is for Standalone Servers Only.

items.description: The default value is `0`.  Adapter port on which the virtual interface will be created.  Range is `0-3`.
items.title: pci_link

uplink_ports.title: uplink_ports - Uplink Port - Object Type: vnic.PlacementSettings

intersight.lan_connectivity.vnics.sriov.description:
Single Root Input Output Virtualization Settings that enable one physical ethernet socket to appear as multiple NICs to the hypervisor.

Note: If this is a vnic_from_template and Connection Settings (SR-IOV|usNIC|VMQ) have been configured in the template, you cannot configure with the vnic_from_template as well.

Required Attributes:
  * `enabled`

Optional Attributes:
  * `completion_queue_count_per_vf`
  * `interrupt_count_per_vf`
  * `number_of_vfs`
  * `receive_queue_count_per_vf`
  * `transmit_queue_count_per_vf`

completion_queue_count_per_vf.description: The default value is `5`.  Completion Queue resources per Virtual Function (VF).
completion_queue_count_per_vf.title: interrupt_count_per_vf

interrupt_count_per_vf.description: The default value is `8`.  The default value is `16`.  Interrupt Count resources per Virtual Function (VF).
interrupt_count_per_vf.title: interrupt_count_per_vf

number_of_vfs.description: The default value is `64`.  Number of Virtual Functions (VF) to be created for this vNIC. Valid values are 1 to 64 when SR-IOV is enabled.
number_of_vfs.title: number_of_vfs

receive_queue_count_per_vf.description: The default value is `4`.  Receive Queue resources per Virtual Function (VF).
receive_queue_count_per_vf.title: receive_queue_count_per_vf

transmit_queue_count_per_vf.description: The default value is `1`.  Transmit Queue resources per Virtual Function (VF).
transmit_queue_count_per_vf.title: transmit_queue_count_per_vf

intersight.lan_connectivity.vnics.sriov.title: sriov - SR-IOV Settings - object_type: vnic.SriovSettings

intersight.lan_connectivity.vnics.usnic.description:
User Space NIC Settings that enable low-latency and higher throughput by bypassing the kernel layer when sending/receiving packets.

Optional Attributes:
  * `class_of_service`
  * `number_of_usnics`
  * `usnic_adapter_policy`

class_of_service.description:
The default value is `5`.  The Class of Service for User Space NICs.

Note: Only for Standalone Servers.

  * `0` - CoS Service Level 0.
  * `1` - CoS Service Level 1.
  * `2` - CoS Service Level 2.
  * `3` - CoS Service Level 3.
  * `4` - CoS Service Level 4.
  * `5` - CoS Service Level 5.
  * `6` - CoS Service Level 6.
class_of_service.title: class_of_service

Virtual Machine Queue Settings for the virtual interface that allow efficient transfer of network traffic to the guest OS.

  * `number_of_sub_vnics`

enable_virtual_machine_multi_queue.description: The default value is `False`.  The default value is `false`.  Enables Virtual Machine Multi-Queue feature on the virtual interface. VMMQ allows configuration of multiple I/O queues for a single VM and thus distributes traffic across multiple CPU cores in a VM.
enable_virtual_machine_multi_queue.title: enable_virtual_machine_multi_queue

enabled.description: The default value is `False`.  The default value is `false`.  Enables VMQ feature on the virtual interface.

number_of_interrupts.description: The default value is `16`.  The number of interrupt resources to be allocated. Recommended value is the number of CPU threads or logical processors available in the server.

number_of_sub_vnics.description: The default value is `64`.  The number of sub vNICs to be created.

number_of_virtual_machine_queues.description: The default value is `4`.  The number of hardware Virtual Machine Queues to be allocated. The number of VMQs per adapter must be one more than the maximum number of VM NICs.

vmmq_adapter_policy.description: The default value is `default`.  Ethernet Adapter policy to be associated with the VMQ vNICs. The Transmit Queue and Receive Queue resource value of VMMQ adapter policy should be greater than or equal to the configured number of sub vNICs.  If this policy is originating from a shared organization, include the organization name with the policy via `org_name/policy_name`.
vmmq_adapter_policy.title: vmmq_adapter_policy

iscsi_boot_policy.description: The default value is `default`.  The Name of the iSCSI Boot Policy to Assign to the vNIC.  Only define if the `Allow Override` flag is checked with the template.

automatic_pci_link_assignment.description: The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true` if `pci_link` is undefined, otherwise this is ignored.  Enable or disable automatic assignment of the PCI Link in a dual-link adapter. This option applies only to 13xx series VICs that support dual-link. If enabled, the system determines the placement of the vNIC/vHBA on either of the PCI Links.
automatic_pci_link_assignment.title: automatic_pci_link_assignment

automatic_slot_id_assignment.description: The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true` if `slot_id` is undefined, otherwise this is ignored.  Enable or disable automatic assignment of the VIC slot ID. If enabled and the server has only one VIC, the same VIC is chosen for all the vNICs. If enabled and the server has multiple VICs, the vNIC/vHBA are deployed on the first VIC. The Slot ID determines the first VIC. MLOM is the first Slot ID and the ID increments to 2, 3, and so on.
automatic_slot_id_assignment.title: automatic_slot_id_assignment

pci_link.description: The default value is `0`.  Adapter port on which the virtual interface will be created.  Range is `0-3`.
pci_link.title: pci_link

pci_order.description: The default value is `0`.  PCI Order Assigned to the Interface. The order should start from zero with no overlaps, including LAN and SAN Policies.  Range is `0-256`.
pci_order.title: pci_order

slot_id.description: The default value is ``.  PCIe Slot where the VIC adapter is installed. Supported values are (1-15) and MLOM.
slot_id.title: slot_id

switch_id.description:
The default value is `None`.  The fabric port to which the vNICs will be associated.

Note: This can only be configured if the Allow Override is configured at the Template Level

  * `A` - Fabric A of the FI cluster.
  * `B` - Fabric B of the FI cluster.
  * `None` - Fabric Id is `None` for a standalone server use-case where the server is not connected to Fabric Interconnects.
switch_id.title: switch_id

uplink_port.description: The default value is `0`.  Adapter port on which the virtual interface will be created.  Range is `0-3`.
uplink_port.title: pci_link

intersight.ldap.base_settings.description:
Base settings of LDAP required while configuring LDAP policy.
  * `Required Attributes:`
  * `domain

Optional Attributes:
  * `base_dn`
  * `timeout`

base_dn.description: The default value is ``.  Distinguished Name (DN) of the user, that is used to authenticate against LDAP servers.  An example would be `DC=example,DC=com`
base_dn.title: base_dn

domain.description: The default value is `example.com`.  The LDAP domain that all users must be in.  An example would be `example.com`.

intersight.ldap.binding_parameters.description:
Base settings of LDAP required while configuring LDAP policy.

Required Attributes:
  * `bind_method`

Optional Attributes:
  * `bind_dn`

bind_dn.description: The default value is ``.  Base Distinguished Name (DN). Starting point from where server will search for users and groups.  An example is `CN=admin,DC=example,DC=com`
bind_dn.title: bind_dn

bind_method.description:
The default value is `LoginCredentials`.  Authentication method to access LDAP servers.
  * `LoginCredentials` - Requires the user credentials. If the bind process fails, then user is denied access.
  * `Anonymous` - Requires no username and password. If this option is selected and the LDAP server is configured for Anonymous logins, then the user gains access.
  * `ConfiguredCredentials` - Requires a known set of credentials to be specified for the initial bind process. If the initial bind process succeeds, then the distinguished name (DN) of the user name is queried and re-used for the re-binding process. If the re-binding process fails, then the user is denied access.'
bind_method.title: bind_method

password.description: The default value is `1`.  The LDAP Binding Parameter Password Sensitive Variable Identifier.  A number Between 1-64 to denote the variables binding_parameters_password_[1-64].  i.e. `export binding_parameters_password_1='cisco123'`.
password.title: password

intersight.ldap.binding_parameters.title: binding_parameters

DNS settings used to access LDAP Providers.

  * `search_domain`

Optional Attributes:
  * `search_forest`
  * `source`

search_domain.description: The default value is `example.com`.  Domain name that acts as a source for a DNS query.  An example would be `example.com`.
search_domain.title: search_domain

search_forest.description: The default value is `example.com`.  Forest name that acts as a source for a DNS query.  An example would be `example.com`.
search_forest.title: search_forest

source.description:
The default value is `Extracted`.  Source of the domain name used for the DNS SRV request.
  * `Extracted` - The domain name extracted-domain from the login ID.
  * `Configured` - The configured-search domain.
  * `ConfiguredExtracted` - The domain name extracted from the login ID than the configured-search domain.

domain.description: The default value is `example.com`.  LDAP server domain the Group resides in.  If this is not defined the base domain from `base_settings` will be used.

end_point_role.description: The default value is `readonly`.  The name of the end point role.  The role determines the level of access the users in the LDAP group will have to CIMC/IPMI.  The `admin` role will have full access to all features and settings in Intersight.  The `readonly` role will have read-only access to all features and settings in Intersight.  The `user` role will have access to all features and settings in Intersight except for user management and policy management.
end_point_role.title: end_point_role

group_dn.description: The default value is ``.  LDAP Group DN in the LDAP server database..  An example would be `CN=admin,OU=Users,DC=example,DC=com`

The default value is `OpenLDAP`.  LDAP server vendor type used for authentication.
  * `OpenLDAP` - Open source LDAP server for remote authentication.
  * `MSAD` - Microsoft active directory for remote authentication.
vendor.title: vendor

Base settings of LDAP required while configuring LDAP policy.Required Attributes:`

The default value is `normal`.  Flag used to indicate whether LACP PDUs are to be sent 'fast', i.e., every 1 second.
  * `normal` - The expanded 4th generation UCS Fabric Interconnect with 108 ports.
  * `fast` - The standard 4th generation UCS Fabric Interconnect with 54 ports.

suspend_individual.description: The default value is `False`.  The default value is `false`.  Flag tells the switch whether to suspend the port if it didn’t receive LACP PDU.

intersight.local_user.password_propertie.description:
Set password properties for endpoint users.

Optional Attributes:
  * `always_send_user_password`
  * `enable_password_expiry`
  * `enforce_strong_password`
  * `grace_period`
  * `notification_period`
  * `password_expiry_duration`
  * `password_history`

always_send_user_password.description: The default value is `False`.  The default value is `false`.  User password will always be sent to endpoint device. If the option is not selected, then user password will be sent to endpoint device for new users and if user password is changed for existing users.
always_send_user_password.title: always_send_user_password

enable_password_expiry.description: The default value is `False`.  The default value is `false`.  Enables password expiry on the endpoint.
enable_password_expiry.title: enable_password_expiry

enforce_strong_password.description:
The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true`.  Enables a strong password policy. Strong password requirements:`
  * A. The password must have a minimum of 8 and a maximum of 20 characters.
  * B. The password must not contain the User's Name.
  * C. The password must contain characters from three of the following four categories.
    - 1) English uppercase characters (A through Z). 2) English lowercase characters (a through z).
    - 3) Base 10 digits (0 through 9).
    - 4) Non-alphabetic characters (! , @, #, $, %, ^, &, *, -, _, +, =).
enforce_strong_password.title: enforce_strong_password

grace_period.description: The default value is `1`.  Time period until when you can use the existing password, after it expires.
grace_period.title: grace_period

notification_period.description: The default value is `15`.  The duration after which the password will expire.
notification_period.title: notification_period

password_expiry_duration.description: The default value is `90`.  Set time period for password expiration. Value should be greater than notification period and grace period.
password_expiry_duration.title: password_expiry_duration

password_history.description: The default value is `0`.  The default value is `5`.  Tracks password change history. Specifies in number of instances, that the new password was already used.
password_history.title: password_history

intersight.local_user.password_propertie.title:
password_properties - Local User Policy Password Settings.

enabled.description: The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true`.  Enables the user account on the endpoint.
enabled.title: enabled

password.description: The default value is `1`.  The Local User Authentication Password Sensitive Variable Identifier.  A number Between 1-64 to denote the variables local_user_password_[1-64].  i.e. `export local_user_password_1='cisco123'`.
password.title: password

role.description:
The default value is `readonly`.  The name of the end point role.
  * `admin`
  * `readonly`
  * `user`
role.title: role

username.description: The default value is `admin`.  Name of the user to be created on the endpoint. It can be any string that adheres to the following constraints. It can have alphanumeric characters, dots, underscores and hyphen. It cannot be more than 16 characters.
username.title: username

A block of MAC addresses.

Optional Attributes:

Note: Only assign either size or to, not both.

intersight.mac.description: Pool represents a collection of MAC addresses that can be allocated to VNICs of a server profile.
intersight.mac.title: mac - MAC Pools - Intersight Uri: macpool/Pools

from.description: The default value is `00:25:B5:0A:00:00`.  Staring MAC Address.  An Example is `00:25:B5:0A:00:00`
from.title: from

size.description: The default value is `255`.  Size of the Pool.  Allowed range is `1-1024`.
size.title: size

to.description: The default value is `00:25:B5:0A:04:00`.  Ending MAC Address.  An Example is `00:25:B5:0A:04:00`
to.title: to

allOf.description: A placeholder for MACSec (Media Access Control Security) configuration parameters, Primary/Fallback key chain and EAPol (Extensible Authentication Protocol over LAN) configurations.

chipher_suite.description:
The default value is `GCM-AES-XPN-256`.  Cipher suite to be used for MacSec encryption.
  * `GCM-AES-128` - This Cipher Suite employs the Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) with a 128-bit key in Galois/Counter Mode, offering both encryption and authentication.
  * `GCM-AES-256` - This Cipher Suite utilizes Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) with a 256-bit key in Galois/Counter Mode, offering a higher level of security compared to GCM-AES-128 due to the larger key size.
  * `GCM-AES-XPN-128` - An extended Cipher Suite of GCM-AES-128  used in MacSec (Media Access Control Security) that incorporates Extended Packet Numbering (XPN) to enhance security and scalability.
  * `GCM-AES-XPN-256` - An extended Cipher Suite of GCM-AES-256 used in MacSec (Media Access Control Security) that incorporates Extended Packet Numbering (XPN) for enhanced security and scalability.
chipher_suite.title: chipher_suite

confidentiality_offset.description:
The default value is `CONF-OFFSET-0`.  The MacSec confidentiality offset specifies the number of bytes starting from the frame header. MacSec encrypts only the bytes after the offset in a frame.
  * `CONF-OFFSET-0` - A value of 0 means the entire ethernet frame is encrypted.
  * `CONF-OFFSET-30` - The first 30 bytes of the ethernet frame are not encrypted, and the rest of the frame is encrypted.
  * `CONF-OFFSET-50` - The first 50 bytes of the ethernet frame are not encrypted, and the rest of the frame is encrypted.
confidentiality_offset.title: chipher_suite

include_icv_indicator.description: The default value is `False`.  The default value is `false`.  Configures inclusion of the optional integrity check value (ICV) indicator as part of the transmitted MacSec key agreement protocol data unit (PDU).
include_icv_indicator.title: include_icv_indicator

key_server_priority.description: The default value is `16`.  The key server is selected by comparing key-server priority values during MacSec key agreement (MKA) message exchange between peer devices. Valid values range from 0 to 255. The lower the value, the higher the chance it will be selected as the key server.
key_server_priority.title: key_server_priority

replay_window_size.description: The default value is `148809600`.  Defines the size of the replay protection window. It determines the number of packets that can be received out of order without being considered replay attacks.
replay_window_size.title: replay_window_size

sak_expiry_time.description: Default value is undefined.  The key server is selected by comparing key-server priority values during MacSec key agreement (MKA) message exchange between peer devices. Valid values range from 0 to 255. The lower the value, the higher the chance it will be selected as the key server.
sak_expiry_time.title: sak_expiry_time

security_policy.description:
The default value is `Should-secure`.  The security policy specifies the level of MacSec enforcement on network traffic passing through a given interface.
Should secure allows unencrypted traffic to flow until the MacSec key agreement (MKA) session is secured. After the MKA session is secured, the policy switches to only allow encrypted traffic to flow. Must secure imposes only MacSec encrypted traffic to flow. Traffic will be dropped, until the MKA session is not secured.
  * `Should-secure` - Should secure allows unencrypted traffic to flow until the MacSec key agreement (MKA) session is secured. After the MKA session is secured, the policy switches to only allow encrypted traffic to flow.
  * `Must-secure` - Must secure imposes only MacSec encrypted traffic to flow. Traffic will be dropped, until the MKA session is not secured.
security_policy.title: security_policy

allOf.title: mac_sec - MacSec - Intersight Uri: fabric/MacSecPoliciess

intersight.mac_sec.description: A placeholder for MACSec (Media Access Control Security) configuration parameters, Primary/Fallback key chain and EAPol (Extensible Authentication Protocol over LAN) configurations.
intersight.mac_sec.title: mac_sec - MacSec - Intersight Uri: fabric/MacSecPolicies

intersight.mac_sec.eapol_configurations.description:
Extensible authentication protocol over LAN (EAPoL). MacSec transmits MacSec key agreement (MKA) protocol data units (PDUs) using EAPoL packets to establish a secure session.

Required Attributes:
  * `ether_type`
  * `mac_address`

ether_type.description: The default value is `0x888e`.  Ethertype to use in extensible authentication protocol over LAN (EAPoL) frames for MacSec key agreement (MKA) protocol data units (PDUs). The range is between 0x600 - 0xffff.
ether_type.title: ether_type

mac_address.description: The default value is `0180.C200.0003`.  MAC address to use in extensible authentication protocol over LAN (EAPoL) for MacSec key agreement (MKA) protocol data units (PDUs). EAPol mac address should not be equal to all-zero (0000.0000.0000).
mac_address.title: mac_address

intersight.mac_sec.eapol_configurations.title: EAPoL Configurations

intersight.mac_sec.mac_sec_fallback_key_.description:
Fallback key chain for managing an alternative set of security keys to be used when a secure session cannot be established using the primary key chain.

Required Attributes:
  * `fallback_key_chain_name`

Optional Attributes:
  * `add_key`

add_key.description:
A collection of security keys used for encrypting and decrypting data frames to ensure secure communication between devices.

Required Attributes:
  * `cryptographic_algorithm`
  * `id`
  * `secret`

Optional Attributes:
  * `key_lifetime`
add_key.title: add_key - Add Key - Intersight Uri: fabric.SecKey

fallback_key_chain_name.description: The default value is `default`.  Name of the Policy.
fallback_key_chain_name.title: fallback_key_chain_name

intersight.mac_sec.mac_sec_fallback_key_.title: mac_sec_fallback_key_chain - Fallback Keychain

cryptographic_algorithm.description:
The default value is `AES_256_CMAC`.  The cryptographic algorithm that employs the cipher-based message authentication code (CMAC) mode of operation with advanced encryption standard (AES).
  * `AES_256_CMAC` - Uses the AES (Advanced Encryption Standard) algorithm with a 256-bit key to generate a CMAC.
  * `AES_128_CMAC` - Uses the AES (Advanced Encryption Standard) algorithm with a 128-bit key to generate a CMAC.
cryptographic_algorithm.title: cryptographic_algorithm

id.description: The default value is `ABCD1234`.  Must have an even number of hexadecimal characters (including 0-9 and A-F, only) with a length between 2 and 64 characters. For example, "10", "2000", "ABCD1234".
id.title: id

secret.description: The default value is `1`.  The MacSec Key Secret Sensitive Variable Identifier.  A number Between 1-64 to denote the variables mac_sec_fallback_key_chain_secret_[1-64].  i.e. `export mac_sec_fallback_key_chain_secret_1='cisco123'`.
secret.title: secret

intersight.mac_sec.mac_sec_fallback_key_.description:
Key Lifetime attributes for the fallback key.

Required Attributes:
  * `always_active`

Optional Attributes:
  * `end_time`
  * `start_time`
  * `timezone`

Note:  If `always_active` is true the rest of the attributes are ignored.  If `always_active` is false, start_time is required.  If end_time is not defined when always_active is true the lifetime is set to infinite.

  * `dscp`
  * `gateway_ip_address`

destination_ip_address.description: The default value is `198.18.1.5`.  Netflow collector IP address, The Netflow collector receives flow records from one or more exporters.  It processes the received export packets and stores the flow record information.
destination_ip_address.title: destination_ip_address

dscp.description: The default value is `0`.  Differentiated Services Code Point (DSCP) value for export packets to ensure they receive proper QoS treatment. By default, NetFlow export packets may use the default DSCP value (usually 0, equivalent to Best Effort).
dscp.title: dscp

udp_port.description: The default value is `9996`.  NetFlow export packets are encapsulated within UDP datagrams for transmission to the NetFlow collector.

intersight.netflow_configuration.flow_ex.description:
Netflow Configuration > Flow Exporters > Flow Collector.

Optional Attributes:
  * `option_exporter_stats_timeout`
  * `option_interface_table_timeout`
  * `template_data_timeout`

option_exporter_stats_timeout.description: The default value is `600`.  The time interval, in seconds, during which a NetFlow collector maintains an option template after it has been received from an exporter. An Option Template Record is a special type of template in NetFlow used to export metadata or control information, rather than flow data such as sampling parameters or exporter statistics.
option_exporter_stats_timeout.title: option_exporter_stats_timeout

option_interface_table_timeout.description: The default value is `600`.  The time interval, in seconds, during which a NetFlow collector maintains an interface option template after it has been received from an exporter. The optionInterfaceTable refers to an option data record exported by NetFlow exporters that provides metadata about network interfaces such as interface names, types, and speeds.
option_interface_table_timeout.title: option_interface_table_timeout

template_data_timeout.description: The default value is `600`.  The time interval, in seconds, during which a NetFlow collector maintains a template after it has been received from an exporter. templateData refers to the actual flow record data that is exported from a exporter to a collector, using a previously defined template. The template specifies the structure and the templateData provides the corresponding values for those fields for each flow record.
template_data_timeout.title: template_data_timeout

allOf.description: The Persistent Memory policy defines the reusable Persistent Memory related configuration that can be applied on many servers. The encryption of the Persistent Memory Modules can be enabled through this policy by providing a passphrase.

  * `configured-from-intersight` - The Persistent Memory Modules are configured from Intersight thorugh Persistent Memory policy.
  * `configured-from-operating-system` - The Persistent Memory Modules are configured from operating system thorugh OS tools.

Optional Attributes are:

  * `socket_memory_id`

The encryption of the Persistent Memory Modules can be enabled through this policy by providing a passphrase.

memory_mode_percentage.description: The default value is `1`.  Volatile memory percentage.

persistent_memory_type.description:

  * `app-direct` - The App Direct interleaved Persistent Memory type.
  * `app-direct-non-interleaved` - The App Direct non-interleaved Persistent Memory type.

intersight.persistent_memory.namespaces.description: Persistent Memory Namespace specification that needs to be applied to the associated servers through this policy. This would result in the creation, modification, or deletion of a Namespace on the servers.

capacity.description: The default value is `1`.  Capacity of this Namespace that is created or modified.
capacity.title: capacity

mode.description:
The default value is `raw`.  Mode of this Namespace that is created or modified.
  * `raw` - The raw mode of Persistent Memory Namespace.
  * `block` - The block mode of Persistent Memory Namespace.
mode.title: mode

name.description: The default value is `default`.  Name of this Namespace to be created on the server.
name.title: name

socket_id.description:
The default value is `1`.  Socket ID of the region on which this Namespace has to be created or modified.
  * `1` - The first CPU socket in a server.
  * `2` - The second CPU socket in a server.
  * `3` - The third CPU socket in a server.
  * `4` - The fourth CPU socket in a server.
socket_id.title: socket_id

socket_memory_id.description:
The default value is `2`.  Socket Memory ID of the region on which this Namespace has to be created or modified.
  * `Not Applicable` - The socket memory ID is not applicable if app-direct persistent memory type is selected in the goal.
  * `2` - The second socket memory ID within a socket in a server.
  * `4` - The fourth socket memory ID within a socket in a server.
  * `6` - The sixth socket memory ID within a socket in a server.
  * `8` - The eighth socket memory ID within a socket in a server.
  * `10` - The tenth socket memory ID within a socket in a server.
  * `12` - The twelfth socket memory ID within a socket in a server.
socket_memory_id.title: socket_memory_id

intersight.policies.description:
Policies in Cisco Intersight provide different configurations for UCS servers, including BIOS settings, firmware versions, disk group creation, Simple Mail Transfer Protocol (SMTP), Intelligent Platform Management Interface (IPMI) settings, and more. A policy that is once configured can be assigned to any number of servers to provide a configuration baseline. Policies in Cisco Intersight are native to the application and are not directly imported from the UCS Systems. Policy-based configuration with Server Profiles is a Cisco Intersight Essentials functionality.

Options are:

  * `adapter_configuration`
  * `auditd`
  * `bios`
  * `boot_order`
  * `certificate_management`
  * `drive_security`
  * `device_connector`
  * `ethernet_adapter`
  * `ethernet_network`
  * `ethernet_network_control`

  * `iscsi_adapter`

bios.description:
A BIOS policy automates the configuration of BIOS settings on servers. You can create one or more BIOS policies that contain a specific grouping of BIOS settings, matching the needs of a server or a set of servers. If you do not specify a BIOS policy for a server, the BIOS settings will default to set of values for a brand new baremetal server or to a set of values previously configured using Cisco IMC. If a BIOS policy is specified, its values replace any previously configured values on the server.

All BIOS tokens are not applicable to all servers. If unsupported tokens are pushed to a server, those tokens are ignored.

Required Attributes:
  * `name`

Recommended Attribute:

  * `bios_template`

Optional Attributes:
  * `description`
  * `tags`

bios.title: bios - Bios - Intersight Uri: bios/Policies

boot_order.description:
The Boot Order policy configures the linear ordering of devices and enables you to change the boot order and boot mode. You can also add multiple devices under various device types, rearrange the boot order, and set parameters for each boot device type.

Required Attributes:
  * `boot_devices`
  * `enable_secure_boot`
  * `name`

Optional Attributes:
  * `boot_mode`
  * `description`
  * `tags`
boot_order.title: boot_order - Boot Order - Intersight Uri: boot/PrecisionPolicies

certificate_management.description:
Allows you to specify the certificate and private key-pair details for an external certificate.

Note: Currently, Cisco Intersight supports Certificate Management of IMC Certificates only.

Required Attributes:
  * `assigned_sensitive_data`
  * `certificate`
  * `name`
  * `private_key`

Optional Attributes:
  * `enabled`
  * `description`
  * `tags`
certificate_management.title: certificate_management - Certificate Management - Intersight Uri: certificatemanagement/Policies

device_connector.description:
Device Connector Policy lets you choose the Configuration from Intersight only option to control configuration changes allowed from Cisco IMC. The Configuration from Intersight only option is enabled by default. You will observe the following changes when you deploy the Device Connector policy in Intersight:`

  * Validation tasks will fail:
    - If Intersight Read-only mode is enabled in the claimed device.
    - If the firmware version of the Cisco UCS Standalone C-Series Servers is lower than 4.0(1).
  * If Intersight Read-only mode is enabled, firmware upgrades will be successful only when performed from Intersight. Firmware upgrade performed locally from Cisco IMC will fail.
  * IPMI over LAN privileges will be reset to read-only level if Configuration from Intersight only is enabled through the Device Connector policy, or if the same configuration is enabled in the Device Connector in Cisco IMC.

Required Attributes:
  * `name`

Optional Attributes:
  * `configuration_from_intersight_only`
  * `description`
  * `tags`
device_connector.title: device_connector - Device Connector - Intersight Uri: deviceconnector/Policies

drive_security.description:
In Intersight Managed Mode, the Drive Security Policy allows you to specify the KMIP server details and attach the policy to the server profile.  Either `manual_key` or `remote_key_management` must be specified, but not both.

Required Attributes:
  * `name`

Optional Attributes:
  * `description`
  * `manual_key`
  * `remote_key_management`
  * `tags`
drive_security.title: drive_security - Drive Security - Intersight Uri: storage/DriveSecurityPolicies

ethernet_adapter.description:
An Ethernet adapter policy governs the host-side behavior of the adapter, including how the adapter handles traffic. For each VIC Virtual Ethernet Interface, you can configure various features like Virtual Extensible LAN (VXLAN), Network Virtualization using Generic Routing Encapsulation (NVGRE), Accelerated Receive Flow Steering (ARFS), Interrupt settings, and TCP Offload settings.

The Ethernet Adapter policy include the recommended settings for the virtual Ethernet interface, for each supported server operating system. Operating systems are sensitive to the settings in these policies. In general, the storage vendors require non-default adapter settings. You can find the details of these required settings on the support list provided by those vendors.

IMPORTANT NOTE: If GENEVE Offload is Enabled be sure to Read the Following Guidance: https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/unified_computing/Intersight/b_Intersight_Managed_Mode_Configuration_Guide/b_intersight_managed_mode_guide_chapter_0110.html#reference_ofm_khm_5jb

Required Attributes:
  * `name`

Recommended Attribute:

 `adapter_template`

Optional Attributes:
  * `completion`
  * `description`
  * `enable_accelerated_receive_flow_steering`
  * `enable_advanced_filter`
  * `enable_ether_channel_pinning`
  * `enable_geneve_offload`
  * `enable_interrupt_scaling`
  * `enable_nvgre_offload`
  * `enable_precision_time_protocol`
  * `enable_vxlan_offload`
  * `interrupt_settings`
  * `receive`
  * `receive_side_scaling`
  * `roce_settings`
  * `tags`
  * `tcp_offload`
  * `transmit`
  * `uplink_failback_timeout`
ethernet_adapter.title: ethernet_adapter - Ethernet Adapter - Intersight Uri: vnic/EthAdapterPolicies

ethernet_network.description:
An Ethernet Network policy determines if the port can carry single VLAN (Access) or multiple VLANs (Trunk) traffic. You can specify the VLAN to be associated with an Ethernet packet if no tag is found.

Required Attributes:
  * `name`

Optional Attributes:
  * `default_vlan`
  * `description`
  * `tags`
  * `vlan_mode`
ethernet_network.title: ethernet_network - Ethernet Network - Intersight Uri: vnic/EthNetworkPolicies

ethernet_network_control.description:
Ethernet Network Control policies configure the network control settings for the UCS Domain. This policy is applicable only for the Appliance Ports defined in a Port Policy and for the vNICs defined in a LAN Connectivity Policy, on an FI-Attached UCS Servers.

Required Attributes:
  * `name`

Optional Attributes:
  * `action_on_uplink_fail`
  * `cdp_enable`
  * `description`
  * `lldp_enable_receive`
  * `lldp_enable_transmit`
  * `mac_register_mode`
  * `mac_security_forge`
  * `tags`
ethernet_network_control.title: ethernet_network_control - Ethernet Network Control - Intersight Uri: fabric/EthNetworkControlPolicies

ethernet_network_group.description:
Ethernet Network Group policies configure the Allowed VLANs and Native VLAN settings for the UCS Domain.

Required Attributes:
  * `allowed_vlans`
  * `name`

Optional Attributes:
  * `description`
  * `native_vlan`
  * `qinq_vlan`
ethernet_network_group.title: ethernet_network_group - Ethernet Network Group - Intersight Uri: fabric/EthNetworkGroupPolicies

ethernet_qos.description:
An Ethernet Quality of Service (QoS) policy assigns a system class to the outgoing traffic for a vNIC. This system class determines the quality of service for the outgoing traffic. For certain adapters, you can also specify additional controls like burst and rate on the outgoing traffic.

Required Attributes:
  * `name`

Optional Attributes:
  * `burst`

fc_zone.description:
This policy allows you to set up access control between hosts and storage devices.

  * Certain points to be noted when creating the FC Zone policy:`
  * Deploying a storage VSAN using a domain profile, for the first time, clears all the unmanaged zones from the Fabric Interconnect.
  * SAN boot targets with a storage VSAN have a zone entry in the Fabric Interconnect.
  * A one-time SAN boot with a storage VSAN has a zone entry in the Fabric Interconnect.
  * Editing the FC Zone policy causes the server profile status to be changed to Pending Changes.
  * When the Fabric Interconnect is rebooted, there is a replay of zones in the configuration.
  * Detection of configuration drift is not supported for FC Zone policy.

Required Attributes:
  * `name`
  * `targets`

Optional Attributes:
  * `description`
  * `fc_target_zoning_type`
  * `tags`

A Fibre Channel adapter policy governs the host-side behavior of the adapter, including how the adapter handles traffic. You can enable FCP Error Recovery, change the default settings of Queues, and Interrupt handling for performance enhancement.
Note: We recommend that you use the values in these policies for the applicable operating system. Do not modify any of the values in the default policies unless directed to do so by Cisco Technical Support.

This policy allows you to configure a VLAN and associate it with an IP address through the IP pool address.  In-Band IP address, Out-of-Band IP address, or both In-Band and Out-of-Band IP addresses can be configured using IMC Access Policy and are supported on the following:`

ipmi_over_lan.description:
Defines the protocols for interfacing with a service processor that is embedded in a server platform. The Intelligent Platform Management Interface (IPMI) enables an operating system to obtain information about the system health and control system hardware and directs the Cisco IMC to perform the required actions. You can create an IPMI Over LAN policy to manage the IPMI messages through Cisco Intersight. You can assign these user roles to an IPMI user per session:`
  * `admin` — IPMI users can perform all available actions. If you select this option, IPMI users with the `Administrator` user role can create admin, user, and read-only sessions on this server.
  * `read-only` — Can view information but cannot make any changes. IPMI users with the `Administrator`, `Operator`, or `User` user roles can only create read-only IPMI sessions, regardless of their other IPMI privileges.
  * `user` — IPMI users can perform some functions but cannot perform administrative tasks. If you select this option, IPMI users with the `Administrator` or `Operator` user role can create user and read-only sessions on this server.

Required Attributes:
  * `name`

Optional Attributes:
  * `description`
  * `enabled`
  * `privilege`
  * `tags`
ipmi_over_lan.title: ipmi_over_lan - Ipmi Over Lan - Intersight Uri: ipmioverlan/Policies

iscsi_boot.description:
iSCSI boot support allows you to initialize the Operating System on FI-attached blade and rack servers from a remote disk across a Storage Area Network. The remote disk, known as the target, is accessed using TCP/IP and iSCSI boot firmware.
Prerequisites - The following are required to configure the iSCSI boot device:

  * iSCSI Static Target Policy—When you select Static as the mode for configuring the iSCSI boot policy, you can use the iSCSI Static Target policy to specify the primary target details. You can also specify the details of a secondary target, if required.
  * iSCSI Adapter Policy—Using this policy you can specify the TCP and DHCP Connection Timeout and the retry count when the logical unit number of the boot device is busy.
  * Creating an IQN Pool—Using this policy you can specify the TCP and DHCP Connection Timeout and the retry count when the logical unit number of the boot device is busy.

Required Attributes:
  * `name`

Optional Attributes:
  * `authentication`
  * `description`

  * `initiator_ip_source`
  * `initiator_static_ipv4_config`
  * `tags`

iscsi_boot.title: iscsi_boot - Iscsi Boot - Intersight Uri: vnic/IscsiBootPolicies

persistent_memory.description:
Persistent Memory Modules (PMem Modules) are non-volatile memory modules that bring together the low latency of memory and the persistence of storage. PMem Modules provide faster access to data and retain across power cycles, based on the mode. Intersight supports the configuration of Intel® Optane™ PMem Module modules on the UCS M5 servers that are based on the Second Generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processors. Intel® Optane™ PMem Modules can be used only with the Second-Generation Intel® Xeon® Scalable processors. The Persistent Memory Policy allows the configuration of security, Goals, and Namespaces of Persistent Memory Modules.

server_pool_qualification.description:
A server will be included in the resource pool if it matches the conditions of all the qualifiers present in the policy. The qualifiers are of four types:`
  * `Domain` — All FI-attached servers in any domain that match the provided Fabric Interconnect PID and domain name qualify under this type.
  * `Server` — All blade and/or rack servers that match the provided server IDs, PIDs, tags, user labels, and chassis IDs qualify under this type.
  * `Tag` — All servers that match any of the provided server tags, domain profile, and chassis tags qualify under this type. Domain profile and chassis tags are applicable only for FI-attached servers.
  * `Hardware` — All servers with hardware components that match the provided CPU, memory, GPU, and Network adapters qualify under this type.

This policy is applicable for Intersight Managed Mode and Standalone servers.

Required Attributes:
  * `name`

Optional Attributes:
  * `description`
  * `domain_qualifiers`
  * `hardware_qualifiers`
  * `server_qualifiers`
  * `tag_qualifiers`

vhba_template.description:
A vNIC or vHBA template consists of common configurations that you can reuse across multiple vNICs or vHBAs, used in various Server Profiles. This approach simplifies network configuration across multiple servers. You can create vNICs or vHBAs from the template using the Derive operation while creating the policy. Additionally, you can attach an existing vNIC or vHBA to a template to utilize the configurations set in the template. These templates can be created with or without override options. The override option allows the configuration of the derived vNIC or vHBA to override the template configuration.

Required Attributes:
  * `fibre_channel_qos_policy`
  * `name`

Optional Attributes:
  * `allow_override`
  * `description`
  * `fc_zone_policies`
  * `fibre_channel_adapter_policy` - required if allow_override is false`
  * `fibre_channel_network_policy` - required if allow_override is false`
  * `persistent_lun_bindings`
  * `placement_switch_id`
  * `tags`
  * `vhba_type`
  * `wwpn_pool` - required if allow_override is false
vhba_template.title: vhba_template - vHBA Template - Intersight Uri: vnic/VhbaTemplate

virtual_kvm.title: virtual_kvm - Virtual Kvm - Intersight Uri: kvm/Policies

virtual_media.title: virtual_media - Virtual Media - Intersight Uri: vmedia/Policies

vlan.description:
VLAN policies create a connection to a specific external LAN. The VLAN isolates traffic to that external LAN, including broadcast traffic. You can create VLANs and Private VLANs using the VLAN policy.
Note: Ensure that each VLAN is associated with a multicast policy. You can edit the existing VLANs and associate them to a multicast policy. You cannot associate a Multicast policy to a Private VLAN.

Required Attributes:
  * `name`
  * `target_platform`

Optional Attributes:
  * `description`
  * `tags`
  * `vlans`
vlan.title: vlan - Vlan - Intersight Uri: fabric/EthNetworkPolicies

vnic_template.description:
A vNIC or vHBA template consists of common configurations that you can reuse across multiple vNICs or vHBAs, used in various Server Profiles. This approach simplifies network configuration across multiple servers. You can create vNICs or vHBAs from the template using the Derive operation while creating the policy. Additionally, you can attach an existing vNIC or vHBA to a template to utilize the configurations set in the template. These templates can be created with or without override options. The override option allows the configuration of the derived vNIC or vHBA to override the template configuration.

Required Attributes:
  * `ethernet_network_control_policy`
  * `ethernet_network_group_policy`
  * `ethernet_qos_policy`
  * `name`

Optional Attributes:
  * `allow_override`
  * `cdn_source`
  * `cdn_value`
  * `description`
  * `enable_failover`
  * `ethernet_adapter_policy` - required if allow_override is false`
  * `flow_monitor_policies`
  * `iscsi_boot_policy`
  * `mac_address_pool` - required if allow_override is false`
  * `placement_switch_id`
  * `sriov`
  * `tags`
  * `usnic`
  * `vmq`
vnic_template.title: vnic_template - vNIC Template - Intersight Uri: vnic/VnicTemplate

vsan.description:
With the Virtual SAN Identifier (VSAN) policy, you can create Virtual SANs (VSANs) to isolate devices physically connected to the same SAN fabric. VSANs improve security and stability in Fibre Channel fabrics and let you create several logical SANs over a common physical infrastructure.

Required Attributes:
  * `name`

Optional Attributes:
  * `description`
  * `tags`
  * `uplink_trunking`
  * `vsans
vsan.title: vsan - Vsan - Intersight Uri: fabric.FcNetworkPolicies

intersight.pools.description:
Pools are the basic building blocks for uniquely identifying hardware resources. As the basis for the UCS management model, they allow Service Profiles to be associated with any blade server, while still providing the exact same ID and presentation to the upstream LAN or SAN. If a pool is actively associated with a profile, the pool cannot be deleted.

Options are:
  * `ip`
  * `iqn`
  * `name`_prefix`
  * `name`_suffix`
  * `resource`
  * `uuid`
  * `wwnn`
  * `wwpn`

ip.description:
An IP pool is a sequential range of IP addresses within a certain network. You can assign multiple pool blocks within a pool configuration.

Required Attributes:
  * `name`

Optional Attributes:
  * `assignment_order`
  * `description`
  * `ipv4_blocks`
  * `ipv4_configuration`
  * `ipv6_blocks`
  * `ipv6_configuration`
  * `tags`
Note: For each Address Family IPv4/IPv6, both ipv(4|6)_blocks and ipv(4|6)_configuration must be defined.
ip.title: ip - IP Pool - ippool.Pool

iqn.description:
An IQN pool is a collection of iSCSI Qualified Names (IQNs) for use as initiator identifiers by iSCSI vNICs. IQN pool members are of the form prefix: suffix: number, where you can specify the prefix, suffix, and a block (range) of numbers.

Required Attributes:
  * `iqn_blocks`
  * `name`

Optional Attributes:
  * `assignment_order`
  * `description`
  * `prefix`
  * `tags`
iqn.title: iqn - Iqn Pool - iqnpool.Pool

mac.description:
A MAC pool is a collection of network identities, or MAC addresses, that are unique in their Layer 2 environment and are available to be assigned to vNICs on a server. If you use MAC pools in server profiles, you do not have to manually configure the MAC addresses to be used by the server associated with the server profile.

Required Attributes:
  * `mac_blocks`
  * `name`

Optional Attributes:
  * `assignment_order`
  * `description`
  * `tags`
mac.title: mac - MAC Pool - macpool.Pool

resource.description:
A resource pool is an aggregation of homogeneous resources that are managed in Intersight. A resource pool enables you to manage the utilization of resources for certain Intersight features like Server Profile. Currently, Intersight supports only server type as a resource for the resource pool.

Required Attributes:
  * `name`
  * `serial_number_list`

Optional Attributes:
  * `assignment_order`
  * `description`
  * `pool_type`
  * `resource_type`
  * `tags`
resource.title: resource - Resource Pool - resourcepool.Pool

uuid.description:
A Universally Unique Identifier (UUID) pool is a collection of UUIDs that are assigned to servers. The prefix and suffix of the UUID are variable values. A UUID pool ensures that these variable values are unique for each server associated with a server profile that uses a particular pool to avoid conflicts.

Required Attributes:
  * `name`
  * `uuid_blocks`

Optional Attributes:
  * `assignment_order`
  * `description`
  * `prefix`
  * `tags`
uuid.title: uuid - UUID Pool - uuidpool.Pool

wwnn.description:
A WWNN pool is a WWN pool that contains only WW node names. If you include a pool of WWNNs in a server profile, the associated server is assigned a WWNN from that pool.

Required Attributes:
  * `id_blocks`
  * `name`

Optional Attributes:
  * `assignment_order`
  * `description`
  * `tags`
wwnn.title: wwnn - WWNN Pool - fcpool.Pool

wwpn.description:
A WWPN pool is a WWN pool that contains only WW port names. If you include a pool of WWPNs in a server profile, the port on each vHBA of the associated server is assigned a WWPN from that pool.

Required Attributes:
  * `id_blocks`
  * `name`

Optional Attributes:
  * `assignment_order`
  * `description`
  * `tags`
wwpn.title: wwpn - WWPN Pool - fcpool.Pool

intersight.pools.title: Intersight Pools

device_model.description:
The default value is `UCS-FI-6454`.  This field specifies the device model that this Port Policy is being configured for.
  * `UCS-FI-6454` - The standard 4th generation UCS Fabric Interconnect with 54 ports.
  * `UCS-FI-64108` - The expanded 4th generation UCS Fabric Interconnect with 108 ports.
  * `UCS-FI-6536` - The standard 5th generation UCS Fabric Interconnect with 36 ports.
  * `UCSX-S9108-100G` - Cisco UCS Fabric Interconnect 9108 100G with 8 ports.
  * `UCS-FI-6664` - The standard 6th generation UCS Fabric Interconnect with 64 ports.
  * `UCS-FI-6652` - The standard 6th generation UCS Fabric Interconnect with 52 ports.
  * `UCSXE-ECMC-G1` - Cisco UCS XE ECMC G1 with 2 ports.
device_model.title: device_model

The default value is `0`.  Breakout port Identifier of the Switch Interface.
When a port is not configured as a breakout port, the aggregatePortId is set to 0, and unused.
When a port is configured as a breakout port, the 'aggregatePortId' port number as labeled on the equipment,
e.g. the id of the port on the switch.
breakout_port_id.title: breakout_port_id

port_id.description:
The default value is `1`.  Port Identifier of the Switch/FEX/Chassis Interface.
When a port is not configured as a breakout port, the portId is the port number as labeled on the equipment,
e.g. the id of the port on the switch, FEX or chassis.
When a port is configured as a breakout port, the 'portId' represents the port id on the fanout side of the breakout cable.

allOf.description: Power Management policy models a configuration that can be applied to Chassis or Server to manage Power Related Features.

dynamic_power_rebalancing.description:
The default value is `Enabled`.  Sets the Dynamic Power Rebalancing mode of the Chassis. If enabled, this mode allows the chassis to dynamically reallocate the power between servers depending on their power usage. This option is only supported for Cisco UCS X series Chassis.
  * `Enabled` - Set the value to Enabled.
  * `disabled` - Set the value to Disabled.
dynamic_power_rebalancing.title: dynamic_power_rebalancing

extended_power_capacity.description:
The default value is `Enabled`.  Sets the Extended Power Capacity of the Chassis. If Enabled, this mode allows chassis available power to be increased by borrowing power from redundant power supplies.  This option is only supported for Cisco UCS X series Chassis.
  * `Enabled` - Set the value to Enabled.
  * `disabled` - Set the value to Disabled.
extended_power_capacity.title: extended_power_capacity

power_allocation.description: The default value is `0`.  Sets the Allocated Power Budget of the Chassis (in Watts). This field is only supported for Cisco UCS X series Chassis.
power_allocation.title: power_allocation

power_priority.description:
The default value is `Low`.  Sets the Power Priority of the Server. This priority is used to determine the initial power allocation for servers. This field is only supported for Cisco UCS X series servers.
  * `Low` - Set the Power Priority to Low.
  * `Medium` - Set the Power Priority to Medium.
  * `High` - Set the Power Priority to High.
power_priority.title: power_priority

power_profiling.description:
The default value is `Enabled`.  Sets the Power Profiling of the Server. If Enabled, this field allows the power manager to run power profiling  utility to determine the power needs of the server.  This field is only supported for Cisco UCS X series servers.
  * `Enabled` - Set the value to Enabled.
  * `disabled` - Set the value to Disabled.
power_profiling.title: power_profiling

power_redundancy.description:
The default value is `Grid`.  Sets the Power Redundancy Mode of the Chassis/Server.  Redundancy Mode determines the number of PSUs the chassis keeps as redundant.  N+2 mode is only supported for Cisco UCS X series Chassis.
  * `Grid` - Grid Mode requires two power sources. If one source fails, the surviving PSUs connected to the other source provides power to the chassis.
  * `NotRedundant` - Power Manager turns on the minimum number of PSUs required to support chassis power requirements. No Redundant PSUs are maintained.
  * `N+1` - Power Manager turns on the minimum number of PSUs required to support chassis power requirements plus one additional PSU for redundancy.
  * `N+2` - Power Manager turns on the minimum number of PSUs required to support chassis power requirements plus two additional PSU for redundancy. This Mode is only supported for UCS X series Chassis.
power_redundancy.title: power_redundancy

power_restore.description:
The default value is `LastState`.  Sets the Power Restore State of the Server. In the absence of Intersight connectivity, the chassis will use this policy  to recover the host power after a power loss event.  This field is only supported for Cisco UCS X series servers.
  * `AlwaysOff` - Set the Power Restore Mode to Off.
  * `AlwaysOn` - Set the Power Restore Mode to On.
  * `LastState` - Set the Power Restore Mode to LastState.
power_restore.title: power_restore

power_save_mode.description:
The default value is `Enabled`.  Sets the Power Save mode of the Chassis. If the requested power budget is less than available power capacity,  the additional PSUs not required to comply with redundancy policy are placed in Power Save mode. This option is only supported for Cisco UCS X series Chassis.
  * `Enabled` - Set the value to Enabled.
  * `Disabled` - Set the value to Disabled.
power_save_mode.title: power_save_mode

processor_package_power_limit.description:
The default value is `Default`.  Sets the Processor Package Power Limit (PPL) of a server. PPL refers to the amount of power that a CPU can draw from the power supply. The Processor Package Power Limit (PPL) feature is currently available exclusively on Cisco UCS C225/C245 M8 servers.
  * `Default` - Set the Package Power Limit to the platform defined default value.
  * `Maximum` - Set the Package Power Limit to the platform defined maximum value.
  * `Minimum` - Set the Package Power Limit to the platform defined minimum value.
processor_package_power_limit.title: processor_package_power_limit

target_platform.description:
The default value is ``.  The platform for which the server profile is applicable. It can either be a server that is operating in standalone mode or which is attached to a Fabric Interconnect managed by Intersight.
  * `Standalone` - Servers which are operating in standalone mode i.e. not connected to a Fabric Interconnected.
  * `FIAttached` - Servers which are connected to a Fabric Interconnect that is managed by Intersight.
target_platform.title: target_platform

intersight.profiles.description:
Profiles in Cisco Intersight assign the Policy Configurations to UCS chassis/domains/servers, Policy-based configuration with Chassis/Domain/Server Profiles is a Cisco Intersight Essentials functionality.
Options are:

  * `chassis`
  * `domain`
  * `name`_prefix`
  * `name`_suffix`
  * `server`

chassis.description:
A UCS Chassis profile enables to create and associate chassis policies to an Intersight Managed Mode (IMM) claimed chassis. When a chassis profile is associated to a chassis, Cisco Intersight automatically configures the chassis to match the configuration specified in the policies of the chassis profile. The chassis-related policies can be attached to the profile either at the time of creation or later.

Important:
  * The chassis profile feature is available in Cisco Intersight only if:
     - You have installed the Cisco Intersight Essentials License.
     - You are either an Account Administrator or Server Administrator.
  * Policies that are attached to a chassis profile can be created ahead of creating a profile or during the creation of the profile.
  * If chassis polices are changed after deployment, the chassis profile will be set in Pending Changes state and you must manually re-associate the changed policies to chassis.
  * Chassis policies will be applied to both the input/output modules (IFMs/IOMs) in a chassis. The chassis policies association workflow will fail if the policy cannot be applied to one of the IFMs/IOMs.

Required Base Attributes:
  * `action`
  * `attach_template`
  * `targets`

Optional Base Attributes:
  * `tags`
  * `ucs_chassis_profile_template`

#=======================================
# Chassis Configuration
#=======================================
  * `imc_access_policy`
  * `power_policy`
  * `snmp_policy`
  * `thermal_policy`
chassis.title: chassis - Chassis Profiles - Intersight Uri: chassis/Profiles

domain.description:
A UCS Domain Profile configures a Fabric Interconnect pair through reusable policies, allowing for configuration of the ports and port channels, and configures the VLANs and VSANs in the network. It defines the characteristics of, and configures ports on Fabric Interconnects. You can create a UCS Domain profile and associate it with a Fabric Interconnect Domain. The Domain-related policies can be attached to the profile either at the time of creation or later. One UCS Domain profile can be assigned to one Fabric Interconnect Domain.

Important:
  * You can use a single port policy per UCS Domain profile or a port-policy per FI.
  * Policies that are attached to a UCS Domain profile can be created ahead of creating a profile or during the creation of the profile.
  * Policies that are attached to a UCS Domain and the global policies of all UCS Domain Profiles associated with a specific UCS Domain are shared.

Required Base Attributes:
  * `action`
  * `name`
  * `serial_numbers`

Optional Base Attributes:
  * `attach_template`
  * `description`
  * `tags`
  * `user_label`
  * `ucs_domain_profile_template` - to assign to a template

#=======================================
# VLAN & VSAN Configuration
#=======================================
  * `vlan_policies` - required (If not attached to a domain template) - A list of one or two VLAN policies.  A list of one will assign the same policy to both fabrics.
  * `vsan_policies` - A list of one or two VSAN policies.  A list of one will assign the same policy to both fabrics.

#=======================================
# Ports Configuration
#=======================================
  * `port_policies` - required (If not attached to a domain template)

#=======================================
# UCS Domain Configuration - Management
#=======================================
  * `certificate_management_policy`
  * `ldap_policy`
  * `network_connectivity_policy`
  * `ntp_policy`
  * `snmp_policy`
  * `syslog_policy`

#=======================================
# UCS Domain Configuration - Network
#=======================================
  * `auditd_policy`
  * `netflow_configuration`
  * `switch_control_policy`
  * `system_qos_policy` - required (If not attached to a domain template)
domain.title: domain - Domain Cluster Profile - Intersight Uri: fabric/SwitchClusterProfiles

server.description:
In Cisco Intersight, a Server Profile enables resource management by streamlining policy alignment, and server configuration. You can create Server Profiles using the Server Profile wizard or you can import the configuration details of C-series servers in standalone mode and FI-attached servers in Intersight Managed Mode (IMM), directly from Cisco IMC. You can create Server Profiles using the Server Profile wizard to provision servers, create policies to ensure smooth deployment of servers, and eliminate failures that are caused by inconsistent configuration. The Server Profiles wizard groups the server policies into the following four categories to provide a quick Summary View of the policies that are attached to a profile:`
  * Compute Policies — BIOS, Boot Order, Firmware, Power, Thermal, UUID Pool, and Virtual Media.
  * Management Policies — Certificate Management, Device Connector, IMC Access, IPMI Over LAN, LDAP, Local User, Network Connectivity, SMTP, SNMP, SSH, Serial over LAN, Syslog, NTP, and Virtual KVM policies
  * Network Policies — Adapter Configuration, LAN Connectivity, and SAN Connectivity policies.
     - The LAN Connectivity policy requires you to create an Ethernet Adapter Policy, Ethernet Network Policy (Standalone only), and Ethernet QoS Policy. When you attach a LAN Connectivity policy to a server profile, the addresses of the MAC address Pool, or the static MAC address, are automatically assigned.
     - Note: A LAN Connectivity policy that has a static MAC address can be attached to only one server profile.
     - The SAN Connectivity policy requires you to create an Fibre Channel Adapter Policy, Fibre Channel Network Policy, and Fiber Channel QoS Policy. When you attach a SAN Connectivity policy to a server profile, the addresses of the WWPN and WWNN Pools, or the static WWPN and WWNN addresses, are automatically assigned.
     - Note:  A SAN Connectivity policy that has a static WWPN, or a static WWNN can be attached to only one server profile.
  * Storage Policies — Drive Security, SD Card, and Storage policies.

IMPORTANT:
  * The server profile feature is available in Cisco Intersight only if:
     - You have assigned a Cisco Intersight Essentials License to the physical servers.

Required Base Attributes:
  * `action`
  * `attach_template`
  * `targets`
  * `target_platform`

Required for FIAttached when using pre-assignment with the targets:
  * `domain_name`

Optional Attributes
  * `detach_template`
  * `ignore_reservations`
  * `resource_pool`
  * `tags`
  * `ucs_server_profile_template`


#===========================================================
# FIAttached and Unified Edge - Policies
#===========================================================

#=======================================
# Compute Configuration
#=======================================
  * `bios_policy_policy`
  * `boot_order_policy`
  * `firmware_policy`
  * `memory_policy`
  * `pcie_connectivity_policy` - FIAttached
  * `power_policy`
  * `scrub_policy
  * `thermal_policy` - FIAttached
  * `uuid_pool`
  * `virtual_media_policy`

#=======================================
# Management Configuration
#=======================================
  * `certificate_management_policy`
  * `imc_access_policy`
  * `ipmi_over_lan_policy`
  * `local_user_policy`
  * `serial_over_lan_policy`
  * `snmp_policy`
  * `syslog_policy`
  * `virtual_kvm_policy`

#=======================================
# Storage Configuration
#=======================================
  * `drive_security_policy` - FIAttached
  * `sd_card_policy` - FIAttached
  * `storage_policy`

#=======================================
# Network Configuration
#=======================================
  * `lan_connectivity_policy`
  * `san_connectivity_policy` - FIAttached


#===========================================================
# Standalone - Policies
#===========================================================

#=======================================
# Compute Configuration
#=======================================
  * `bios_policy_policy`
  * `boot_order_policy`
  * `firmware_policy`
  * `memory_policy`
  * `persistent_memory_policy`
  * `power_policy`
  * `thermal_policy`
  * `virtual_media_policy`

#=======================================
# Management Configuration
#=======================================
  * `certificate_management_policy`
  * `device_connector_policy`
  * `ipmi_over_lan_policy`
  * `ldap_policy`
  * `local_user_policy`
  * `network_connectivity_policy`
  * `ntp_policy`
  * `serial_over_lan_policy`
  * `smtp_policy`
  * `snmp_policy`
  * `ssh_policy`
  * `syslog_policy`
  * `virtual_kvm_policy`

#=======================================
# Storage Configuration
#=======================================
  * `drive_security_policy`
  * `sd_card_policy`
  * `storage_policy`

#=======================================
# Network Configuration
#=======================================
  * `adapter_configuration_policy`
  * `lan_connectivity_policy`
  * `san_connectivity_policy`
server.title: server - Server Profile - Intersight Uri: server/Profiles

unified_edge.description:
A Unified Edge Profile configures a the chassis and eCMC modules through reusable policies, allowing for configuration of the ports and port channels, and configures the VLANs in the network. It defines the characteristics of, and configures ports on the Unified Edge system. You can create a Unified Edge profile and associate it with a Unified Edge system. The related policies can be attached to the profile either at the time of creation or later. One Unified Edge profile can be assigned to one system.

Important:
  * You can use a single port policy per Unified Edge profile or a port-policy per eCMC.
  * Policies that are attached to a Unified Edge profile can be created ahead of creating a profile or during the creation of the profile.
  * Policies that are attached to a Unified Edge and the global policies of all Unified Edge Profiles associated with a specific Unified Edge are shared.

Required Base Attributes:
  * `action`
  * `name`
  * `serial_number`

Optional Base Attributes:
  * `attach_template`
  * `description`
  * `tags`
  * `unified_edge_profile_template` - to assign to a template

#=======================================
# Chassis Configuration
#=======================================
  * `power_policy`
  * `thermal_policy`

#=======================================
# Switch Configuration
#=======================================
  * `port_policies` - required (If not attached to a domain template)
  * `switch_control_policy`
  * `system_qos_policy` - required (If not attached to a domain template)
  * `vlan_policies` - required (If not attached to a domain template) - A list of one or two VLAN policies.  A list of one will assign the same policy to both fabrics.

#=======================================
# Management Configuration
#=======================================
  * `local_user_policy`
  * `network_connectivity_policy`
  * `ntp_policy`
  * `syslog_policy`
unified_edge.title: unified_edge - Unified Edge Cluster Profile - Intersight Uri: fabric/SwitchClusterProfiles

intersight.profiles.server.targets.pre_a.description:
Enabled the Pre-Assignment of a Server Profile to either a Chassis/Slot or using the Serial Number.  * Note - If using Chassis/Slot make sure you have defined the domain_name as well to the Server Profile Section.
For Chassis/Slot assignment:`
  * `chassis`
  * `slot`

For Serial Pre-Assignment:`
  * `serial_number`

chassis_id.description: The default value is `0`.  The default value is `0` (Unused).  The Chassis Id to Pre-Assign this Server Profile to.
chassis_id.title: chassis_id

serial_number.description: The default value is ``.  Serial Number of the Device.  You can assign unknown if you want to generate the Profile without assigning hardware.
serial_number.title: serial_number

slot_id.description: The default value is `1`.  The Slot Identity to Assign the Server Profile to.
slot_id.title: slot_id

  * `pool_name`

allOf.description: SAN connectivity policy determines the network storage resources and the connections between the server and the SAN on the network. This policy enables configuration of vHBAs that the servers use to communicate with the storage network.

  * `Standalone - a server that is operating independently`
  * `FIAttached - A Server attached to a Intersight Managed Domain.

vhba_placement_mode.description:
The default value is `custom`.  The mode used for placement of vNICs on network adapters. It can either be Auto or Custom.
  * `auto` - The placement of the vNICs / vHBAs on network adapters is automatically determined by the system.
  * `custom` - The placement of the vNICs / vHBAs on network adapters is manually chosen by the user.
vhba_placement_mode.title: vhba_placement_mode

vhbas.description:

  * `fibre_channel_adapter_policy`

  * `fibre_channel_qos_policy`
  * `placement`

Optional Attributes:
  * `fc_zone_policies`
  * `persistent_lun_bindings`
  * `pin_group_names`
  * `vhba_type`
  * `wwpn_pools`
  * `wwpn_static_addresses`

  * `name`
  * `placement`

vhba_type.description:
The default value is `fc-initiator`.  vHBA Type configuration for SAN Connectivity Policy. This configuration is supported only on Cisco VIC 14XX series and higher series of adapters.
  * `fc-initiator` - The default value set for vHBA Type Configuration. Fc-initiator specifies vHBA as a consumer of storage. Enables SCSI commands to transfer data and status information between host and target storage systems.
  * `fc-nvme-initiator` - Fc-nvme-initiator specifies vHBA as a consumer of storage. Enables NVMe-based message commands to transfer data and status information between host and target storage systems.
  * `fc-nvme-target` - Fc-nvme-target specifies vHBA as a provider of storage volumes to initiators. Enables NVMe-based message commands to transfer data and status information between host and target storage systems. Currently tech-preview, only enabled with an asynchronous driver.
  * `fc-target` - Fc-target specifies vHBA as a provider of storage volumes to initiators. Enables SCSI commands to transfer data and status information between host and target storage systems. fc-target is enabled only with an asynchronous driver.
vhba_type.title: vhba_type

wwpn_static_addresses.description:
A List of Static WWPN Addresses to Assign to the vHBA's if the Allocation Type is set to STATIC.  The WWPN address must be in hexadecimal format xx:xx:xx:xx:xx:xx:xx:xx.
Allowed ranges are 20:00:00:00:00:00:00:00 to 20:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF or from 50:00:00:00:00:00:00:00 to 5F:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF.
To ensure uniqueness of WWN's in the SAN fabric, you are strongly encouraged to use the WWN prefix - 20:00:00:25:B5:xx:xx:xx.

items.description:
The default value is `20:00:00:25:B5:00:00:00`.  The WWPN address must be in hexadecimal format xx:xx:xx:xx:xx:xx:xx:xx.
Allowed ranges are 20:00:00:00:00:00:00:00 to 20:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF or from 50:00:00:00:00:00:00:00 to 5F:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF.
To ensure uniqueness of WWN's in the SAN fabric, you are strongly encouraged to use the WWN prefix - 20:00:00:25:B5:xx:xx:xx.
