intersight.san_connectivity.vhbas.placem.description:
Placement Settings for the virtual interface.

Required Attributes:
  * `pci_order`

Optional Attributes:
  * `pci_links`
  * `slot_ids`
  * `switch_ids`
  * `uplink_ports`

automatic_pci_link_assignment.description: The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true` if `pci_links` is undefined, otherwise this is ignored.  Enable or disable automatic assignment of the PCI Link in a dual-link adapter. This option applies only to 13xx series VICs that support dual-link. If enabled, the system determines the placement of the vNIC/vHBA on either of the PCI Links.
automatic_pci_link_assignment.title: automatic_pci_link_assignment

automatic_slot_id_assignment.description: The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true` if `slot_ids` is undefined, otherwise this is ignored.  Enable or disable automatic assignment of the VIC slot ID. If enabled and the server has only one VIC, the same VIC is chosen for all the vNICs. If enabled and the server has multiple VICs, the vNIC/vHBA are deployed on the first VIC. The Slot ID determines the first VIC. MLOM is the first Slot ID and the ID increments to 2, 3, and so on.
automatic_slot_id_assignment.title: automatic_slot_id_assignment

pci_links.description: The PCI Link used as transport for the virtual interface. All VIC adapters have a single PCI link except VIC 1385 which has two.

items.description: The default value is `0`.  Adapter port on which the virtual interface will be created.  Range is `0-3`.
items.title: pci_link

pci_links.title: pci_links - Pci Link - Object Type: vnic.PlacementSettings

pci_order.description: The order in which the virtual interface is brought up. The order assigned to an interface should be unique for all the Ethernet and Fibre-Channel interfaces on each PCI link on a VIC adapter. The order should start from zero with no overlaps. The maximum value of PCI order is limited by the number of virtual interfaces (Ethernet and Fibre-Channel) on each PCI link on a VIC adapter. All VIC adapters have a single PCI link except VIC 1340, VIC 1380 and VIC 1385 which have two.

items.description: The default value is `0`.  PCI Order Assigned to the Interface. The order should start from zero with no overlaps, including LAN and SAN Policies.
items.title: pci_order

pci_order.title: pci_order - Pci Order

slot_ids.description: List of PCIe Slots where the VIC adapter is installed. Supported values are (1-15) and MLOM.

items.description: The default value is ``.  PCIe Slot where the VIC adapter is installed. Supported values are (1-15) and MLOM.
items.title: slot_id

slot_ids.title: slot_ids - Slot Ids - Object Type: vnic.PlacementSettings

switch_ids.description:
A List of the fabric port(s) to which the vNICs will be associated.

  * `A` - Fabric A of the FI cluster.
  * `B` - Fabric B of the FI cluster.
  * `None` - Fabric Id is `None` for a standalone server use-case where the server is not connected to Fabric Interconnects.

items.description:

  * `A` - Fabric A of the FI cluster.
  * `B` - Fabric B of the FI cluster.
  * `None` - Fabric Id is `None` for a standalone server use-case where the server is not connected to Fabric Interconnects.

automatic_pci_link_assignment.description: The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true` if `pci_link` is undefined, otherwise this is ignored.  Enable or disable automatic assignment of the PCI Link in a dual-link adapter. This option applies only to 13xx series VICs that support dual-link. If enabled, the system determines the placement of the vNIC/vHBA on either of the PCI Links.

automatic_slot_id_assignment.description: The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true` if `slot_id` is undefined, otherwise this is ignored.  Enable or disable automatic assignment of the VIC slot ID. If enabled and the server has only one VIC, the same VIC is chosen for all the vNICs. If enabled and the server has multiple VICs, the vNIC/vHBA are deployed on the first VIC. The Slot ID determines the first VIC. MLOM is the first Slot ID and the ID increments to 2, 3, and so on.

baud_rate.description:
The default value is `115200`.  Baud Rate used for Serial Over LAN communication.
  * `9600`   - Use baud rate 9600 for communication.
  * `19200`  - Use baud rate 19200 for communication.
  * `38400`  - Use baud rate 38400 for communication.
  * `57600`  - Use baud rate 57600 for communication.
  * `115200` - Use baud rate 115200 for communication.

com_port.description:
The default value is `com0`.  Serial port through which the system routes Serial Over LAN communication. This field is available only on some Cisco UCS C-Series servers. If it is unavailable, the server uses COM port 0 by default.
  * `com0` - Use serial port com0 for communication.
  * `com1` - Use serial port com1 for communication.

allOf.description:
A server will be included in the resource pool if it matches the conditions of all the qualifiers present in the policy. The qualifiers are of four types:`
  * `Domain` — All FI-attached servers in any domain that match the provided Fabric Interconnect PID and domain name qualify under this type.
  * `Server` — All blade and/or rack servers that match the provided server IDs, PIDs, tags, user labels, and chassis IDs qualify under this type.
  * `Tag` — All servers that match any of the provided server tags, domain profile, and chassis tags qualify under this type. Domain profile and chassis tags are applicable only for FI-attached servers.
  * `Hardware` — All servers with hardware components that match the provided CPU, memory, GPU, and Network adapters qualify under this type.

This policy is applicable for Intersight Managed Mode and Standalone servers.

Required Attributes:
  * `name`

Optional Attributes:
  * `description`
  * `domain_qualifiers`
  * `hardware_qualifiers`
  * `server_qualifiers`
  * `tag_qualifiers`
  * `tags`
allOf.title: server_pool_qualification - Server Pool Qualification - Intersight Uri: resourcepool/QualificationPolicies

intersight.server_pool_qualification.description:
A server will be included in the resource pool if it matches the conditions of all the qualifiers present in the policy. The qualifiers are of four types:`
  * `Domain` — All FI-attached servers in any domain that match the provided Fabric Interconnect PID and domain name qualify under this type.
  * `Server` — All blade and/or rack servers that match the provided server IDs, PIDs, tags, user labels, and chassis IDs qualify under this type.
  * `Tag` — All servers that match any of the provided server tags, domain profile, and chassis tags qualify under this type. Domain profile and chassis tags are applicable only for FI-attached servers.
  * `Hardware` — All servers with hardware components that match the provided CPU, memory, GPU, and Network adapters qualify under this type.

This policy is applicable for Intersight Managed Mode and Standalone servers.

Required Attributes:
  * `name`

Optional Attributes:
  * `description`
  * `domain_qualifiers`
  * `hardware_qualifiers`
  * `server_qualifiers`
  * `tag_qualifiers`
  * `tags`
intersight.server_pool_qualification.title: server_pool_qualification - Server Pool Qualification - Intersight Uri: resourcepool/QualificationPolicies

intersight.server_pool_qualification.dom.description:
Attributes for all FI-attached servers in any domain that match the provided Fabric Interconnect PID and/or domain name qualify under this type.

Optional Attributes:
  * `domain_names`
  * `fabric_interconnect_pids`

domain_names.description: A List of Domain Names to add to the Domain Qualifier for server qualification.  These must be pre-existing domains.  These are the domain system names that show up under targets, not the name of the profile.  The maximum limit is 100

items.description: The default value is `example`.  An Existing domain system name in the Intersight Organization.
items.title: domain_name

domain_names.title: domain_names

fabric_interconnect_pids.description:
A list of Fabric Interconnect product identifiers for the server qualification.

Options are:

  * `UCS-FI-6454` - The standard 4th generation UCS Fabric Interconnect with 54 ports.
  * `UCS-FI-64108` - The expanded 4th generation UCS Fabric Interconnect with 108 ports.
  * `UCS-FI-6536` - The standard 5th generation UCS Fabric Interconnect with 36 ports.
  * `UCSX-S9108-100G` - Cisco UCS Fabric Interconnect 9108 100G with 8 ports.
  * `UCS-FI-6664` - The standard 6th generation UCS Fabric Interconnect with 64 ports.
  * `UCS-FI-6652` - The standard 6th generation UCS Fabric Interconnect with 52 ports.
  * `UCSXE-ECMC-10G` - Cisco UCS XE ECMC 10G with 2 ports.
  * `UCSXE-ECMC-G1` - Cisco UCS XE ECMC G1 with 2 ports.

items.description: The default value is ``.  Fabric Interconnect Product ID.
items.title: fabric_interconnect_pid

fabric_interconnect_pids.title: fabric_interconnect_pids

  * `maximum`

minimum.description: The default value is `1`.  The Minimum CPU Speed Range in Ghz.  Number between 1-99.  Must be less than or equal to the maximum value.

evaluation_type.description:
The default value is `Unspecified`.  The GPU qualifier used for resource qualification is based on gpuEvaluationType.
  * `ServerWithoutGpu` - It is used to specify the evaluation of a resource without a GPU.
  * `ServerWithGpu` - It is used to specify the evaluation of a resource with a GPU.
  * `Unspecified` - It is used to specify that the GPU qualifier should be ignored for the evaluation of a resource.

allOf.description: Name that identifies the SMTP Policy.

enable_smtp.description: The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true`.  If enabled, controls the state of the SMTP client service on the managed device.
enable_smtp.title: enable_smtp

mail_alert_recipients.description: List of Emails to send alerts to.

items.description: The default value is `admin@example.com`.  Mail alert recipient email.
items.title: mail_alert_recipient

mail_alert_recipients.title: mail_alert_recipients - Mail Alert Recipients

minimum_severity.description:
The default value is `critical`.  Minimum fault severity level to receive email notifications. Email notifications are sent for all faults whose severity is equal to or greater than the chosen level.
  * `critical`  - Minimum severity to report is critical.
  * `condition` - Minimum severity to report is informational.
  * `warning`   - Minimum severity to report is warning.
  * `minor`     - Minimum severity to report is minor.
  * `major`     - Minimum severity to report is major.
minimum_severity.title: minimum_severity

smtp_alert_sender_address.description: The default value is `intersight@example.com`.  The email address entered here will be displayed as the from address (mail received from address) of all the SMTP mail alerts that are received. If not configured, the hostname of the server is used in the from address field.
smtp_alert_sender_address.title: smtp_alert_sender_address

smtp_port.description: The default value is `25`.  Port number used by the SMTP server for outgoing SMTP communication.
smtp_port.title: smtp_port

smtp_server_address.description: The default value is `smtp.example.com`.  IP address or hostname of the SMTP server. The SMTP server is used by the managed device to send email notifications.
smtp_server_address.title: smtp_server_address

allOf.description: Policy to configure SNMP settings on endpoint.

access_community_string.description: The default value is `1`.  The Sensitive Variable Identifier for the Access Community String.  The SNMP Access Community String for devices performing SNMP Queries against the appliance for SNMPv1/v2c. The value can be 18 characters long.  A number Between 1-64 to denote the variables snmp_access_community_string_[1-64].  i.e. `export snmp_access_community_string_1='cisco123'`.
access_community_string.title: access_community_string

enable_snmp.description: The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true`.  State of the SNMP Policy on the endpoint. If enabled, the endpoint sends SNMP traps to the designated host.
enable_snmp.title: enable_snmp

snmp_community_access.description:
The default value is `Disabled`.  Controls access to the information in the inventory tables. Applicable only for SNMPv1 and SNMPv2c users.
  * `disabled` - Blocks access to the information in the inventory tables.
  * `Limited` - Partial access to read the information in the inventory tables.
  * `Full` - Full access to read the information in the inventory tables.
snmp_community_access.title: snmp_community_access

snmp_engine_input_id.description: The default value is ``.  User-defined unique identification of the static engine.
snmp_engine_input_id.title: snmp_engine_input_id

snmp_port.description: The default value is `161`.  Port on which Cisco IMC SNMP agent runs. Enter a value between 1-65535. Reserved ports not allowed (22, 23, 80, 123, 389, 443, 623, 636, 2068, 3268, 3269).
snmp_port.title: snmp_port

List of SNMP Trap Destinations to Assign to the Policy.

Required Attributes:
  * `destination_address`

SNMPv2 Requires:
  * `community_string`

SNMPv3 Requires:
  * `user`

Optional Attributes:
  * `enable`
  * `port`
  * `trap_type`

List of SNMP Users to Assign to the SNMP Policy.

  * `auth_password`
  * `name`
  * `security_level`

Optional Attributes:
  * `auth_type`
  * `privacy_password`
  * `privacy_type`
snmp_users.title: snmp_users - Snmp Users - object_type: snmp.User

system_contact.description: The default value is `UCS Admin at Example.com`.  Contact person responsible for the SNMP implementation. Enter a string up to 64 characters, such as an email address or a name and telephone number.
system_contact.title: system_contact

system_location.description: The default value is `DataCenter`.  Location of host on which the SNMP agent (server) runs.
system_location.title: system_location

trap_community_string.description: The default value is `1`.  The Sensitive Variable Identifier for the Trap Community String.  The SNMP Trap Community String will be used for sending Trap notification to devices utilizing SNMPv1/v2c. The value can be 18 characters long.  A number Between 1-64 to denote the variables snmp_trap_community_[1-64].  i.e. `export snmp_trap_community_1='cisco123'`.
trap_community_string.title: trap_community_string

intersight.snmp.description: Policy to configure SNMP settings on endpoint.
intersight.snmp.title: snmp - SNMP - Intersight Uri: snmp/Policies

intersight.snmp.snmp_trap_destinations.description: Destination Servers for trap message sent from an agent to the manager.

community_string.description: The default value is `1`.  The default value is `0`.  A number Between 1-5 to denote to use one of the variables snmp_auth_password_[1-5].  Any other number means no community string.  A number Between 1-64 to denote the variables snmp_auth_password_[1-64].
community_string.title: community_string

destination_address.description: The default value is `server.example.com`.  Address to which the SNMP trap information is sent.
destination_address.title: destination_address

enable.description: The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true`.  Enables/disables the trap on the server If enabled, trap is active on the server.
enable.title: enable

port.description: The default value is `162`.  Port used by the server to communicate with the trap destination. Enter a value between 1-65535. Reserved ports not allowed (22, 23, 80, 123, 389, 443, 623, 636, 2068, 3268, 3269).
port.title: port

trap_type.description:
The default value is `Trap`.  Type of trap which decides whether to receive a notification when a trap is received at the destination.
  * `Trap` - Do not receive notifications when trap is sent to the destination.
  * `Inform` - Receive notifications when trap is sent to the destination. This option is valid only for V2 users.
trap_type.title: trap_type

user.description: The default value is ``.  SNMP user for the trap. Applicable only to SNMPv3.
user.title: user

intersight.snmp.snmp_trap_destinations.title: snmp_remote_destinations - SNMP Trap Servers.

intersight.snmp.snmp_users.description: User based security model, for communication between an agent and manager. Applicable only for SNMPv3.

auth_password.description: The default value is `1`.  A number Between 1-64 to denote the variables snmp_user_auth_password_[1-64].  i.e. `export snmp_user_auth_password_1='cisco123'`.
auth_password.title: auth_password

auth_type.description:
The default value is `SHA`.  Authorization protocol for authenticating the user.
  * `NA` - Authentication protocol is not applicable.
  * `MD5` - MD5 protocol is used to authenticate SNMP user.
  * `SHA` - SHA protocol is used to authenticate SNMP user.
  * `SHA-256` - SHA-256 protocol is used to authenticate SNMP user.
  * `SHA-384` - SHA-384 protocol is used to authenticate SNMP user.
  * `SHA-512` - SHA-512 protocol is used to authenticate SNMP user.
auth_type.title: auth_type

name.description: The default value is `snmpadmin`.  SNMP username. Must have a minimum of 1 and a maximum of 31 characters.  Cannot be `admin`.
name.title: name

privacy_password.description: The default value is `1`.  A number Between 1-64 to denote the variables snmp_user_privacy_password_[1-64].  i.e. `export snmp_user_privacy_password_1='cisco123'`.
privacy_password.title: privacy_password

privacy_type.description:
The default value is `AES`.  Privacy protocol for the user.
  * `NA` - Privacy protocol is not applicable.
  * `DES` - DES privacy protocol is used for SNMP user.
  * `AES` - AES privacy protocol is used for SNMP user.
privacy_type.title: privacy_type

security_level.description:
The default value is `AuthPriv`.  Security mechanism used for communication between agent and manager.
  * `AuthPriv` - The user requires both an authorization password and a privacy password.
  * `NoAuthNoPriv` - The user does not require an authorization or privacy password.
  * `AuthNoPriv` - The user requires an authorization password but not a privacy password.
security_level.title: security_level

intersight.snmp.snmp_users.title: users - SNMP V3 Users

enable_ssh.title: enable_ssh

ssh_port.description: The default value is `22`.  Port used for secure shell access.
ssh_port.title: ssh_port

ssh_timeout.description: The default value is `1800`.  Number of seconds to wait before the system considers a SSH request to have timed out.
ssh_timeout.title: ssh_timeout

allOf.title: ssh - SSH - Intersight Uri: ssh/Policies

intersight.ssh.description: Secure shell policy on the endpoint.
intersight.ssh.title: ssh - SSH - Intersight Uri: ssh/Policies

allOf.description: The storage policy models the reusable storage related configuration that can be applied on many servers. This policy allows creation of RAID groups using existing disk group policies and virtual drives on the drive groups. The user has options to move all unused disks to JBOD or Unconfigured good state. The encryption of drives can be enabled through this policy using remote keys from a KMIP server.

default_drive_state.description:
The default value is `UnconfiguredGood`.  All unconfigured drives (non-user configured drives) will move to the selected state on deployment. Newly inserted drives will move to the selected state. Select Unconfigured Good option to retain the existing configuration. Select JBOD to move the unconfigured drives to JBOD state. Select RAID0 to create a RAID0 virtual drive on each of the unconfigured drives. If JBOD is selected, unconfigured drives will move to JBOD state on host reboot. This setting is applicable only to selected set of controllers on FI attached servers.
  * `UnconfiguredGood` - Newly inserted drives or on reboot, drives will remain the same state.
  * `Jbod` - Newly inserted drives or on reboot, drives will automatically move to JBOD state if drive state was UnconfiguredGood.
  * `RAID0` - Newly inserted drives or on reboot, virtual drives will be created, respective drives will move to Online state.
default_drive_state.title: default_drive_state

drive_groups.description:
A reusable RAID drive group configuration that specifies a pool of drives and a set of virtual drives that are to be created using this pool of drives.

  * `name`
Optional Attributes are:
  * `automatic_drive_group`
  * `manual_drive_group`
  * `raid_level`
  * `secure_drive_group`
  * `virtual_drives`
drive_groups.title: drive_groups - Drive Groups - Intersight Uri: storage/DriveGroups

global_hot_spares.description: The default value is ``.  A collection of disks that is to be used as hot spares, globally, for all the RAID groups. Allowed value is a number range separated by a comma or a hyphen.
global_hot_spares.title: global_hot_spares

hybrid_slot_configuration:direct_attache.description: The default value is ``.  Only U.3 NVMe drives has to be specified, entered slots will be moved to Direct attached mode. Allowed slots are 1-4, 101-104. Allowed value is a comma or hyphen separated number range.
hybrid_slot_configuration:direct_attache.title: hybrid_slot_configuration:direct_attached_nvme_slots

hybrid_slot_configuration:raid_attached_.description: The default value is ``.  Only U.3 NVMe drives has to be specified, entered slots will be moved to RAID attached mode. Allowed slots are 1-4, 101-104. Allowed value is a comma or hyphen separated number range.
hybrid_slot_configuration:raid_attached_.title: hybrid_slot_configuration:raid_attached_nvme_slots

secure_jbod_disk_slots.description: The default value is ``.  JBOD drives specified in this slot range will be encrypted. Allowed values are 'ALL', or a comma or hyphen separated number range. Sample format is ALL or 1, 3 or 4-6, 8. Setting the value to 'ALL' will encrypt all the unused UnconfigureGood/JBOD disks.
secure_jbod_disk_slots.title: secure_jbod_disk_slots

unused_disks_state.description:
The default value is `NoChange`.  State to which drives, not used in this policy, are to be moved. NoChange will not change the drive state. No Change must be selected if Default Drive State is set to JBOD or RAID0.
  * `NoChange` - Drive state will not be modified by Storage Policy.
  * `UnconfiguredGood` - Unconfigured good state -ready to be added in a RAID group.
  * `Jbod` - JBOD state where the disks start showing up to Host OS.
unused_disks_state.title: unused_disks_state

use_jbod_for_vd_creation.description: The default value is `False`.  The default value is `false`.  Disks in JBOD State are used to create virtual drives. This setting must be disabled if Default Drive State is set to JBOD.
use_jbod_for_vd_creation.title: use_jbod_for_vd_creation

allOf.title: storage

intersight.storage.description: The storage policy models the reusable storage related configuration that can be applied on many servers. This policy allows creation of RAID groups using existing disk group policies and virtual drives on the drive groups. The user has options to move all unused disks to JBOD or Unconfigured good state. The encryption of drives can be enabled through this policy using remote keys from a KMIP server.
intersight.storage.title: storage - Storage - Intersight Uri: storage/StoragePolicies

intersight.storage.drive_groups.description: A reusable RAID drive group configuration that specifies a pool of drives and a set of virtual drives that are to be created using this pool of drives.

name.description: The default value is `dg0`.  The name of the drive group. The name can be between 1 and 64 alphanumeric characters. Spaces or any special characters other than - (hyphen), _ (underscore), : (colon), and . (period) are not allowed.
name.title: name

raid_level.description:
The default value is `Raid1`.  The supported RAID level for the disk group.
  * `Raid0` - RAID 0 Stripe Raid Level.
  * `Raid1` - RAID 1 Mirror Raid Level.
  * `Raid5` - RAID 5 Mirror Raid Level.
  * `Raid6` - RAID 6 Mirror Raid Level.
  * `Raid10` - RAID 10 Mirror Raid Level.
  * `Raid50` - RAID 50 Mirror Raid Level.
  * `Raid60` - RAID 60 Mirror Raid Level.
raid_level.title: raid_level

secure_drive_group.description: The default value is `False`.  The default value is `false`.  Enables/disables the drive encryption on all the drives used in this policy. This flag just enables the drive security and only after remote key setting configured, the actual encryption will be done.
secure_drive_group.title: secure_drive_group

virtual_drives.description:

intersight.storage.drive_groups.automati.description: Pool represents a collection of IPv4 and/or IPv6 addresses that can be allocated to other configuration entities like server profiles.

drive_type.description:
The default value is `Any`.  Type of drive that should be used for this RAID group.
  * `Any` - Any type of drive can be used for virtual drive creation.
  * `HDD` - Hard disk drives should be used for virtual drive creation.
  * `SSD` - Solid state drives should be used for virtual drive creation.
drive_type.title: drive_type

drives_per_span.description: The default value is `1`.  Number of drives within this span group. The minimum number of disks needed in a span group varies based on RAID level. RAID0 requires at least one disk. RAID1 and RAID10 requires at least 2 and in multiples of . RAID5 and RAID50 require at least 3 disks in a span group. RAID6 and RAID60 require atleast 4 disks in a span.
drives_per_span.title: drives_per_span

minimum_drive_size.description: The default value is `1`.  Minimum size of the drive to be used for creating this RAID group.
minimum_drive_size.title: minimum_drive_size

num_dedicated_hot_spares.description: The default value is ``.  Number of dedicated hot spare disks for this RAID group. Allowed value is a comma or hyphen separated number range.
num_dedicated_hot_spares.title: num_dedicated_hot_spares

number_of_spans.description: The default value is `1`.  Number of span groups to be created for this RAID group. Non-nested RAID levels have a single span.
number_of_spans.title: number_of_spans

use_remaining_drives.description: The default value is `False`.  The default value is `false`.  This flag enables the drive group to use all the remaining drives on the server.
use_remaining_drives.title: use_remaining_drives

intersight.storage.drive_groups.automati.title: AutomaticDriveGroups

intersight.storage.drive_groups.manual_d.description:
This drive group is created by specifying the drive slots to be used.

Required Attributes:
  * `drive_array_spans

Optional Attributes:
  * `dedicated_hot_spares`

dedicated_hot_spares.description: The default value is ``.  A collection of drives to be used as hot spares for this Drive Group.
dedicated_hot_spares.title: dedicated_hot_spares

drive_array_spans.description:
Enter list of Disk IDs separated by comma or hypen. Sample format is 1, 3 or 4-6, 8. Non spanned RAID levels like RAID0, RAID1, RAID5 and RAID6 expect a single group of disks whereas spanned RAID levels need multiple groups of disks with each group representing a span group. Non spanned RAID levels expect one span group and spanned RAID levels accept minimum 2 span groups and up to 8.

Required Attributes:
  * `slots`
drive_array_spans.title: drive_array_spans - Drive Array Spans - object_type: storage.SpanDrives

intersight.storage.drive_groups.manual_d.description: This models a single span group of disks in a RAID group.

slots.description: The default value is `1-2`.  Collection of local disks that are part of this span group. Allowed value is a comma or hyphen separated number range. The minimum number of disks needed in a span group varies based on RAID level. RAID0 requires at least one disk, RAID1 and RAID10 requires at least 2 and in multiples of 2, RAID5 RAID50 RAID6 and RAID60 require at least 3 disks in a span group.
slots.title: slots

intersight.storage.drive_groups.virtual_.description: Definition of the list of properties defined in 'storage.VirtualDriveConfiguration', excluding properties defined in parent classes.

boot_drive.description: The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true`.  This flag enables this virtual drive to be used as a boot drive.
boot_drive.title: boot_drive

expand_to_available.description: The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true`.  This flag enables the virtual drive to use all the space available in the disk group. When this flag is enabled, the size property is ignored.
expand_to_available.title: expand_to_available

name.description: The default value is `vd0`.  The name of the virtual drive. The name can be between 1 and 15 alphanumeric characters. Spaces or any special characters other than - (hyphen), _ (underscore), : (colon), and . (period) are not allowed.
name.title: name

size.description: The default value is `0`.  Virtual drive size in MebiBytes. Size is mandatory field except when the Expand to Available option is enabled.
size.title: size

intersight.storage.drive_groups.virtual_.title: VirtualDrive

intersight.storage.m2_raid_configuration.description:
This models the options for creating a virtual drive on M.2 RAID controller.

Required Attributes:
  * `name`
  * `slot`

name.description: The default value is `MStorBootVd`.  Default value is `MStorBootVd`.  The name of the virtual drive. The name can be between 1 and 15 alphanumeric characters. Spaces or any special characters other than - (hyphen), _ (underscore), : (colon), and . (period) are not allowed. This field will be pre-populated with the default or user configured value which can be edited.
name.title: name

slot.description:
The default value is `MSTOR-RAID-1`.  Select the M.2 RAID controller slot on which the virtual drive is to be created. Select 'MSTOR-RAID-1' to create virtual drive on the M.2 RAID controller in the first slot or in the MSTOR-RAID slot, 'MSTOR-RAID-2' for second slot, 'MSTOR-RAID-1, MSTOR-RAID-2' for both slots or either slot.
  * `MSTOR-RAID-1` - Virtual drive  will be created on the M.2 RAID controller in the first slot.
  * `MSTOR-RAID-2` - Virtual drive  will be created on the M.2 RAID controller in the second slot, if available.
  * `MSTOR-RAID-1,MSTOR-RAID-2` - Virtual drive  will be created on the M.2 RAID controller in both the slots, if available.
slot.title: slot

intersight.storage.m2_raid_configuration.title: m2_raid_configuration - object_type: storage.M2VirtualDriveConfig

intersight.storage.single_drive_raid0_co.description:
This models a Single Drive RAID Configuration for creation of on RAID0 virtual drive per physical drive and occupying the full disk space on the same.

Required Attributes:
  * `drive_slots`
  * `virtual_drive_policy`

drive_slots.description: The default value is `1-2`.  The set of drive slots where RAID0 virtual drives must be created.
drive_slots.title: drive_slots

intersight.storage.single_drive_raid0_co.title: single_drive_raid0_configuration - Single Drive Raid0 Configuration - object_type: storage.R0Drive

intersight.storage.virtual_drive_policy.description:
This models the manual drive selection configuration.

Optional Attributes:
  * `access_policy`
  * `drive_cache`
  * `read_policy`
  * `strip_size`
  * `write_policy`

access_policy.description:
The default value is `Default`.  Access policy that host has on this virtual drive.
  * `Default` - Use platform default access mode.
  * `ReadWrite` - Enables host to perform read-write on the VD.
  * `ReadOnly` - Host can only read from the VD.
  * `Blocked` - Host can neither read nor write to the VD.
access_policy.title: access_policy

drive_cache.description:
The default value is `Default`.  Disk cache policy for the virtual drive.
  * `Default` - Use platform default drive cache mode.
  * `NoChange` - Drive cache policy is unchanged.
  * `Enable` - Enables IO caching on the drive.
  * `Disable` - Disables IO caching on the drive.
drive_cache.title: drive_cache

read_policy.description:
The default value is `Default`.  Read ahead mode to be used to read data from this virtual drive.
  * `Default` - Use platform default read ahead mode.
  * `ReadAhead` - Use read ahead mode for the policy.
  * `NoReadAhead` - Do not use read ahead mode for the policy.
read_policy.title: read_policy

strip_size.description:
The default value is `64`.  The default value is `Default`.  Desired strip size - Allowed values are 64, 128, 256, 512, 1024.
  * `64` - Number of bytes in a strip is 64 Kibibytes.
  * `128` - Number of bytes in a strip is 128 Kibibytes.
  * `256` - Number of bytes in a strip is 256 Kibibytes.
  * `512` - Number of bytes in a strip is 512 Kibibytes.
  * `1024` - Number of bytes in a strip is 1024 Kibibytes or 1 Mebibyte.
strip_size.title: strip_size

write_policy.description:
The default value is `Default`.  Write mode to be used to write data to this virtual drive.
  * `Default` - Use platform default write mode.
  * `WriteThrough` - Data is written through the cache and to the physical drives. Performance is improved, because subsequent reads of that data can be satisfied from the cache.
  * `WriteBackGoodBbu` - Data is stored in the cache, and is only written to the physical drives when space in the cache is needed. Virtual drives requesting this policy fall back to Write Through caching when the battery backup unit (BBU) cannot guarantee the safety of the cache in the event of a power failure.
  * `AlwaysWriteBack` - With this policy, write caching remains Write Back even if the battery backup unit is defective or discharged.
write_policy.title: write_policy

intersight.storage.virtual_drive_policy.title: virtual_drive_policy - Storage Virtual Drive Policy.

aes_primary_key.description: The default value is `1`.  Encrypts MacSec keys in type-6 format. If a MacSec key is already provided in a type-6 format, the primary key decrypts it.  A number Between 1-64 to denote the variables switch_control_aes_primary_key_[1-64].  i.e. `export switch_control_aes_primary_key_1='cisco123'`.
aes_primary_key.title: aes_primary_key

fabric_port_channel_vhba_reset.description:
The default value is `Disabled`.  When enabled, a Registered State Change Notification (RSCN) is sent to the VIC adapter when any member port within the fabric port-channel goes down and vHBA would reset to restore the connection immediately. When disabled (default), vHBA reset is done only when all the members of a fabric port-channel are down.
  * `disabled` - Admin configured Disabled State.
  * `Enabled` - Admin configured Enabled State.
fabric_port_channel_vhba_reset.title: fabric_port_channel_vhba_reset

mac_address_table_aging.description:
The default value is `Default`.  This specifies one of the option to configure the MAC address aging time.
  * `Default` - This option sets the default MAC address aging time to 14500 seconds for End Host mode.
  * `Custom` - This option allows the the user to configure the MAC address aging time on the switch. For Switch Model UCS-FI-6454 or higher, the valid range is `120 to 918000` seconds and the switch will set the lower multiple of 5 of the given time.
  * `Never` - This option disables the MAC address aging process and never allows the MAC address entries to get removed from the table.
mac_address_table_aging.title: mac_address_table_aging

mac_aging_time.description: The default value is `300`.  Define the MAC address aging time in seconds. This field is valid when the "Custom" MAC address aging option is selected.
mac_aging_time.title: mac_aging_time

reserved_vlan_start_id.description:
The default value is `3915`.  The starting ID for VLANs reserved for internal use within the Fabric Interconnect. This VLAN ID is the starting ID of a contiguous block of 128 VLANs that cannot be configured for user data.  This range of VLANs cannot be configured in VLAN policy.
If this property is not configured, VLAN range 3915 - 4042 is reserved for internal use by default.
reserved_vlan_start_id.title: reserved_vlan_start_id

switching_mode_ethernet.description:
The default value is `end-host`.  Enable or Disable Ethernet End Host Switching Mode.
  * `end-host` - In end-host mode, the fabric interconnects appear to the upstream devices as end hosts with multiple links.  In this mode, the switch does not run Spanning Tree Protocol and avoids loops by following a set of rules for traffic forwarding.  In case of ethernet switching mode - Ethernet end-host mode is also known as Ethernet host virtualizer.
  * `switch` - In switch mode, the switch runs Spanning Tree Protocol to avoid loops, and broadcast and multicast packets are handled in the traditional way.  This is the traditional switch mode.
switching_mode_ethernet.title: switching_mode_ethernet

switching_mode_fc.description:
The default value is `end-host`.  Enable or Disable FC End Host Switching Mode.
  * `end-host` - In end-host mode, the fabric interconnects appear to the upstream devices as end hosts with multiple links.  In this mode, the switch does not run Spanning Tree Protocol and avoids loops by following a set of rules for traffic forwarding.  In case of ethernet switching mode - Ethernet end-host mode is also known as Ethernet host virtualizer.
  * `switch` - In switch mode, the switch runs Spanning Tree Protocol to avoid loops, and broadcast and multicast packets are handled in the traditional way.  This is the traditional switch mode.
switching_mode_fc.title: switching_mode_fc

target_platform.description:
The default value is `UCS Domain`.  The target platform type of the switch control policy.
  * `UCS Domain` - Profile/policy type for network and management configuration on UCS Fabric Interconnect.
  * `Unified Edge` - Profile/policy type for network, management and chassis configuration on Unified Edge.
target_platform.title: target_platform

vlan_port_count_optimization.description: The default value is `false`.  To enable or disable the VLAN port count optimization.
vlan_port_count_optimization.title: vlan_port_count_optimization

enable_jumbo_frames.description: To enable or disable Jumbo Frames on the switch. On Fabric Interconnects, Jumbo Frames are controlled by the System QoS policy, so this setting is ignored.
enable_jumbo_frames.title: enable_jumbo_frames

mac_address_table_aging.description:
The default value is `Default`.  This specifies one of the option to configure the MAC address aging time.
  * `Default` - This option sets the default MAC address aging time to 14500 seconds for End Host mode.
  * `Custom` - This option allows the the user to configure the MAC address aging time on the switch. For Switch Model UCS-FI-6454 or higher, the valid range is `120 to 918000` seconds and the switch will set the lower multiple of 5 of the given time.
mac_address_table_aging.title: mac_address_table_aging

mac_aging_time.description: The default value is `300`.  Define the MAC address aging time in seconds. This field is valid when the "Custom" MAC address aging option is selected.
mac_aging_time.title: mac_aging_time

target_platform.description:
The default value is `Unified Edge`.  The target platform type of the switch control policy.
  * `Unified Edge` - Profile/policy type for network, management and chassis configuration on Unified Edge.
  * `UCS Domain` - Profile/policy type for network and management configuration on UCS Fabric Interconnect.
target_platform.title: target_platform

intersight.switch_control.description: A policy to configure the Switching Mode, Port VLAN Optimization, MAC Aging Time, Reserved VLAN Range of the FI.
intersight.switch_control.title: switch_control - Switch Control - Intersight Uri: fabric/SwitchControlPolicies

intersight.switch_control.udld_global_se.description: Definition of the list of properties defined in 'fabric.UdldGlobalSettings', excluding properties defined in parent classes.

message_interval.description:
The default value is `15`.  Configures the time between UDLD probe messages on ports that are in advertisement mode and are
currently determined to be bidirectional.
Valid values are from 7 to 90 seconds.
message_interval.title: udld_message_interval

recovery_action.description:
The default value is `reset`.  UDLD recovery when enabled, attempts to bring an UDLD error-disabled port out of reset.
  * `none` - The standard 4th generation UCS Fabric Interconnect with 54 ports.
  * `reset` - The expanded 4th generation UCS Fabric Interconnect with 108 ports.
recovery_action.title: udld_recovery_action

intersight.switch_control.udld_global_se.title: udld_global_settings - UDLD Global Settings - object_type: fabric.UdldGlobalSettings

intersight.syslog.local_logging.description:
Set of local logging clients on the endpoint.

Required Attributes:
  * `minimum_severity`

minimum_severity.description:
The default value is `warning`.  Lowest level of messages to be included in the local log.
  * `warning` - Use logging level warning for logs classified as warning.
  * `emergency` - Use logging level emergency for logs classified as emergency.
  * `alert` - Use logging level alert for logs classified as alert.
  * `critical` - Use logging level critical for logs classified as critical.
  * `error` - Use logging level error for logs classified as error.
  * `notice` - Use logging level notice for logs classified as notice.
  * `informational` - Use logging level informational for logs classified as informational.
  * `debug` - Use logging level debug for logs classified as debug.
minimum_severity.title: minimum_severity

intersight.syslog.local_logging.title: local_logging - Local Syslog Logging

intersight.syslog.remote_logging.description: Lists properties that are common to all remote logging clients. It serves as a base class for all remote logging clients.

enable.description: The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true`.  Enables/disables remote logging for the endpoint If enabled, log messages will be sent to the syslog server mentioned in the Hostname/IP Address field.
enable.title: enable

hostname.description: The default value is `syslog.example.com`.  Hostname or IP Address of the syslog server where logs should be stored.
hostname.title: hostname

minimum_severity.description:
The default value is `warning`.  Lowest level of messages to be included in the remote log.
  * `warning` - Use logging level warning for logs classified as warning.
  * `emergency` - Use logging level emergency for logs classified as emergency.
  * `alert` - Use logging level alert for logs classified as alert.
  * `critical` - Use logging level critical for logs classified as critical.
  * `error` - Use logging level error for logs classified as error.
  * `notice` - Use logging level notice for logs classified as notice.
  * `informational` - Use logging level informational for logs classified as informational.
  * `debug` - Use logging level debug for logs classified as debug.
minimum_severity.title: minimum_severity

port.description: The default value is `514`.  Port number used for logging on syslog server.
port.title: port

protocol.description:
The default value is `udp`.  Transport layer protocol for transmission of log messages to syslog server.
  * `udp` - Use User Datagram Protocol (UDP) for syslog remote server connection.
  * `tcp` - Use Transmission Control Protocol (TCP) for syslog remote server connection.
protocol.title: protocol

intersight.syslog.remote_logging.title: remote_logging - Syslog Remote Logging

  * Platinum
  * Silver

configure_default_classes.description:
The default value is `false`.  Flag to Configure the following class values:

classes:
    - cos: 255
      priority: Best Effort
      state: Enabled
      weight: 5

      weight: 5

      priority: Bronze
      state: Enabled
      weight: 7
    - cos: 4
      priority: Gold
      state: Enabled
      weight: 9
    - cos: 5

      state: Enabled

    - cos: 2

intersight.system_qos.description: Configuration object sent by user to setup Quality of Service (QoS) for this switch.
intersight.system_qos.title: system_qos - System QoS - Intersight Uri: fabric/SystemQosPolicies

cos.description:
The default value is `255`.  Class of service Assigned to the System QoS Class.
  * `Best Effort` - By default is `255` and cannot be changed.
  * `Bronze` - By default is `1`.
  * `FC` - By default is `3` and cannot be changed.
  * `Gold` - By default is `4`.
  * `Platinum` - By default is `5`.
  * `Silver` - By default is `2`.
cos.title: cos

packet_drop.description: The default value is `True`.  The weight of the QoS Class controls the distribution of bandwidth between QoS Classes, with the same priority after the Guarantees for the QoS Classes are reached.
packet_drop.title: packet_drop

priority.description:
The default value is `Best Effort`.  Name of the priority class.  You must configure all the following classes:
  1. Best Effort
  2. Bronze
  3. FC
  4. Gold
  5. Platinum
  6. Silver
priority.title: priority

state.description:
The default value is `Disabled`.  Administrative state for the QoS class.
  * `disabled`  - Admin configured Disabled State.
  * `enabled` - Admin configured Enabled State.
Note: 'Best Effort' and 'FC' Classes are 'Enabled' and cannot be 'Disabled'.
state.title: state

weight.description:
The default value is `1`.  The weight of the QoS Class controls the distribution of bandwidth between QoS Classes,with the same priority after the Guarantees for the QoS Classes are reached.  Default is `1`.
  * `Best Effort` - By Default is `5`.
  * `Bronze` - By Default is `7`.
  * `FC` - By Default is `5`.
  * `Gold` - By Default is `9`.
  * `Platinum` - By Default is `10`.
  * `Silver` - By Default is `8`.
weight.title: weight

cos.description:
The default value is `255`.  Class of service Assigned to the System QoS Class.
  * `Best Effort` - By default is `255` and cannot be changed.
  * `Bronze` - By default is `1`.
  * `Gold` - By default is `4`.
  * `Platinum` - By default is `5`.
  * `Silver` - By default is `2`.
cos.title: cos

priority.description:
The default value is `Best Effort`.  Name of the priority class.  You must configure all the following classes:
  1. Best Effort
  2. Bronze
  3. Gold
  4. Platinum
  5. Silver
priority.title: priority

state.description:
The default value is `Disabled`.  Administrative state for the QoS class.
  * `disabled`  - Admin configured Disabled State.
  * `enabled` - Admin configured Enabled State.
Note: 'Best Effort' Class is 'Enabled' and cannot be 'Disabled'.
state.title: state

weight.description:
The default value is `1`.  The weight of the QoS Class controls the distribution of bandwidth between QoS Classes,with the same priority after the Guarantees for the QoS Classes are reached.  Default is `1`.
  * `Best Effort` - By Default is `5`.
  * `Bronze` - By Default is `7`.
  * `Gold` - By Default is `9`.
  * `Platinum` - By Default is `10`.
  * `Silver` - By Default is `8`.
weight.title: weight

intersight.system_qos.priority_flow_cont.description:
The System QoS Priority Flow Control Watchdog Configuration.

Required Attributes:
  * `shutdown_multiplier`
  * `watchdog_interval`

Optional Attributes:
  * `watchdog_enabled`

shutdown_multiplier.description: The default value is `1`.  The Shutdown Multiplier, multiplied by the watchdog timer, determines the total duration a Priority-based Flow Control (PFC)-enabled queue remains in shutdown mode. The maximum Watchdog Shutdown Multiplier is 10. However, if the Watchdog Interval exceeds 500 milliseconds, the Multiplier limit is reduced to 2.
shutdown_multiplier.title: shutdown_multiplier

watchdog_enabled.description: The default value is `true`.  Enables or disables the Priority-based Flow Control (PFC) watchdog feature. When enabled, the watchdog actively monitors PFC pause frames. By default Priority Flow Control is enabled for new QoS policies. Existing policies remain unaffected.
watchdog_enabled.title: watchdog_enabled

watchdog_interval.description: The default value is `500`.  Time in milliseconds for the PFC Watchdog. The Watchdog product (Watchdog Interval (in milliseconds) × Shutdown Multiplier) cannot exceed 1000ms.
watchdog_interval.title: watchdog_interval

intersight.system_qos.priority_flow_cont.title: Priority Flow Control Watchdog Configuration

intersight.system_qos.templates.description: Map of System QoS template class values derived from configure_default_classes and configure_recommended_classes.

intersight.tags.description:
An arbitrary key and value pair that can be used to tag REST resources and organize managed objects by
assigning meta-data tags to any object.

Required Attributes:
  * `key`
  * `value`
intersight.tags.title: tags - Tags

key.description: The default value is `key`.  The tag key for which usage information is provided.
key.title: key

type.description:
The default value is `KeyValue`.  An enum type that defines the type of tag. Supported values are `PathTag` and `KeyValue`.
  * `KeyValue` - KeyValue type of tag. Key is required for these tags. Value is required for these tags.
  * `PathTag` - Key contain path information. Value is not present for these tags. The path is created by using the \'/\' character as a delimiter.For example, if the tag is "A/B/C", then "A" is the parent tag, "B" is the child tag of "A" and "C" is the child tag of "B".'
type.title: type

value.description: The default value is `value`.  The tag value that has been assigned to this Key.
value.title: value

intersight.templates.description:
Use this section to Create or Define UCS Templates.  Options are:

  * `chassis`
  * `domain`
  * `name`_prefix`
  * `name`_suffix`
  * `server`

chassis.description:
In Cisco Intersight, a Chassis Profile Template enables resource management by streamlining policy alignment, and chassis configuration. You can create Chassis Profile Templates using the UCS Chassis Profile Template wizard to provision chassis, create policies to ensure smooth deployment of a chassis, and eliminate failures that are caused by inconsistent configuration. The UCS Chassis Profile Templates wizard has the following policies that are attached to a template:

  * `Chassis Configuration` — IMC Access, Power, SNMP and Thermal

Required Base Attributes:
  * `create_template`
  * `name`

Optional Base Attributes:
  * `description`
  * `tags`

#=======================================
# Chassis Configuration
#=======================================
  * `imc_access_policy`
  * `power_policy`
  * `snmp_policy`
  * `thermal_policy`
chassis.title: chassis - Chassis Profile Template - Intersight Uri: chassis/ProfileTemplates

domain.description:
In Cisco Intersight, a Domain Profile Template enables resource management by streamlining policy alignment, and domain configuration. You can create Domain Profile Templates using the UCS Domain Profile Template wizard to provision Fabric Interconnects, create policies to ensure smooth deployment of a Pair of Fabric Interconnects, and eliminate failures that are caused by inconsistent configuration. The Domain Profile Templates wizard groups the domain policies into the following three categories to provide a quick Summary View of the policies that are attached to a template:
  * `VLAN & VSAN Configuration` — VLAN and VSAN policies for Fabric Interconnect A and Fabric Interconnect B.
     - Note: Both Fabrics can share the same policies or they can be distinct.
  * `Ports Configuration` — Port policies.
     - Note: Both Fabrics can share the same port policy or they can be distinct.
  * `UCS Domain Configuration` — Management: NTP, Syslog, Network Connectivity and SNMP.  Network: System QoS and Switch Control.

Required Base Attributes:
  * `create_template`
  * `name`

Optional Base Attributes:
  * `description`
  * `tags`

#=======================================
# VLAN & VSAN Configuration
#=======================================
  * `vlan_policies` - required - A list of one or two VLAN policies.  A list of one will assign the same policy to both fabrics.
  * `vsan_policies` - A list of one or two VSAN policies.  A list of one will assign the same policy to both fabrics.

#=======================================
# Ports Configuration
#=======================================
  * `port_policies` - required

#=======================================
# UCS Domain Configuration - Management
#=======================================
  * `certificate_management_policy`
  * `ldap_policy`
  * `network_connectivity_policy`
  * `ntp_policy`
  * `snmp_policy`
  * `syslog_policy`

#=======================================
# UCS Domain Configuration - Network
#=======================================
  * `auditd_policy`
  * `netflow_configuration`
  * `switch_control_policy`
  * `system_qos_policy` - required
domain.title: domain - Domain Cluster Profile Template - Intersight Uri: fabric/SwitchClusterProfileTemplates

server.description:
In Cisco Intersight, a Server Profile Template enables resource management by streamlining policy alignment, and server configuration. You can create Server Profile Templates using the UCS Server Profile Template wizard to provision servers, create policies to ensure smooth deployment of servers, and eliminate failures that are caused by inconsistent configuration. The Server Profile Templates wizard groups the server policies into the following four categories to provide a quick Summary View of the policies that are attached to a profile:`
  * Compute Policies — BIOS, Boot Order, Firmware, Power, Thermal, UUID Pool, and Virtual Media.
  * Management Policies — Certificate Management, Device Connector, IMC Access, IPMI Over LAN, LDAP, Local User, Network Connectivity, SMTP, SNMP, SSH, Serial over LAN, Syslog, NTP, and Virtual KVM policies
  * Network Policies — Adapter Configuration, LAN Connectivity, and SAN Connectivity policies.
     - The LAN Connectivity policy requires you to create an Ethernet Adapter Policy, Ethernet Network Policy (Standalone only), and Ethernet QoS Policy. When you attach a LAN Connectivity policy to a server profile, the addresses of the MAC address Pool, or the static MAC address, are automatically assigned.
     - Note: A LAN Connectivity policy that has a static MAC address can be attached to only one server profile.
     - The SAN Connectivity policy requires you to create an Fibre Channel Adapter Policy, Fibre Channel Network Policy, and Fiber Channel QoS Policy. When you attach a SAN Connectivity policy to a server profile, the addresses of the WWPN and WWNN Pools, or the static WWPN and WWNN addresses, are automatically assigned.
     - Note:  A SAN Connectivity policy that has a static WWPN, or a static WWNN can be attached to only one server profile.
  * Storage Policies — Drive Security, SD Card, and Storage policies.

Required Attributes:
  * `create_template`
  * `name`
  * `target_platform`

Optional Attributes:
  * `description`
  * `tags`


#===========================================================
# FIAttached and Unified Edge - Policies
#===========================================================

#=======================================
# Compute Configuration
#=======================================
  * `bios_policy_policy`
  * `boot_order_policy`
  * `firmware_policy`
  * `memory_policy`
  * `pcie_connectivity_policy` - FIAttached
  * `power_policy`
  * `scrub_policy
  * `thermal_policy` - FIAttached
  * `uuid_pool`
  * `virtual_media_policy`

#=======================================
# Management Configuration
#=======================================
  * `certificate_management_policy`
  * `imc_access_policy`
  * `ipmi_over_lan_policy`
  * `local_user_policy`
  * `serial_over_lan_policy`
  * `snmp_policy`
  * `syslog_policy`
  * `virtual_kvm_policy`

#=======================================
# Storage Configuration
#=======================================
  * `drive_security_policy` - FIAttached
  * `sd_card_policy` - FIAttached
  * `storage_policy`

#=======================================
# Network Configuration
#=======================================
  * `lan_connectivity_policy`
  * `san_connectivity_policy` - FIAttached


#===========================================================
# Standalone - Policies
#===========================================================

#=======================================
# Compute Configuration
#=======================================
  * `bios_policy_policy`
  * `boot_order_policy`
  * `firmware_policy`
  * `memory_policy`
  * `persistent_memory_policy`
  * `power_policy`
  * `thermal_policy`
  * `virtual_media_policy`

#=======================================
# Management Configuration
#=======================================
  * `certificate_management_policy`
  * `device_connector_policy`
  * `ipmi_over_lan_policy`
  * `ldap_policy`
  * `local_user_policy`
  * `network_connectivity_policy`
  * `ntp_policy`
  * `serial_over_lan_policy`
  * `smtp_policy`
  * `snmp_policy`
  * `ssh_policy`
  * `syslog_policy`
  * `virtual_kvm_policy`

#=======================================
# Storage Configuration
#=======================================
  * `drive_security_policy`
  * `sd_card_policy`
  * `storage_policy`

#=======================================
# Network Configuration
#=======================================
  * `adapter_configuration_policy`
  * `lan_connectivity_policy`
  * `san_connectivity_policy`
server.title: server - Server Profile Template - Intersight Uri: server/ProfileTemplates

unified_edge.description:
In Cisco Intersight, a Unifed Edge Template enables resource management by streamlining policy alignment, and configuration. You can create Unified Edge Profile Templates using the Unified Edge Profile Template wizard to provision, create policies to ensure smooth deployment of a Unified Edge system; eliminating failures that are caused by inconsistent configuration. The Unifed Edge Profile Templates wizard groups the unified edge policies into the following three categories:
  * `Chassis Configuration` — Power and Thermal policies for the chassis.
  * `Switch Configuration` — VLAN, Port, System QoS, and Switch Control policies.
     - Note: Unified Edge eCIMC can share the same port and vlan policies or they can be distinct.
  * `Management Configuration` — NTP, Syslog, Network Connectivity and Local User.

Required Base Attributes:
  * `create_template`
  * `name`

Optional Base Attributes:
  * `description`
  * `tags`

#=======================================
# Chassis Configuration
#=======================================
  * `power_policy`
  * `thermal_policy`

#=======================================
# Switch Configuration
#=======================================
  * `port_policies` - required
  * `switch_control_policy`
  * `system_qos_policy` - required
  * `vlan_policies` - required - A list of one or two VLAN policies.  A list of one will assign the same policy to both eCMCs.

#=======================================
# Management Configuration
#=======================================
  * `local_user_policy`
  * `network_connectivity_policy`
  * `ntp_policy`
  * `syslog_policy`
unified_edge.title: unified_edge - Unified Edge Cluster Profile Template - Intersight Uri: fabric/SwitchClusterProfileTemplates

The default value is `FIAttached`.  The platform for which the server profile is applicable. It can either be a server that is operating in standalone mode or which is attached to a Fabric Interconnect managed by Intersight.

  * `FIAttached` - Servers which are connected to a Fabric Interconnect that is managed by Intersight.

allOf.description: Thermal Management policy models a configuration that can be applied to Chassis or Server to manage Thermal Features.

fan_control_mode.description:
The default value is `Balanced`.  Sets the Fan Control Mode of the System. High Power, Maximum Power and Acoustic modes are only supported for Cisco UCS X series Chassis.
  * `Balanced` - The fans run faster when needed based on the heat generated by the server. When possible, the fans returns to the minimum required speed.
  * `LowPower` - The Fans run at the minimum speed required to keep the server cool.
  * `HighPower` - The fans are kept at higher speed to emphasizes performance over power consumption. This Mode is only supported for UCS X series Chassis.
  * `MaximumPower` - The fans are always kept at maximum speed. This option provides the most cooling and consumes the most power. This Mode is only supported for UCS X series Chassis.
  * `Acoustic` - The fan speed is reduced to reduce noise levels in acoustic-sensitive environments. This Mode is only supported for UCS X series Chassis.
fan_control_mode.title: fan_control_mode

The default value is `A`.  The fabric port to which the vHBA will be associated.

  * `None` - Fabric Id is not set to either A or B for the standalone case where the server is not connected to Fabric Interconnects. The value 'None' should be used.
  * `A` - Fabric A of the FI cluster.
  * `B` - Fabric B of the FI cluster.
placement_switch_id.title: placement_switch_id

vhba_type.description:
The default value is `fc-initiator`.  vHBA Type configuration for SAN Connectivity Policy. This configuration is supported only on Cisco VIC 14XX series and higher series of adapters.
  * `fc-initiator` - The default value set for vHBA Type Configuration. Fc-initiator specifies vHBA as a consumer of storage. Enables SCSI commands to transfer data and status information between host and target storage systems.
  * `fc-nvme-initiator` - Fc-nvme-initiator specifies vHBA as a consumer of storage. Enables NVMe-based message commands to transfer data and status information between host and target storage systems.
  * `fc-nvme-target` - Fc-nvme-target specifies vHBA as a provider of storage volumes to initiators. Enables NVMe-based message commands to transfer data and status information between host and target storage systems. Currently tech-preview, only enabled with an asynchronous driver.
  * `fc-target` - Fc-target specifies vHBA as a provider of storage volumes to initiators. Enables SCSI commands to transfer data and status information between host and target storage systems. fc-target is enabled only with an asynchronous driver.
vhba_type.title: vhba_type

authentication_protocol.description:
The default value is `none`.  Type of Authentication protocol when CIFS is used for communication with the remote server.
  * `none` - (Default) No authentication is used.
  * `ntlm` - NT LAN Manager (NTLM) security protocol. Use this option only with Windows 2008 R2 and Windows 2012 R2.
  * `ntlmi` - NTLMi security protocol. Use this option only when you enable Digital Signing in the CIFS Windows server.
  * `ntlmv2` - NTLMv2 security protocol. Use this option only with Samba Linux.
  * `ntlmv2i` - NTLMv2i security protocol. Use this option only with Samba Linux.
  * `ntlmssp` - NT LAN Manager Security Support Provider (NTLMSSP) protocol. Use this option only with Windows 2008 R2 and Windows 2012 R2.
  * `ntlmsspi` - NTLMSSPi protocol. Use this option only when you enable Digital Signing in the CIFS Windows server.
authentication_protocol.title: authentication_protocol

device_type.description:
The default value is `cdd`.  Defualt is `cdd`.  Type of remote Virtual Media device.
  * `cdd` - (Default) Uses compact disc drive as the virtual media mount device.
  * `hdd` - Uses hard disk drive as the virtual media mount device.
device_type.title: device_type

file_location.description:
The default value is `https://server.example.com/os.iso`.  The remote file location path for the virtual media mapping. Accepted formats are:
  * `HDD` for CIFS: cifs://hostname-or-IP/filePath/fileName.img`
  * `CDD` for CIFS: cifs://hostname-or-IP/filePath/fileName.iso`
  * `HDD` for HTTP/S: http[s]://hostname-or-IP/filePath/fileName.img`
  * `CDD` for HTTP/S: http[s]://hostname-or-IP/filePath/fileName.iso`
  * `HDD` for NFS: nfs://hostname-or-IP/filePath/fileName.img`
  * `CDD` for NFS: nfs://hostname-or-IP/filePath/fileName.iso
file_location.title: file_location

mount_options.description:
The default value is ``.  Mount options for the Virtual Media mapping. The field can be left blank or filled in a comma separated list with the following options.

For NFS, supported options are:
  * `ro`
  * `rw`
  * `nolock`
  * `noexec`
  * `soft`
  * `port=VALUE`
  * `timeo=VALUE`
  * `retry=VALUE`

For CIFS, supported options are:
  * `soft`
  * `nounix`
  * `noserverino`
  * `guest`
Note: For CIFS version less than 3.0, `vers=VALUE` is mandatory. e.g. `vers=2`.

For HTTP/HTTPS, the only supported option is:`
  * `noauto`
mount_options.title: mount_options

name.description: The default value is `remote_map`.  Identity of the image for Virtual Media mapping.
name.title: name

password.description: The default value is `1`.  A number Between to denote to use one of the variables.  A number Between 1-64 to denote the variables vmedia_password_[1-64].  i.e. `export vmedia_password_1='cisco123'`.
password.title: password

protocol.description:
The default value is `https`.  Protocol to use to communicate with the remote server.
  * `nfs` - NFS protocol for vmedia mount.
  * `cifs` - CIFS protocol for vmedia mount.
  * `http` - HTTP protocol for vmedia mount.
  * `https` - HTTPS protocol for vmedia mount.
protocol.title: protocol

username.description: The default value is ``.  username for either CHAP or Mutual Chap Authentication
username.title: username

openshift.cluster_name.description:
The default value is `default`.  The default value is `cluster-1`.  OpenShift Cluster Name.  Keep cluster names under 24 characters to prevent issues with generated subdomain names and API object limits.

The cluster name must be unique within the cluster domain and can consist of lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens. It must start with a letter and end with a letter or number.

container_network_version.title: OpenShift Container Networking Intersight (CNI) Version

dns_servers.description: This array provides the DNS server configuration for the cluster.  The cluster svc will add the internal resolver and there is a limit of three DNS servers for each pod, this should not be more than two DNS servers.

routes.description:
This dictionary provides the host routing configuration.  This is defined at the cluster level and will be assigned to the individual nodes.

Required fields are:
  * `destination`
  * `gateway`
routes.title: OpenShift Container Platform (OCP) - Networking - Routes

openshift.install.cluster_networking.description:
This dictionary provides the required configuration settings for RedHat Openshift cluster network configuration.

Required Fields are:
  * `api`
  * `ingress`
  * `machine_network_gateway`
  * `routes`

Optional fields are:
  * `container_network_interface`
  * `container_network_version`
  * `dns_search_domains`
  * `dns_servers`
  * `service_network`
openshift.install.cluster_networking.title: OpenShift Container Platform (OCP) - Networking

v7.description: The default value is `2001:db8::9`.  OpenShift Cluster API - IPv6 address.
v7.title: OpenShift Cluster API - IPv6 address

openshift.install.cluster_networking.api.description:
This dictionary provides the required configuration settings for RedHat Openshift cluster API addressing.

Optional fields are:
  * `v4`
  * `v6`
openshift.install.cluster_networking.api.title: OpenShift Container Platform (OCP) - Networking - API

v4_cidr.description: The default value is `10.128.0.0/14`.  OpenShift Cluster Network - IPv4 CIDR address.  Refers to the Pod Network CIDR, which is the IP address range from which individual Pods within the cluster are allocated IP addresses.
v4_cidr.title: OpenShift Cluster Network - IPv4 CIDR address

v4_host_prefix.description: The default value is `23`.  OpenShift Cluster Network - IPv4 Host Prefix.  It defines the subnet prefix length assigned to each individual node within the cluster's overall network CIDR.
v4_host_prefix.title: OpenShift Cluster Network - IPv4 Host Prefix

v6_cidr.description: The default value is `2001:db8:128::/48`.  OpenShift Cluster Network - IPv6 CIDR address.  Refers to the Pod Network CIDR, which is the IP address range from which individual Pods within the cluster are allocated IP addresses.
v6_cidr.title: OpenShift Cluster Network - IPv6 CIDR address

v6_host_prefix.description: The default value is `48`.  OpenShift Cluster Network - IPv6 Host Prefix.  It defines the subnet prefix length assigned to each individual node within the cluster's overall network CIDR.
v6_host_prefix.title: OpenShift Cluster Network - IPv6 Host Prefix

openshift.install.cluster_networking.clu.description:
This dictionary provides the required configuration settings for RedHat Openshift cluster network addressing.  The cluster network refers to the interconnected system of logical and physical links that enable communication within a Kubernetes cluster, adhering to the Kubernetes networking model.

Optional fields are:
  * `v4_cidr`
  * `v4_host_prefix`
  * `v6_cidr`
  * `v6_host_prefix`
openshift.install.cluster_networking.clu.title: OpenShift Container Platform (OCP) - Cluster Network

v4.description: The default value is `198.18.0.10`.  OpenShift Cluster Ingress - IPv4 address.
v4.title: OpenShift Cluster Ingress - IPv4 address

v6.description: The default value is `2001:db8::10`.  OpenShift Cluster Ingress - IPv6 address.
v6.title: OpenShift Cluster Ingress - IPv6 address

openshift.install.cluster_networking.ing.description:
This dictionary provides the required configuration settings for RedHat Openshift cluster ingress addressing.

Optional fields are:
  * `v4`
  * `v6`
openshift.install.cluster_networking.ing.title: OpenShift Container Platform (OCP) - Networking - Ingress

v4.description: The default value is `198.18.0.1/24`.  OpenShift Machine Network Gateway - IPv4 Gateway/Prefix.  This is the frontend network for the cluster.  So we will define the gateway so that it isn't a requirement to also define the default route for the nodes.
v4.title: OpenShift Service Network - IPv4 Gateway address

v6.description: The default value is `2001:db8::1/64`.  OpenShift Machine Network - IPv6 Gateway/Prefix.  This is the frontend network for the cluster.  So we will define the gateway so that it isn't a requirement to also define the default route for the nodes.
v6.title: OpenShift Machine Network - IPv6 Gateway address

openshift.install.cluster_networking.mac.description:
This dictionary provides the required configuration settings for RedHat Openshift machine network gateway addressing.  A "k8s machine network" refers to the networking infrastructure within a Kubernetes cluster that allows all its components—including containers, pods, and nodes—to communicate with each other and with external services.

Optional fields are:
  * `v4`
  * `v6`
openshift.install.cluster_networking.mac.title: OpenShift Container Platform (OCP) - Machine Network Gateway

destination.description: The default value is `198.18.0.0/24`.  IPv4/IPv6 Destination/Prefix.
destination.title: IPv4/IPv6 Destination/Prefix

gateway.description: The default value is `198.18.0.1`.  IPv4/IPv6 gateway/next-hop-address for the destination.
gateway.title: IPv4/IPv6 Gateway

openshift.install.cluster_networking.rou.title: OpenShift Container Platform (OCP) - Machine Network Gateway

v4_cidr.description: The default value is `172.30.0.0/16`.  OpenShift Service Network - IPv4 CIDR address.  The Kubernetes Service Network CIDR refers to a specific range of IP addresses, defined using Classless Inter-Domain Routing (CIDR) notation, that is reserved for the internal IP addresses of Kubernetes Services within a cluster.
v4_cidr.title: OpenShift Service Network - IPv4 CIDR address

v6_cidr.description: The default value is `2001:db8:30::/64`.  OpenShift Service Network - IPv6 CIDR address.  The Kubernetes Service Network CIDR refers to a specific range of IP addresses, defined using Classless Inter-Domain Routing (CIDR) notation, that is reserved for the internal IP addresses of Kubernetes Services within a cluster.
v6_cidr.title: OpenShift Service Network - IPv6 CIDR address

openshift.install.cluster_networking.ser.description:
This dictionary provides the required configuration settings for RedHat Openshift service network addressing.   The "service network" refers to the system that enables communication between different parts of your application and exposes services both within and outside the cluster. It provides a stable and reliable way to access your applications, abstracting away the dynamic nature of individual Pods.

Optional fields are:
  * `v4_cidr`
  * `v6_cidr`
openshift.install.cluster_networking.ser.title: OpenShift Container Platform (OCP) - Service Network

openshift.install.cluster_version.description: The default value is `4.20.16`.  OpenShift Cluster Version.
openshift.install.cluster_version.title: OpenShift Cluster Version

openshift.install.cpu_architecture.description: The default value is `x86_64`. Defines the CPU Architecture for the physical servers.
openshift.install.cpu_architecture.title: OpenShift CPU Architecture

image_base_url.description: The default value is `http://your-machine-ip:8080`.  Web Server Base URL.
image_base_url.title: Web Server Base URL

image_upload_directory.description: The default value is `/var/www/html/isos`. Local file path for ISO upload directory.
image_upload_directory.title: Local File Path for ISO Upload Directory

password.description: The ISO Web Server password Sensitive Variable Identifier for password based authentication when not usign the `locahost` option.  A number Between 1-64 to denote the variables iso_web_server_password_[1-64].  i.e. `export iso_web_server_password_1='password123'`.
password.title: ISO Web Server Password

server_address.description: The default value is `localhost`.  Server address of the hosting web server for login.
server_address.title: Server Address

ssh_public_key.description: The SSH Public Key Sensitive Variable Identifier for SSH based authentication when not using the `localhost` option.  A number Between 1-64 to denote the variables ssh_public_key_[1-64].  i.e. `export ssh_public_key_1='ssh-rsa AAAA...'`.
ssh_public_key.title: SSH Public Key

username.description: ISO Web Server username for login, if not using the `localhost` option.
username.title: ISO Web Server Username

  * `ssh_public_key`

openshift.install.iso_web_server.title: ISO Web Server Configuration

openshift.ldap.description:
This dictionary defines the LDAP Identity Provider settings for OpenShift.

Required fields are:
  * `base_dn`
  * `bind_dn`
  * `secure_ldap`
  * `url`

base_dn.description: The LDAP Base Distinguished Name used as the search base for user entries (e.g. `DC=example,DC=com`).
base_dn.title: LDAP Base Distinguished Name

bind_dn.description: The Distinguished Name of the service account used to bind to the LDAP directory (e.g. `CN=Service Account,OU=Service Accounts,DC=example,DC=com`).
bind_dn.title: LDAP Bind Distinguished Name

certificate_file_path.description: Path to the CA certificate file used to verify the LDAP server's TLS certificate (e.g. `ca.crt`).
certificate_file_path.title: LDAP CA Certificate File Path

cluster_admins.description: List of LDAP group names to grant OpenShift cluster-admin privileges.

items.description: LDAP group name to grant cluster-admin access.
items.title: Cluster Admin Group

cluster_admins.title: Cluster Admin Groups

additionalProperties.description: OpenShift group name to map this LDAP group DN to.

group_uid_mappings.description: Mapping of LDAP group Distinguished Names to OpenShift group names.  Keys are LDAP group DNs, values are the corresponding OpenShift group names.
group_uid_mappings.title: LDAP Group UID Mappings

secure_ldap.description: The default value is `True`.  Whether to use LDAPS (secure LDAP over TLS) for the connection.
secure_ldap.title: Secure LDAP

sync_url.description: The LDAP URL used for group synchronization (e.g. `ldaps://ad.example.com:636`).
sync_url.title: LDAP Sync URL

url.description: The full LDAP search URL including base DN, attribute, scope, and filter (e.g. `ldaps://ad.example.com:636/DC=example,DC=com?sAMAccountName?sub?(objectclass=*)`).
url.title: LDAP Search URL
