pure_storage.settings.security.directory.description:
Pure Storage Directory Service configuration settings.

Required fields are:
  * `base_dn`
  * `bind_password`
  * `bind_user`
  * `uri_list`

Optional fields are:
  * `ca_certificate`
  * `ca_certificate_group`
  * `enabled`
  * `type`

base_dn.description: The default value is ``.  Base Distinguished Name (DN). Starting point from where server will search for users and groups.
base_dn.title: Base Distinguished Name

bind_password.description: The default value is `1`.  The LDAP Binding Password Sensitive Variable Identifier.  A number Between 1-64 to denote the variables ldap_bind_password_[1-64].  i.e. `export ldap_bind_password_1='cisco123'`.
bind_password.title: Bind Password

bind_user.description: The default value is ``.  Distinguished Name (DN) of the user, that is used to authenticate against LDAP servers.
bind_user.title: Bind Username

ca_certificate.description: The default value is `default`.  The Name of the CA Certificate, configured under SECURITY > Certificates for FlashBlade.  For FlashArray.
ca_certificate.title: CA Certificate

ca_certificate_group.description: The default value is `default`.  The Name of the CA Certificate Group, configured under SECURITY > Certificates.
ca_certificate_group.title: CA Certificate Group

enabled.description: The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true`.  Enable Management Directory Service.
enabled.title: Enable Directory Service

type.description: The default value is `management`.  The type of directory service configuration. FlashArray supports `data` and `management`.  FlashBlade supports `management`, `nfs`, and `smb`.
type.title: Directory Service Type

uri_list.description: List of LDAP Uniform Resource Identifiers (URIs) to connect to.

items.description:
The default value is `ldap://server1.example.com:389`.  Each URI must include the scheme ldap:// or ldaps:// (for LDAP over SSL), a hostname, and a domain name or IP address. For example, ldap://ad.company.com configures the directory service with the hostname "ad" in the domain "company.com" while specifying the unencrypted LDAP protocol.

We highly recommend either configuring StartTLS by enabling a certificate or certificate group, or configuring URIs by using the ldaps:// scheme to use LDAP over SSL, unless there is no need for secure communication in your environment.

If specifying a domain name, it should be resolvable by the configured DNS servers.

If specifying an IP address, for IPv4, specify the IP address in the form ddd.ddd.ddd.ddd, where ddd is a number ranging from 0 to 255 representing a group of 8 bits.

For IPv6, specify the IP address in the form [xxxx:xxxx:xxxx:xxxx:xxxx:xxxx:xxxx:xxxx], where xxxx is a hexadecimal number representing a group of 16 bits. Enclose the entire address in square brackets ( []) . Consecutive fields of zeros can be shortened by replacing the zeros with a double colon ( ::).

If the base DN is not configured and a URI is provided, the base DN will automatically default to the domain components of the URIs.

Optionally specify a port. Append the port number after the end of the entire address. Default ports are 389 for LDAP, and 636 for LDAPS. Non-standard ports can be specified in the URI if they are in use.
items.title: Uniform Resource Identifier (URI)

uri_list.title: Uniform Resource Identifier (URI) List

user_login_attribute.description: The default value is `sAMAccountName`.  The value of the User Login Attribute. The User Login Attribute defaults to the sAMAccountName for Active Directory, or UID for other server types.
user_login_attribute.title: User Login Attribute

user_object_class.description: The default value is `User`.  The value of the User Object Class. The User Object Class defaults to User for AD, posixAccount or shadowAccount for OpenLDAP depending on the server's schema, posixAccount for posix-compliant servers, or person for all other server types.
user_object_class.title: User Object Class

pure_storage.settings.security.directory.title: Pure Storage - Settings > Security > Directory Service > Configuration

pure_storage.settings.security.directory.description:
Directory Service Role Mappings.

Required fields are:
  * `end_point_role`
  * `group_dn`
  * `name`

Optional fields are:
  * `group`

group.description: The default value is ``.  In the Group field, enter the common name (CN) of the directory group. The name should be the Common Name of the group without the "CN=" specifier. If the configured groups are not in the same OU, also specify the OU. For example, "pureusers,OU=PureStorage", where pureusers is the common name of the directory service group.
group.title: Group Common Name

group_base.description: The default value is ``.  In the Group Base field, enter the path to the configured groups in the directory tree. The Group Base consists of one or more path components (OUs and CNs) that, when combined with the base DN attribute and the configured group CNs, complete the full distinguished name of each group. The group base should specify “OU=” for each OU and “CN=” for each CN. Multiple path components should be separated by commas. The order of the path components should get larger in scope from left to right. In the following example, SANManagers contains the sub-organizational unit PureGroups: “OU=PureGroups,OU=SANManagers”.
group_base.title: Group Distinguished Name

role.description:
The default value is `readonly`.  Select a role from the Role list.
  * `array_admin`
  * `ops_admin`
  * `readonly`
  * `storage_admin`
role.title: Endpoint Role

pure_storage.settings.security.directory.title: Pure Storage - Settings > Security > Directory Service > Role Mappings

pure_storage.settings.system.monitoring..description:
Syslog Server Connection settings for Pure Storage.

Required fields are:
  * `name`
  * `services`
  * `uri`

name.description: The default value is `default`.  Name of the Syslog Server Connection.
name.title: Name of the Syslog Server Connection

services.description: Services to send to the syslog server connection.

items.description: The default value is `management`.  Syslog service type(s).

services.title: Services

uri.description:
The default value is `udp://syslog.example.com:514`.  Enter the URI of the remote syslog server.
Specify the URI in the format PROTOCOL://HOSTNAME:PORT, where
PROTOCOL is TCP, TLS, or UDP.
Note that when establishing TLS communication, the syslog server must be configured with either a CA certificate or CA certificate group (for multiple remote syslog servers). See Selecting CA Certificates for Syslog Server Connections. Additionally, your firewall must be set to allow in-bound and out-bound traffic.

HOSTNAME is the syslog server's hostname or IP address. If specifying an IP address, for IPv4, specify the IP address in the form ddd.ddd.ddd.ddd, where ddd is a number ranging from 0 to 255 representing a group of 8 bits.
For IPv6, specify the IP address in the form [xxxx:xxxx:xxxx:xxxx:xxxx:xxxx:xxxx:xxxx], where xxxx is a hexadecimal number representing a group of 16 bits. Enclose the entire address in square brackets ([]). Consecutive fields of zeros can be shortened by replacing the zeros with a double colon (::).

PORT is the port at which the server is listening. Append the port number after the end of the entire address. This is optional if the standard port number 514 for UDP or TCP, or the standard port number 6514 for TLS, is used.
uri.title: URI

pure_storage.settings.system.monitoring..title: Syslog Server Connections

  * `remote_assist_connected`

phone_home_enabled.description: The default value is `True`.  The default value is `true`.  Flag to tell the script to enable Phone Home support.
phone_home_enabled.title: Phone Home Enabled

proxy_server.description:
The default value is `http://proxy.example.com:8080`.  The Proxy Server to use for the arrays.

This is optional, but if you have a proxy server, you can set it here.
proxy_server.title: Proxy Server

remote_assist_connected.description: The default value is `24`.  Length of time to enable the Remote Assistance.
remote_assist_connected.title: Remote Assist Enabled
